questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The interxylary phloem is found in the stem of
questions.solution: Interxylary phloem is found in Calotropis stem. It is said to be interxylary due to its presence inside xylem. It is also found in same member of family-Solanceae.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Tyloses an out growth from ray or axial parenchyma cell into the lumen of a vessel, which partially or completely blocks the cavity are present in
questions.solution: Tyloses are found in heart wood, sometimes the xylem parenchyma develop balloon-like structure to which penetrates into the adjacent xylem. Vessels, which are called tyloses, block the luman of the xylem.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The structures present in the roots to absorb water and minerals is
questions.solution: Epidermal extensions are always unicellular, while epidermal appendages may be uni or multicellular. Root hairs are epidermal extensions formed by outward elongated bulging of wall of epidermal cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is a useful product of epidermal origin?
questions.solution: Cotton is obtained from the epidermal hair present on the surface of seeds of  Gossypium sp. These are made up of cellulose only and may be of two types, i.e., extractable lint and non-extractable fluffy fuzz. Cotton fibres are mainly used for textiles, celluloid, cellophane, rayon and paper pulp.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Vascular tissues in flowering plants develop from
questions.solution:
Histogen theory for shoot apical meristem has been proposed by Hanstein (1870). It advocates that there are three distinct meristematic zones (layers) called dermatogen periblem and plerome. The dermatogen is the outermost histogen giving rise to epidermis, periblem is the middle on producing the cortex and plerome is the innermost resulting in central cylinder (i.e., vascular tissue).
Cork cambium (phellogen) is the secondary lateral meristem found in outer cortical region. Its cells divide periclinally cutting off cells towards the outside (forming cork or phellem) and inside (forming secondary cortex or phelloderm).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Angular collenchyma occurs in
questions.solution: Angular collenchyma occurs in Cucurbita. It has thickening at the angles and there are no intercellular spaces. It is generally found in leaf petioles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Phellogen and phellem respectively denote
questions.solution: In the dicot stem the cortical cells gets differentiated to give rise to another meristematic tissue, which is called cork cambium or phellogen. On the other side, it forms phellem (cork) and in the inner region it forms secondary cortical cells (phelloderm).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The quiescent centre in root meristem serves as a
options: site for storage of food, which is utilised during maturation
reserve for replenishment of damaged cells of the meristem
questions.solution: The quiescent centre is found in the centre of the root apex. Cells of this region have lower content of RNA and smaller nuclei. This represents inactive region of root apex (Clowers; 1958) and serves as a reserve for replenishment of damaged cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The term 'bark' refers to
options: phellogen, phellem, phelloderm and secondary phloem
questions.solution: All the dead cells lying outside the phellogen constitute the bark of plant. It may include epidermis, cork, hypodermis and even part of cortex depending upon the position of origin of cork cambium or phellogen.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What differentiates leaf of dicots from monocots?
options: Differentiation of palisade and spongy parenchyma
Stomata both on upper and lower sides

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In the sieve elements, which one of the following is the most likely function of P-proteins?
options: Deposition of callose on sieve plates
questions.solution:
In most angiospermic plants, sieve tube elements of phloem, are P-proteins. Later is found in all dictos
and in many monocots but it is absent in gymnosperms and cryptogams.
The main function of P -proteins is in sealing off damaged sieve elements by plugging up sieve plate pores.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Species can be identified on the basis of
questions.solution: The prevention of inbreeding between the populations of two different species is known as reproductive isolation. Reproductive isolation leads to the origin of new species.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Duramen is present in
questions.solution: After certain years of growth, the xylem elements of the stems of a number of trees develop dark brown colouration, especially in the central or innermost layers. This region comprises dead element with highly lignified walls and is called heart wood or duramen. It is present in inner region of secondary wood.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Meristematic tissue in vascular bundle is
questions.solution: The cambium present between the xylem and phloem of vascular bundle is called vascular cambium. It is a lateral meristem that gives rise to secondary xylem and phloem and occurs in the form of a thin strip. Vascular cambium is also called fascicular cambium.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Vessels and companion cells are found in
questions.solution: Angiosperms are those seed plants in which seeds are formed inside fruits and the sporophylls are organized into flower. In angiosperms, xylem contains vessels and phloem possesses sieve tubes and companion cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In a woody dicotyledonous tree, which of the following parts will mainly consist of primary tissues?
questions.solution:
Primary tissues are those meristematic tissues, which are derived directly from embryonal tissues.
e.g., shoot apex and root apex:

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Commercial cork is obtained from
questions.solution: The 'cork' of commerce is obtained from Quercus suber. The first formed periderm, called virgin cork is removed when the tree is just 20 years old.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pencils are prepared from the wood of
questions.solution: Juniperus virginiana wood is used for making pencils.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In monocot roots which types of vascular bundles are found?
questions.solution: When xylem and phloem groups are located on different radii, the bundles are said to be radial e.g. root. The protoxylem element lie at the periphery and metaxylem in the centre, this condition is called exarch e.g. root.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Periderm includes :
options: cork cambium, cork and secondary cortex
questions.solution: Periderm formation occurs in the root and stems of dicots and gymnosperm in extra stelar region with the help of cork cambium. Periderm is external secondary protective tissue and compreses of cork, cork cambium and secondary cortex.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In pteridophytes, phloem is without
questions.solution: In angiosperm, companion cells are living cells along with sieve tube and phloem parenchyma of the phloem. In pteridophytes and gymno sperm phloem is without companion cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Jute is a
questions.solution: Fibres are elongated cells with usually lignified walls. Bast fibres from phloem is called jute. There are secondary phloem cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is an epidermal cell containing chloroplast?
questions.solution: Guard cell are the specialised epidermis cells containing chloroplast.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following tissues consist of living cells?
questions.solution: Companion cells are present between sieve tubes in the phloem of angiosperms. These are the living cells with large nucleus that controls the activity of non-nucleated sieve tubes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Wound healing is due to
questions.solution: Secondary meristem are the meristem that are formed secondarly from the permanent tissue. Healing of wound in plant take place by the activity of secondary meristem.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Secretion of pancreatic juice is stimulated by
questions.solution: The secretion of pancreatic juice is stimulated by both secretin and cholecystokinin (CCK). These two hormones, however, are secreted in response to different stimuli and they have different effects on the composition of pancreatic juice.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Quiescent centre is the zone of :
options: least mitotic activity in the root apex
least mitotic activity in the shoot apex
maximum mitotic activity in the root apex
maximum mitotic activity in the shoot apex
questions.solution: Quiescent centre is an area at the top of the root apical meristem where the cells have least mitotic activity i.e., the rate of cell division is lower than in surrounding tissue.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Torsion of visceral mass is seen in animals belonging to class
questions.solution: Animals of class-Gastropoda of phylumMollusca undergo twisting or torsion of the visceral mass during development, leading to conversion of symmetrical embryo into an asymmetrical adult.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Consider following statements and Choose correct ones from given options.
I. Shark do not have any bone in its body.
II. Water snake and salamander belongs to same class and have largest RBC.
III. Silver fish is a true fish while cuttle and star fishes are molluscs and echinoderms respectively.
IV. Ornithorhynchus is a connecting link between reptiles and mammals.
questions.solution:
- Shark is a member of Chondrichthyes class, which is a cartilaginous fish.
- Water snake is a reptile and salamander is an amphibian having largest RBC in animal kingdom.
- Silver fish (Lepisma) is an arthropod, cuttle fish (Sepia) is a mollusc and star fish (Asterias) is an echinodermate. So none of them is a true fish (Pisces).
- Ornithorhychus is a mammal which is also known as duck-billed playtypus. It is a member of Monotremata order which is a connecting link between reptiles and mammals.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What is the symmetry of medusa?
questions.solution: The medusa of Obelia shows radial symmetry. It is a free-swimming, umbrella-like sexual generation. It is formed by blastostyle. It brings about sexual reproduction and dispersal of species.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Most advanced invertebrates are
questions.solution: Cephalopods such as octopus, cuttlefish and squid (coleoids) are the most advanced invertebrates among the given options. They belong to class-Cephalopoda of phylum - Mollusca. These organisms have a prominent head and a set arms or tentacles. They are widely regarded as the most intelligent of invertebrates and have well-developed senses and larger brains. Their nervous system is the most complex of all invertebrates.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Radula is a part of which animal.
questions.solution: Radula is a part of Mollusca group of animals. It is a rasping organ of molluscs situated in a sac on the underside of buccal cavity. It is used for tearing plant material by rubbing it against the hardened surface of the month.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Food storage in Leucosolenia occur by
questions.solution: It occur by thesocyte which have lobose pseudopodia and are filled with food reserves, thus acting as storage cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The structure present in all adult vertebrates is
questions.solution: Dorsal tubular nerve cord is the characteristic feature of all adult vertebrate. It is a gel-like substance and is ramnant of notochord.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following in birds indicates their reptilian ancestry?
options: Two special chambers crop and gizzards in their digestive tract
questions.solution: The birds have evolved from reptiles. Both birds and reptiles lay eggs which are large and telo lecithal with thick hard calcarious shell i.e., cleiodic eggs.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Notochord originates from
questions.solution: Notochord originates from mesoderm germ layer.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The scientific name of gharial is
questions.solution: Scientific name of gharial or gavial is Gavialis gangeticus, which lives in rivers Ganga and Brahamputra.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Which of the given option is correct regarding the statments?
Statement I Cephalochordata bears notochord all along the body throughout life.
Statements II Urochordate bears vertebral column only in tail region throughout the life.
options: Both I and II are wrong
questions.solution: Cephalochordate have notochord all along the body through out life. But urochordates have notochord in the tail region in their larval stage only.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chondrichthyes is characterised by
questions.solution: Chondrichthyes (cartilaginous fishes) are marine. Their mouth is ventral and they have cartilaginous endoskeleton. Dermal placoid scales are present, e.g., Scoliodon, Pristis, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ichthyology is study of
questions.solution: Study of amphibians and reptiles is called Herpetology. Study of aves is called Ornithology. Study of fishes is called Ichthyology.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select incorrect pair from teh following.
questions.solution: Kingdom-Monera includes all prokaryotes (autotrophic or heterotrophic) viz., mycoplasmas, bacteria, Actinomycetes (mycelial bacteria) and photosynthetic cyanobacteria, while all unicellular eukaryotic organisms like flagellates, diatoms, dinoflagellates, slime moulds, sarcodina, etc. are included in kingdom-Protista.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Bilateral symmetry, metameric segmentation, coelom and open circulatory system are the features of
questions.solution: The members of phylum-Arthropoda show bilateral symmetry, three germ layers in body wall, external metamerism, jointed and paired appendages, haemocoel and open type of circulatory system with dorsal heart.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Correctly matched set of phylum, class and example is
questions.solution: Pinctada sp. are the bivalve molluscs, commonly known as pearl oysters. These belong to sub-class-Lamellibranchia, class-Bivalvia or Pelycipoda, phylum-Mollusca and kingdom-Animalia.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Branch of zoology dealing with the study of fishes is called
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{l|l}
\hline \text{Branch of Zoology} & \text{Area} \\
\hline \text{Arthrology} & \text{Study of joints} \\
\text{Ichthyology} & \text{Study of fishes} \\
\text{Saurology} & \text{Study of lizards} \\
\text{Herpetology} & \text{Study of reptiles} \\
\hline
\end{array}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Solenocytes are the main excretory structures in
questions.solution:
Solenocytes (also called flame cells) are meant for excretion and osmoregulation in Platyhelminthes. Annelids have metanephridia for excretion. Molluscs have kidneys (although different from vertebrate kidneys) for excretion.
In echinodermates, no special excretory organs are found for excretion. In them, excretion takes place through diffusion or osmosis or through active transport.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The canal system is a characteristic feature of
questions.solution: Sponges possess an extensive system of interconnected cavities called canal system, which typically consists of incurrent canals, radial canals, excurrent canals and spongocoel. The system is useful for nutrition, respiration and excretion.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which type of cells are absent in sponges?
questions.solution: Cnidocytes or stinging cells are spherical or oval cells found in entire epidermis except that of basal disc and are found only in cnidarians. Other cells are found in sponges.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following animals possess non-elastic lungs with elastic air sacs connected to them?
questions.solution: It is the feature of birds (Aves) to have non-distensible lungs continuous with thin elastic air sacs.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Flame cells are present in
questions.solution:
Flame cells are present in Platyhelminthes.
These are excretory organs of animals. Aschelminthes have renette cells as excretory organs.
Annelids and cephalochordates have nephridic as excretory organs.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Vetebrate brain differentiates from
questions.solution: The brain is the centre of the nervous system in all vertebrates and most invertebrate animals. The neural plate of ectoderm forms the brain, spinal cord and nerves.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chondrichthyes is characterised by
questions.solution: Chondrichthyes is one of the classes of super-class-Pisces, sub-phylum-Vertebrata and phylum-Chordata. The members of class-Chondrichthyes are marine animals with streamlined body and have cartilaginous endoskeleton. Mouth is located ventrally. The skin is tough, containing minute placoid scales. The teeth aremodified placoid scales which are backwardly directed, e.g., Dog fish (Scoliodon), saw fish (Pristis), great white shark (Carcharodon), sting ray (Trygon), etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Secondary body cavity with segmented mesodermal lining is called
questions.solution: The body cavity which is lined by mesoderm is called coelom. Animals possessing coelom are called coelomates, e.g., annelids, molluscs, arthropods, echinoderms, hemichordates and chordates.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The echinoderms are
questions.solution: Echinoderms are marine, spiny skinned animals. All members of phylum-Echinodermata are marine. These have water vascular system which helps in locomotion, eg, starfish, Antedon. Arboreal insect are insects, which living on trees. Freshwater forms are animals or plants living in freshwater of ponds, eg, Hydrilla.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following is not a vertebrate?
questions.solution: See hare belongs to phyllum-Mollusca and is not a vertebrate.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pneumatic bones are expected to be found in
questions.solution: Bones of Aves (eg, pigeon) are pneumatic. Pneumatic bones contain air cavities to reduce weight, which help in aerial mode of life.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following have notochord throughout life?
questions.solution:
Notochord is present in any stage of life of a chordate. It is replaced by vertebral column in vertebrates. Notochord is present throughout life in members of subphylum Cephalochordata, eg, Amphioxus.
In birds, fishes and snakes, notochord is replaced by vertebral column.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is common between earthworm, leech and centipede?
questions.solution: Ventral nerve cord possesses segmental ganglia. It is common between earthworm, leech and centipede.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Tube feet is the locomotory organ of
questions.solution: Tube feet are extensible tubular processes bearing suckers and present in rows on the under-surface of echinoderms. Tube feet are connected internally with water vascular system. Each tube foot has an ampulla, a podium and a sucker. Tube feet are sensory structures that take part in adhesion, locomotion, respiration and food capturing. They are locomotory organs of echinoderms like starfish. Jelly fish is a member of phylum-Coelenterata. Scoliodon is a fish and silver fish is an arthropod.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Shell of molluscs is derived from
questions.solution: Molluscs are the soft bodied, unsegmented animals covered by a shell. In between the shell and body wall, is a covering called mantle, which secretes the shell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: One animal that does not perform locomotion is
questions.solution: Sycon (Scypha) is a sponge and does not perform locomotion. It is a genus of calcareous sponges belonging to family-Scyettidae, Class-Calcaronea and phylum-Porifera. These sponges are small, growing upto 5 cm in total length, and are tube shaped and often white to cream in colour.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following cell type is capable of giving rise to other cell types in sponges ?
questions.solution: Archaeocytes are the totipotent cells, which provide great regenerating power to sponges. Sex cells (sperm and ova arise from undifferentiated archaeocytes.Thesocytes are the amoebocytes with reserve food granules.Pinacocytes are the polygonal flat cells present as outer layer of cells, called pinacoderm lining the spongocoel or body cavity in Leucosolenia.Cnidocytes occur in entire epidermis except that of basal disc and are found only in cnidarians. Cnidocytes are spherical or oval cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Polyp phase is absent in
questions.solution:
Polyp and medusa are the asexual and sexual phase present respectively in coelenterates. The members of different classes can be differentiated by the presence or absence of these stages.
Aurelia (jelly fish) belongs to class-Scyphozoa, in which medusoid phase is dominant and polypoid phase absent.
Hydra, Obelia, Physalia are the members of class- Hydrozoa in which, both medusoid and polypoid stages are present.
Metridium, Tubipora etc., belong to classAnthozoa, with all polyps but no medusae.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match the following and select the correct option.
$\begin{array}{ll|ll}
\hline & \text{List I} & & \text{List II} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Cyclostomes} & \text{1}. & \text{Hemichordata} \\
\text{B.} & \text{Aves} & \text{2.} & \text{Urochordata} \\
\text{C.} & \text{Tunicates} & \text{3.} & \text{Agantha} \\
\text{D.} & \text{Balanoglossus} & \text{4.} & \text{Pisces} \\
\text{E.} & \text{Osteichthyes} & \text{5.} & \text{Tetrapod} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{lll}\text {  Cyclostomes } & - & \text { Agantha } \\ \text { Aves } & - & \text { Tetrapod } \\ \text { Tunicates } & - & \text { Urochordata } \\ \text { Balanoglossus } & - & \text { Hemichordata } \\ \text { Osteichthyes } & - & \text { Pisces }\end{array}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Turbellarians are free living
questions.solution: Turbellaria (L. turbella - a string) is a class of phylum-Platyhelminthes. Turbellarians are mostly free, living flat worms, majorly aquatic (marine), presence of cilia, body unsegmented, mouth ventral, suckers absent with tango, chemo and photoreceptors, eg, Planaria (Dugesia), Bipalium etc. Trematoda (Gr. trema = hole; eidos $=$ form), class of phylum-Platyhelminthes. Commonly called 'flukes', ecto or endoparasites, body without cilia, unsegmented with suckers and hooks, eg, Fasciola, Schistosoma etc.Cestoda (Gr. kestos $=$ girdle eidos $=$ form) is also one of the class of phylum-Platyhelminthes. They are commonly called 'tapeworms' parasites without cilia and sense organs body segmented (pseudometamerism), digestive system absent, eg, Taenia and Echinococcus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Malpighian tubules are
questions.solution: Malpighian tubules are the excretory organs of insects. It opens at the junction of midgut and hindgut (ileum) in cockroach. Malpighian tubules absorb excretory substances from haemolymph and fat bodies and pass into the proctodaeum. The excretory product of insects is uric acid related with water conservation. These tubules are concerned with homeostasis, osmoregulation and excretion,Respiratory organ of insects istracheae, which opens outside by ten pairs of spiracles, present in thoracic (2 pairs) and abdominal (8 pairs) parts.Endocrine glands of insects consist of corpora allata, corpora cardiaca and prothoracic gland, which regulate metamorphosis in insects through different hormonal secretion. Excretory organ of frog is kidney (mesonephric), large urinary bladder, excretion ureotelic.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The characteristic larva of phylum'Coelenterata' is
questions.solution: Coelenterata (coelom + enteron) or phylum Cnidaria shows both sexual and asexual reproduction. The larval stages areplanula (Obelia) andephyra (Aurelia).Cysticercus (bladder worm) is the third larval stage seen in Taenia besides oncosphere, hexacanth (other two larval stages). Cysticercus is seen after the hexacanth released in the stomach of pig, which, goes through blood circulation and on reaching muscles get encysted in the form of bladderworm.Rhabditiform is the first larval stage in Ascaris which is formed within $10-14$ days. It is non-infective juvenile stage which rests for a week and completes first moult within egg and becomes second stage rhabditiform larva which is infective. The larva is called rhabditiform or rhabditoid because of its close resemblance with Rhabditis.Wriggler is the larva of mosquito. It is free-swimming, active, aquatic larva performing wriggling movements. It has a life-span of 3-4 days.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: His...... handling resulted in all that destruction and damage.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
rearrange the parts P, Q, R and S to make a proper sentence.
Towards midnight
(P) so that the sky was lighted with
(Q) and the clouds drifted away
(R) the rain ceased
(S) the incredible lamp of stars
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
rearrange the parts P, Q, R and S to make a proper sentence.
Without books
(P) no cultured society is possible
(Q) no fresh ideas are possible
(R) and
(S) without fresh ideas
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: 'Sea fan' belongs to :
questions.solution: Sea fan is common name of Gorgonia. It belongs to phylum-Coelenterata, class-Anthozoa and order-Gorgonacea.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Trochophore is the larva of :
questions.solution: Trochophore is cilliated, larval stage of polychaetes, mollsucs and rotifers. Neopilina, Chiton and Pila belong to phylum-Molusca. Molluscan development is either direct or with metamorphosis through the trochophore stage.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Food storage in Leucosolenia occur by :
questions.solution: Food storage in Leucosolenia occurs by thesocyte. Thesocytes have lobose pseudopodia and are filled with food reserves, thus, acting as storage cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Class Crustacea differs from Insecta in having :
questions.solution:
The main characteristics of class Crustacea and Insecta are as follows :
(1) Class Crustacea : Mostly aquatic with gills, two pairs of antennae and atleast three pairs of jaws, appendages mostly biramous and always more than four in the trunk region.
Example : prawn, crab, lobster etc.
(2) Class Insecta : Hexapod arthropods with a pair of antennae and three pairs of Jaws.
Example : cockroach, grasshopper, bees, housefly etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following character is present essentialy in chordates ?
options: Blood flow in forward direction in ventral blood vessels
questions.solution:
Chordate animals, at some time in their life history, exhibit the following three diagnostic charcters :
(1) Presence of notochord.
(2) Presence of dorsal tubular nerve cord.
(3) Presence of gill clefts during development.
Beside these, some other characters are :
(4) Ventral heart.
(5) Hepatic portal system.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which insect is useful for us?
questions.solution: Bombyx mori is a silk producing insect, which is reared on mulberry leaves for commercial production of silk whereas mosquito, Musca and Periplanata are harmful arthropodes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Animals of class-Mammalia have
questions.solution: The number of cervical vertebrae are seven in almost all mammals including human beings.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Mantle, foot and shell are the characteristics of
questions.solution: Mantle, foot and shell are characteristics of a mollusc. Out of the given options Nautilus is a mollusc, it is a tetrabranch cephalopod. The shell is external and coiled over the head. Nauctilus is always active at night and rests on bottom during day.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Moulting hormone is secreted by
questions.solution: Moulting hormone (ecdysone) is secreted by prothoracic gland. These glands are paired, bilateral sheet of cells in the thorax. In Periplaneta, this endocrine gland is $X$-shaped. This gland is stimulated by prothoracicotropic hormone. Ecdysone, by causing transformation from larval to pupal stage, initiates post embryonic growth in insects.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not an insect?
questions.solution: Spider is not an insect. It belongs to the order Araneae of the class-Arachnida. It bears four pairs of walking legs unlike insects (have three pairs of walking legs). Loust, Lepisma and Termites are come in the category of insects.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which cells are found only in sponges?
questions.solution: Choanocytes are found only in sponges. The choanocyte is an ovoid cell with free ends bearing a transparent contractile collar. The collar surrounds a single, long flagellum. Choanocytes are used in feeding and for ensuring flow of water within the animal's body.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The scientist, who described the birds as glorified reptiles, was
questions.solution: Huxley stated that birds are glorified reptiles. The missing link Archaeopteryx is a fossil lizard bird. It was founded by Andreas Wagner (1861) from upper Jurassic limestone rocks of Solenbofen in Bowaria, Germany.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What is left when bath sponges dries up?
questions.solution: Euspongia is called bath spongia. The sponge are classified on the basis of skeleton which is made up of spongin fibere. After the bath sponge dries only the skeleton is left.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Mollusca, eye is present over a stalk, called
questions.solution: In Mollusca, each eye is located upon a stumpy peduncle called ommatophore. In Mollusca, a loose skin fold, called mantle, dorsally covers most of the body and encloses a large mantle cavity. The mantle cavity contains the rectum, genital duct, a penis in males and a small bipectinate chemoreceptor called osphradium. In Mollusca, the large oval aperture or mouth of the shell can be tightly closed by a thick, plate-like operculum attached to the foot.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The group of Anamniota includes
questions.solution: Amnion is an extraembryonic membrane that surrounds embryo in reptiles, birds and mammals. The vertebrate animals which lack amnion are known as anamniotes, eg, fishes and amphibians.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In contrast to annelids the platyhelminthes show
questions.solution: Platyhelminths do not have a coelomic or pseudocoelomic cavity for digestion of food as found in higher organisms. Because these organisms obtain digested food directly.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Core zone, buffer zone and manipulation zone are found in
questions.solution: Biosphere reserves are a special category of protected areas of land and/or coastal environments, wherein people are an integral component of the ecosystem. It represents a specified area zonated for particular activity and consists of core zone without any human activity, buffer zone with limited human activities and manipulation zone with several manipulating human activities.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Newly formed Biosphere Reserve of India (2013) is
questions.solution: Panna Biosphere Reserve is situated in Chhattarpur Districts of Madhya Pradesh in India.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select the incorrect statement.
options: Stellar's sea cow and passenger pigeon got extinct due to over exploitation by men
The mitotic convention on biological diversity was held in 1992
Species diversity increase as we move away from the equator towards the poles
Lantana and Eichhornia are invasive weed species in India
questions.solution: Species diversity on earth is not uniformly distributed but shows interesting patterns. It is generally highest in the tropics and decreases towards the poles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following species are restricted to an area?
questions.solution: Endemic species represents a population that are restricted geographically to a particular area in a given time.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Identify the diagram of heterocyst.
questions.solution:
The diagram in option (a) is heterocyst. These are specialised nitrogen- fixing cells formed during nitrogen starvation by some filamentous cyanobacteria such as Nostoc shown in option (a). Hetrocyst create microanaerobic environment for functioning of nitrogenase enzyme around the cell that help in fixation of free atomospheric nitrogen.
The organisms in other option are as follows
1. Euglena
2. Paramecium
3. Mushroom (Agaricus)

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cell wall of fungi is composed of
questions.solution: The cell wall of fungi is composed of chitin. It is the second most abundant carbohydrate. It is a homopolymer of N -Acetyl Glucosamine (NAG) joined with $\beta$ 1-4 linkages. NAG is a modification of glucose molecule.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not a derivative of cholesterol?
questions.solution: Among the given options, vitamin- B is not a derivative of cholesterol. It (cholesterol) is a type of lipid molecule. It is an essential structural component of all animal cell membranes. It also serves as a precursor for the biosynthesis of steroid hormones, bile salts and vitamin-D.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What base is responsible for hot spots for spontaneous point mutations?
questions.solution: Hot spots in any region in a gene that mutates at very high frequency than the neighbouring regions of the gene.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Macromolecule chitin is
questions.solution:
Macromolecule chitin is an example complex structural hetero polysaccharide. It has nitrogen containing glucose derivatives such as N -acetyl glycosamine.
It is present as the structural component of fungal cell walls and exoskeleton of arthropods etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is the 21 st amino acid?
questions.solution: Selenocysteine ( Sec ) is the 21 st amino acid in the genetic code. This amino acid contains selenium.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Among the following choose the correct epimers.
questions.solution: Epimers are optical isomers that differ in the configuration of a single carbon atom. In the question, glucose and galactose sugars are example of epimers. They differ only in the configuration at $\mathrm{C}-4$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: If the free energy change of a reaction is greater than zero, then the reaction is
questions.solution: An endergonic reaction (also called a non-spontaneous reaction) is a chemical reaction in which the standard change in free energy is positive and energy is absorbed.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Citrulline structure
questions.solution:
The correct structure of citrulline is given in option (a).
Citrulline is an amino acid. It is made from ornithine and carbamoyl phosphate in one of the control reactions in urea cycle.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Proteins are needed in diet because
options: all amino acids are not available in body
proteins act as building blocks of our body
questions.solution: Proteins are polymers of amino acids. These are divided into two categories; essential and nonessential amino acids. Essential amino acids cannot be synthesised in the body and therefore must be included in the diet. Proteins are used as structural components of tissues as channels, transporters, regulatory molecules and enzymes. Proteins can also be utilised as energy sources. Normally, this is a secondary function and becomes important only when there is not enough carbohydrate and fat in the body, e.g. during fasting.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match the enzyme in column I with its function in column II and choose the correct option.
$\begin{array}{|l|l|}
\hline \text{Column I} & \text{Column II} \\
\hline \text{A. } \beta-\text{galactosidase} & 1. \text{Joining of DNA fragments} \\
\hline \text{B. Permease} & 2. \text{Peptide bond formation} \\
\hline \text{C. Ligase} & 3. \text{Hydrolysis of lactose} \\
\hline \text{D. Ribozyme} & 4. \text{Increases permeability to } \beta-\text{galactosidase} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
questions.solution: $\beta$-gal, coded by $Z$ structural gene is primarily responsible for the hydrolysis of the diasaccharide lactose into its monomeric units, galactose and glucose.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following represent uridylic acid?
questions.solution: It is a nucleoside of uridine formed in hydrolysis of RNA.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The statement All biological catalysts are proteins is no longer valid after the discovery of
questions.solution: Ribozyme is a RNA molecule (non-proteinaceous) and is capable of catalysing reaction's.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Many elements are found in living organisms either free or in form of compounds. One of the following is negligible is living organisms
questions.solution: Silicon is a micronutrient / trac element which is found only in few organisms.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Given below is the chemical formula of
questions.solution: $\mathrm{C}_3\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_{14}\mathrm{COOH}$ is the chemical formula of palmitic acid. It is a saturated fatty acid.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one out of (a) to (d) given below correctly represents the structural formula of the basic amino acid?
questions.solution: Basic amino acids have an additional amino group without forming amides thus, they are diaminomonocarboxylic acids. e.g., lysine arginine, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following structural formula of two organic compounds is correctly identified along with its related function?
options: A-lecithin - a component of cell membrane
B-adenine - a nucleotide that makes up nucleic
questions.solution: 'A' is a structural formula of lecithin. It is probably the most common phospholipid. Phospholipids are major components in the lipid bilayers of cell membrane.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Lactose is composed of
questions.solution:
Lactose is popularly known as milk sugar. It is a disaccharide composed of one molecule of glucose and one molecule of galactose.
It is a reducing sugar.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Nucleotides are formed by
questions.solution: The nucleotide is formed by the union of a phosphate group with a nucleoside. A nucleoside infact contains a sugar molecule alongwith an organic nitrogenous base. Thus, a nucleotide contains an organic nitrogenous base (purine or pyrimidine) alongwith a sugar molecule and a phosphate group, i.e., Nucleoside $=$ Sugar molecule + organic nitrogenous base.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: An example of competitive inhibition of an enzyme is the inhibition of
questions.solution: Classical example of competitive inhibition is reduction of activity of succinate dehydrogenase by malonate, oxaloacetate and other anions. In competitive inhibition, a competitive inhibitor which has the resemblance with substrate molecule, competes with the substrate for the active site of an enzyme. While the inhibitor (I) occupies the active site, it prevents binding of the substrate to the enzyme.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: During strenuous exercise, glucose is converted into
questions.solution: During strenuous exercise, deficiency of oxygen takes place. So, anaerobic respiration of glucose takes place. During anaerobic respiration, pyruvic acid (a product of glycolysis of glucose) change into lactic acid. Accumulation of lactic acid is responsible for muscle fatigue. Glycogen is a polysaccharide and storing material of animal body. Glycogen stored in muscle and liver. Starch is a polysaccharide and storing material of plant body.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one is component of Ornithine cycle?
questions.solution: Ornithine cycle or Krebs-Henseleit cycle or urea cycle was discovered by Hans Krebs andKurt Henseleit. It takes place in liver cells for synthesis of urea. The main component of ornithine cycle are arginine, ornithine and citrulline.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Paraffin wax is
questions.solution: Waxes are esters formed between a long chain alcohol and saturated fatty acids. This material is typically pliable and soft when warm but hard and water resistant when cold, eg, paraffin wax. Steroids are complex compounds commonly found in cell membranes and animal hormones. The best known of these is the sterolcalledcholesterol which reinforces the structure of cell membrane in animal cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: There was so much......material in the speech that it was difficult to know what the speaker wanted to say.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Non-essential amino acid is
questions.solution: Among the given options, arginine is a non-essential amino acid. Such amino acids can be made within the body and so are not essential to the human diet. Valine, histidine and lysine are essential amino acids.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match the biological molecule listed under column I with their biological function listed under column II. Choose the answer which gives correct combination of alphabets of the two columns.
$\begin{array}{ll|ll}
\hline & \text{Column I} & & \text{Column II} \\
& \text{(Biological Molecule)} & & \text{(Function)} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Glycogen} & \text{1.} & \text{Hormone} \\
\text{B.} & \text{Globulin} & \text{2.} & \text{Biocatalyst} \\
\text{C.} & \text{Steroid} & \text{3.} & \text{Antibody} \\

Codes
questions.solution: Glycogen is stored in liver and muscler of animals. All steroid hormones are derived from cholesterol. Immunoglobulin is an antibody that freely circulates in blood plasma. Thrombin brings about the conversion of fibrinogen to fibrin during blood clotting mechanism.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Omega 3 fatty acid is present in
questions.solution: Omega 3 fatty acid is present in flax seed oil. These are Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids (PUFAs). They are important for normal metabolism. Mammals are unable to synthesise omega 3 fatty acids.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions given below it. Journalism combines writing with news gathering and interpretation. While the journalist's work obviously varies from newspaper to newspaper and from magazine to magazine, all journalists are as much research workers, as they are writers. They cannot write their news or feature stories, unless they locate it and research them first. They must be able to read the in-between lines of the main source-news and interpret that. A great many stories hunt the journalist, who rejects most of them. He carefully sorts and sifts those, taking only a relatively very small proportion of news, may be, coming from an unexpected source. A successful journalist may gather news to the tune of 100 percent but he can use them for his profession only 3-5 percent. He must be able to see or forecast to himself, the news of tomorrow or the day after, from the news of today; because newspapers want advance news or advance warning to give a good, exclusive and exhaustive coverage to anything of interest. But a real 'scoop' is a very rare event. And bogus scoops also bring disrepute to the newspaper.Question: What does the passage say about successful journalists?
options: They reject a major portion of the gathered news
They can use a relatively small portion of the news
Their news come from unexpected sources
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cell theory was proposed by
questions.solution: M J Schleiden and T Schwann (1838-1839) proposed cell theory.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pneumatic bones are expected to be found in
questions.solution: Bones of Aves (eg, pigeon) are pneumatic. Pneumatic bones contain air cavities to reduce weight. Pneumatic bones help in aerial mode of life.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The back bone of RNA consists of which of the following sugar?
questions.solution: The back bone of RNA is made up of ribose sugar (5-carbon) whereas DNA consists of deoxyribose sugar.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not a pyrimidine ?
questions.solution:
The nitrogenous bases are of two types :
(a) Pyrimidine base-cytosine, thymine
(b) Purine base-adenine, guanine.
These nitrogenous bases are found in DNA/RNA. Uracil is found in RNA in place of thymine.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Carbohydrates, the most abundant biomolecules on earth, are produced by:
options: fungi, algae and green plant cells
some bacteria, algae and green plant cells
questions.solution:
Some photosynthetic bacteria such as Rhodopseudomonas can prepare carbohydrates. But during this type of food synthesis $\mathrm{O}_2$ does not evolved because in this case hydrogen donor is other than $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. Algae (green and blue green) and all green plant cells prepare their food (carbohydrate) through photosynthesis.
Here, hydrogen ions are donated by water molecules by the process of photolysis of water i.e., $\mathrm{O}_2$ is released during this type of food synthesis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A nucleotide is a molecule consisting of a :
questions.solution:
A nucleotide is a nucleoside (sugar + nitorgen base) with phosphate group.
Thus, nucleoside $=$ sugar + nitrogenous base nucleotide $=$ nucleoside + phosphate group

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Non-protein part of enzyme is known as :
questions.solution:
The enzyme complex (holoenzyme) is made up of two parts i.e., protein part (apoenzyme) and non-protein part (cofactor). Cofactor is of three types -
(i) prosthetic group
(ii) coenzymes
(iii) metal ions
Holoenzymes $=$ Apoenzyme + Prosthetic group (Enzyme complex) (Protein part) (Non-protein part).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The basic unit of chitin is
questions.solution: The chitin (= polyglycosamine) is an acetate of mucopolysaccharide called glycosamine which is formed by the combination of polysaccharide with small peptide molecules. The basic unit (monomer) of chitin is $N$-acetylglucosamine, monomers are joined by $1-4 \beta$ linkages.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Phage genome site on bacterial chromosome resulted in the structure
questions.solution: Prophage is the DNA of bacteriophage that is repressed for lytic functions and is maintained in the host bacterium in a stable state. The phage genome is integrated into DNA of its bacterial host and may be replicated along with the host DNA, as in the case or bacteriophage lamda or may be maintained as extra chromosomal DNA, as in the case for bacteriophage, lambda $P_1$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Jacob and Monod named some enzymes as allosteric whose activity is regulated by
questions.solution: Allosteric modulation or feed back inhibition of enzymes is influenced by end product. It was shown by Jacob and Monod 1961 through lac operon in E.coli.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Arachidonic acid is
questions.solution: Arachidonic acid is polyunsaturated (i.e., have more than one double bond) and essential fatty acids. Essential fatty acids cannot be synthesised by the body of animal and has to be taken from outside to fulfil the body requirement. Linoleic acid and linolenic acid are also essential fatty acids.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following amino acids is not optically active?
questions.solution: Glycine is the simplest and smallest amino acid. A compound shows optical activity, when a chiral carbon or asymmetrical carbon (ie, carbon which attached to four different groups or atoms) is present. Because chiral carbon is absent in glycine. So, it does not show optical activity. Other amino acids contain at least single asymmetric carbon atoms, so they show optical activity, eg, valine, leucine and isoleucine.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Apoenzyme is
questions.solution:
Enzymes are proteinaceous molecules. Simple enzymes are composed of only protein, while holoenzymes are composed of protein part (apoenzyme) and non-protein part (prosthetic group).
group).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is the simplest amino acid?
questions.solution: Proteins are polymers of amino acids in which amino acids are joined by peptide bonds. Glycine has the simplest structure.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In DNA, which is absent?
questions.solution: Uracil remains absent in DNA. It is present in RNA. The nitrogen bases of DNA and RNA are as follows :

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
The enzyme hexokinase which catalysis glucose to a glucose-6-phosphate in glycolysis is inhibited by glucose-6-phosphate. This is an example of
I. competitive inhibition
II. non-competitive inhibition
III. feedback allosteric inhibition
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution: Hexokinase catalyses the phosphorylation of hexose in general and is found in all cells that metabolise glucose. It has low $K_m$ (high affinity, strong bonding) with glucose in such a way that it is active at low glucose concentration. It shows allosteric feed beat inhibition by to product glucose-6-phosphate.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The Sub-units of 80 S ribosome will be
questions.solution: 60 S and 40 S are sub-units of eukaryotic (80S) ribosomes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The chromosome in which centromere is situated close to one end are
questions.solution: In acrocentric chromosome, the centromere is situated close to one end of the chromosome.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The powerhouse of cell is first discovered by
questions.solution: Mitochondria which is also known as the power house of the cell was first discovered by Kolliker in 1850. It is the site of cellular respiration.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one is only found in plant?
questions.solution: Out of the given options, glyoxysomes are exclusively found in plant cells only. They were discovered by Breuers (1919) in endosperm of germinating seeds. They are involved in fat metabolism through glyoxylate cycle.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Statement I: Microtubules are formed only in animal cells.
Statement II: Microtubules are made up of a protein called myosin.
Choose the correct option
options: Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect.
Statement II is correct and statement I is incorrect.
questions.solution: Microtubules are found in both animal and plant cells and are formed of protein tubulin.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What is common between a eukaryotic and prokaryotic flagella?
options: Both are extension of cell membrane
questions.solution: The common feature between eukaryotic and prokaryotic flagella is that both are used for locomotion. Prokaryotic flagellum is single stranded while eukaryotic flagellum is 11-stranded. Eukaryotic flagellum has two parts, basal body and shaft, while prokaryotic flagellum consists of three parts, basal body, hook and filament. Prokaryotic and eukaryotic flagellum are made up of flagellin and tubulin proteins, respectively.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: To make chromosomal studies easier, chromosomes are classified into certain groups. So, the chromosome number 21,22 and Y are listed in
questions.solution:
To make chromosomal studies easier, chromosomes are classified into certain groups like A to G. So chromosome number 21, 22 and Y are listed in G.
Group A contains 1-3 chromosomes
B contains 4-5 chromosomes
C contains 6-12 chromosomes and X-chromosome
D contains 13-15 chromosomes
E contains $16-18$ chromosomes
F contains 19-20 chromosomes
G contains 21-22 chromosomes and Y chromosome

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: PPLO are smallest cell in the living world. The extend form of PPLO is
questions.solution: Pleuro Pneumonia Like Organism (PPLO) is smallest organism or cell containing size of 0.1 to 0.3 mm .

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match the following column I and II for organismic respiration.
$\begin{array}{l|l}
\hline \text{Column I} & \text{Column II} \\
\hline \text{A. Respiration in bacteria} & \text{1. Mitochondria} \\
\hline \text{B. Respiration in cyanobacteria} & \text{2. Cytoplasmic membrane} \\
\hline \text{C. Respiration in eukaryotic cell} & \text{3. Mesosomes} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
Codes
questions.solution:
Respiration is carried out by different types of organisms at different sites. In bacteria and cyanobacteria, the mitochondria is not present, so respiration occurs in them in mesosomes.
Eukaryotes have distinct well developed mitochondria which has respiratory enzymes and hence, it is called power house of the cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following sequences represent a possible pathway in the production of a secretory protein?
options: Rough ER $\longrightarrow$ Secretory vesicle $\longrightarrow$ Ribosome $\longrightarrow$ Golgi apparatus
Ribosome $\longrightarrow$ Rough $E R \longrightarrow$ Golgi apparatus $\longrightarrow$ Secretory vesicle
Secretory vesicle $\longrightarrow$ Golgi apparatus $\longrightarrow$ Ribosomes $\longrightarrow$ Rough ER
Rough ER $\longrightarrow$ Ribosomes $\longrightarrow$ Secretory vesicles $\longrightarrow$ Golgi apparatus
questions.solution: Ribosome are the place where protein synthesis takes place $\rightarrow$ rough ER produces proteins and helps them fold properly $\rightarrow$ Golgi bodies does packaging of proteins $\rightarrow$ secretory vesicles does transport of proteins.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ribosomes are particles about $200 Å$ units in diameter consisting of protein and RNA. The percentage of protein and RNA is respectively
questions.solution: Chemically, ribosomes is made up of $r$ RNA $60 \%$ and protein $40 \%$. These are known as protein factories as these are the sites for protein synthesis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is substitution of mitochondria in E. coli?
questions.solution: Mesosomes will be equivalent to mitochondria of eukaryotes because of respiratory enzymes present in aerobic bacteria.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Animal cell differ from plant cell in possessing
questions.solution: Centrosome is a self-propagating cytoplasmic organelle, located near nucleus of animal cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Keeping is view the structure of cell membrane, which one of the following statements is correct with respect to the movements of liquid and proteins from one liquid monolayer to the other (flip flop movement).
options: While proteins can flip flop, liquids can not
Neither lipids, nor proteins can flip flop
Both lipids and proteins can flip flop
While lipids can rarely flip flop, proteins can not
questions.solution: The flip flop movement refers to transfer of lipid molecules from one side of the bilayer to the other, Enzymes are concerned with the enzymatic activity of the membrane.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following organelles does not contain RNA?
questions.solution: The plasmalemma series as the interface between the machinery in the interior of the cell and the Extracellular Fluid (ECF) that bathes all cells. It is devoid of ribosome.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Dutrochet has given the concept about cell in
questions.solution:
In 1824, Pene Dutrochet, a French scientist wrote that all animal and plant tissues were aggregate of globular cells.
15. Scientific name of gharial or gavial is Gavialis gangeticus, which lives in rivers Ganga and Brahamputra.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Polysome is formed by
options: several ribosomes attached to a single mRNA
many ribosomes attached to a strand of endoplasmic reticulum
ribosomes attached to each other in a linear arrangement
questions.solution:
In prokaryotes, ribosomes attach to the $5^{\prime}$ end of $m$ RNA as soon as transcription begins. A bunch of ribosome moves along a single mRNA molecule adding 15 amino acids/second to the polypeptide chain, almost the same speed at which RNA polymerase transcribes the $m$ RNA. The group of ribosomes together with the single mRNA molecules, they are translating is called polysome. The use of polysomes is advantageous to a cell since, the overall rate of protein synthesis is increased compared to the rate that would occur if there were no polysomes.
In eukaryotic cells, the ribosomes are attached to rough endoplasmic reticulum by ribophorin protein. Electron microscopy reveals that membranes of homogenised endoplasmic reticulum disrupt to form closed vesicles called microsomes. Microsomes derived from rough endoplasmic reticulum are studied with ribosomes and are called rough ribosomes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In mitochondria, enzyme cytochrome oxidase is present in
questions.solution: Mitochondria contains various enzymes as follows : (i) Outer membrane Acetyltransferase, glycerophosphatase, phospholipase-A, monoamine oxidase, etc. (ii)Inner membrane Cytochrome oxidase, dehydrogenase, succinate NADH, dehydrogenase, ATPase, etc. (iii)Perimitochondrial space Adenylate kinase, nucleoside diphosphokinase, etc. (iv)Matrix Pyruvate dehydrogenase, citrate synthase, aconitase, isocitrate dehydrogenase, fumarase, $\alpha$-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase, malate dehydrogenase, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following cell organelles stores hydrolytic enzymes?
questions.solution: Lysosomes are the single membrane bound cell organelles, which contain hydrolytic enzymes. These are also known as suicidal bags.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The thickness of unit membrane is
questions.solution: The unit membrane, described by $J$ David Robertson, was considered as $75 Å$ thick trilaminar (3 layered membrane). According to his unit membrane or trilaminar model, unit membrane consists of $35 Å$ thick bimolecular phospholipid layer between two protein layers, each with $20 Å$ thickness.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The phrase 'Omnis cellula e cellula' was given by
questions.solution: In 1855 Rudolf Virchow extended the cell theory and stated that all cells arise from pre-existing cells, i.e., Omnis cellula e cellula.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In prokaryotes, chromatophores are
options: specialised granules responsible for colouration of cells
structures responsible for organising the shape of the organism
inclusion bodies lying free inside the cells for carrying out various metabolic activities
internal membrane systems that may become extensive and complex in photosynthetic bacteria
questions.solution: In some prokaryotes like, photosynthetic bacteria and blue-green algae possess small membrane lined chromatophores, which is similar to but chemically simpler than the chlorophyll of plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What is the role of sterol in cell membrane?
questions.solution: The role of sterol in cell membrane is to provide stability. Steroles are steroids with $8-10$ carbon long aliphatic side chain at carbon 17 and at least one alcoholic hydroxyl. Cholesterol $\left(\mathrm{C}_{27} \mathrm{H}_{45} \mathrm{OH}\right)$ is a common sterol found in many animal cell membranes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is incorrect with reference to chloroplast?
options: Occurs only in cells with aerobic respiration
questions.solution: Option (3) is incorrect about chloroplasts. They are spherical, ovoid or disc-shaped cell organelles. These are found in plants and algae. They are involved in photosynthesis due to the presence of chlorophyll in them. During photosynthesis, $\mathrm{O}_2$ is released. They are not associated with any type of respiration, i.e. aerobic and anaerobic and can be found in any type of cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Balbiani rings are the structural features of
questions.solution: Balbiani rings are large RNA puffs reported in the salivary gland chromosomes of Chironomus insect during larval development.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is the correct sequence of modification and transport of secretory vesicles?
options: RER $\rightarrow$ Cell membrane $\rightarrow$ Secretory vesicles $\rightarrow$ Golgi body
RER $\rightarrow$ Cell membrane $\rightarrow$ Secretory vesicles $\rightarrow$ Golgi body
SER $\rightarrow$ Golgi body $\rightarrow$ Cell membrane $\rightarrow$ Secretory vesicles
questions.solution:
The correct sequence of modification and transport of secretory vesicles is follows
RER $\rightarrow$ Golgi body $\rightarrow$ Secretory vesicles $\rightarrow$ Cells membrane
Protein destined for secretion are made on ribosomes bound to the RER. The proteins move through the endomembrane system and are dispatched from the trans face of the Golgi apparatus in transport vesicles that move through the cytoplasm and then fuse with the plasma membrane releasing the protein to the outside of the cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Aspirin is a/an
questions.solution: Antipyretic drug is the drug which decreases body temperature, eg, aspirin. Antibiotics are drugs, which inhibit growth of microorganism, eg, penicillin. Antiseptics are substances which desroy the living organism from living surfaces like skin, eg, Savlon.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Adenosine triphosphate was discovered by
questions.solution: Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) was discovered by Karl Lohmann in 1929 from muscles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chicory powder which is mixed with coffee powder is obtained from
questions.solution: Chicory powder is a flavouring material, which is mixed with coffee powder (from seeds of Coffea arabica). It is obtained from roots of Cichorium intybus of family-Compositae. Tea is dried leaves of Thea sinensis (family Theaceae). Ephedrine is obtained from stem of Ephedra gerardiana. It is useful against asthma and as cardiac stimulant.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Endoplasmic reticulum is in continuation with
questions.solution: Endoplasmic reticulum is the network of delicate strands and vesicles which extends from cell membrane to nuclear membrane. It acts as a circulatory system of cell and is of two typesrough endoplasmic reticulum and smooth endoplasmic reticulum. Rough endoplasmic reticulum involves in protein synthesis along with attached ribosomes and smooth endoplasmic reticulum helps in lipid and steroid synthesis. Cell wall is the outermost covering of plant cell generally made up of cellulose. Golgi bodies are double membrane bound cell organelles of eukaryotic cells which consist of vesicle, tubule and cisternae. Mitochondria is a rod-shaped, double membrane bound cell organelles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Contractile vacuole in protozoan Amoeba is meant for
questions.solution: Contractile vacuole is the clear rounded, pulsating body present in the posterior part of endoplasm of Amoeba. It is found only in fresh water forms and is mainly concerned with osmoregulation, ie, removal of excess of water. Plasmalemma, a trilaminar and selectively permeable membrane covering the body, functions both in excretion (ammonia) and respiration in Amoeba.Pseudopodia (false feet) are finger-like and blunt-extensions of cell body in protozoans. In Amoeba, the type of pseudopodium found in lobopodium, composed of both ectoplasm and endoplasm. It is temporary locomotory structure which is also meant for feeding in Amoeba. Locomotion of Amoeba is known as'amoeboid movement' (Sol-gel theory).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The vacuole is lined by a membrane called
questions.solution: The vacuole is lined by a membrane called tonoplast.Jacket layer is present around archegonia and antheredia in bryophytes.Tonoplasm is the content or liquid present inside the vacuole enclosed by a tonoplast membrane.Cell membrane is the semi-permeable membrane enclosing the protoplasmic material of a cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
choose the alternative which is most nearly the same in meaning to the word given in italics in the sentence.
Some people adopt excellent manners only to  < i>camouflage < /i> their real character.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
choose the alternative which can be substituted for the given words/sentence.
A man who is having the qualities of woman
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Given below is a schematic break-up of the phases/stages of cell cycle. Which one of the following is the correct indication of the stage/ phase in the cell cycle?
questions.solution: In cell cycle there are two main phases-interphase and mitotic phase. Interphase is divided into three stage $\mathrm{G}_1, \mathrm{~S}$ and $G_2 . G_1$ is first growth phase. $S$ is synthetic phase and $G_2$ is second growth phase.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The reabsorption of water in the kidney is under the control of
questions.solution: Antidiuretic hormone (ADH) controls the tubular permeability and concentration of sodium in the extracellular fluid. It associates with the renal pressure system and then regulates the fluid volume of body through the agency of hypothalamic osmoreceptors.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
choose the alternative which can be substituted for the given words/sentence.
A word no longer in use
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Accessory sexual character in female is promoted by
questions.solution: Estrogens are secreted by cells of Graafian follicles. Estradiol is the principal feminizing estrogen. It stimulates the development of female secondary sex characteristics during puberty and maintains them through the reproductive years of adult life.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Who for the first time developed electron microscope ?
questions.solution: Knoll and Ruska discovered electron microscope in 1932.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Gases found in primitive atmosphere are
questions.solution: The primitive atmosphere was chiefly consisting of methane, ammonia, water vapour, hydrogen gas, nitrogen gas and some carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A genophore is made up of
questions.solution: Bacteria being prokaryotes, lack organized nucleus. Instead of a ring-shaped, double helical, double stranded, single DNA molecule is present. The incipient nucleus or primitive nucleus is named as nucleoid or genophore.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: RNA is not found in
questions.solution: RNA is not found in plasmalemma. Plasmalemma is the cell membrane which also lines the connecting plasmodesmata.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: During DNA replication in prokaryotes DNA is anchored to
questions.solution: On the plasma membrane of bacteria generally at mid point, there are present some circular coiled bodies called mesosomes. Mesosomes are more prominent in Gram +ve bacteria. Mesosomes receive DNA during conjugation and DNA replicaton enzyme.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Membrane is absent in
questions.solution: Nucleolus is a darkly stained granular, naked organelle with no limiting membrane. It is formed of proteinaceous part pars amorpha, fibrillar zone and granular zone of ribonucleoprotein and granules.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A student wishes to study the cell structure under a light microscope having 10X eyepiece and 45 X objective. He should illuminate the object by which one of the following colours of light so as to get the best possible resolution?
questions.solution:
Resolving power or resolution is the ability of the lens to distinguish fine details and structure. Specifically it refers to the ability of the lenses to distinguish between two points at a specified distance apart. Resolving power depends on two factors :
(i) Wavelength of light used for illumination
(ii) Power of objective lenses
Resolving power $=\frac{\text { Wavelength of light }}{2 \times \mathrm{NA}}$
Since the limit of resolving power of a microscope is fixed by the structure of light, the shortest wavelength of visible light will give the maximum resolution. Among yellow, green, red and blue light colour. Blue (500 nm) have shortest wavelength so, it will give best resolution.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chlorophyll in chloroplasts is located in
questions.solution: Chloroplast, which is a cytoplasmic cell organelle finds only in eukaryotic plant cells. These structures help in the manufacture of food through, photosynthesis. Chlorophyll is a specialized light absorbing pigment which is found in the inner wall of granum. Each granum is a flat, sac like structure in which light reaction of photosynthesis takes place.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Genes for cytoplasmic male sterility in plants are generally located in
questions.solution:
Mitochondria are the eukaryotic cell organelles (do not present in prokaryotic cells). These originate from pre-existing mitochondria only. Mitochondria are also known as semi autonomous organelles because they contain a circular, double stranded DNA molecule, RNA and 70 S type of ribosomes. The genes, located outside of nucleus (i.e., within the cytoplasm) also governes some traits, are referred to as plasmogenes or cytoplasmic genes.
Cytoplasmic male sterility (i.e., dominance of female cytoplasmic genes over male) is due to plasmogenes located in mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cell membrane in animals is composed mainly of the molecule of :
questions.solution: Chemically, the plasma membrane or cell membrane is made up of $60 \%$ proteins and $40 \%$ lipids. The percentage of carbohydrates ranges from 1-10 which are in the form of glycoproteins or glycolipids. Proteins may be extrinsic (peripheral) and intrinsic (integral) while lipids may be phospholipids, glycolipids and sterols.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one is single membrane cell organelle?
questions.solution: Endoplasmic reticulum, mitochondria and chloroplast are double membraned cell organelles whereas lysosome is a single membraned cell organelle.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pigment anthocyanin is located in :
questions.solution: Vacuoles are the storage depots of cells, surrounded by a single membrane. They contain excess water, waste products, mineral elements, cell sap and sometimes a soluble pigment anthocyanin also.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cell theory was propounded in 1838-39 by :
questions.solution: The cell theory or cell doctrine is credited to Matthias Schleiden (1838) a botanist of Germany and Theodor Schwann (1839), a zoologist also of Germany.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The thylakoid in chloroplast are arranged as
questions.solution: Most of the lamellae (folding forms a series of parallel membranous sheets) in the chloroplasts are organised to form sac like structures called thylakoids. These thylakoids may be stacked like a pile of coins, this formation has been termed as grana. Chlorophyll found in grana and light reaction of photosynthesis occur in grana.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In bacteria respiration occurs in
questions.solution: In bacteria, respiration occurs in cytoplasmic membrane because bacteria is a prokaryotic cell (true nucleus and membrane bounded organalle are absent).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Spindle fibre is made up of
questions.solution: 'Tubulin' is a cytoskeletal globular protein that polymerizes to formmicrotubules. It plays a motile and contractile role in cell structure and cell division. During cell division, the microtubules radiate from each end of the cell form a basket like arrangement (the spindle) which helps in the movement of chromosomes to poles. The principal protein of cilia and flagella is also tubulin. Microtubules play a key role in the movement of cilia and flagella.Intermediate filaments are tough rope-like bundles or protein (keratin, desmin, peripherin, vimentin etc). Intermediate filaments provide mechanical stability to cells. They interact with microfilaments (made of protein, actin) and microtubules to form cytoskeleton.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which type of enzyme is present in lysosome?
questions.solution: Lysosomes or suicidal bags are bounded by single unit membrane. It contains hydrolytic enzymes which help to digest the nucleic acids, proteins and polysaccharide etc. (i.e., extracellular material). It also helps in autolysis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Phospholipids are
questions.solution: The phospholipid is a bipolar molecule having long fatty acid called hydrophobic tail (water hating) or non-polar end. Another is hydrophillic (water loving) or polar end, hence, it is amphipathic in nature.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: L-shaped chromosomes are also called
questions.solution: The shape of the chromosome is determined by the position of centromere on the chromosome. Chromosomes may be of following five types on the basis of their shape. (i) Acentric- Centromere remains absent. (ii)Telocentric- Centromere present at one end of chromosome. (iii)Acrocentric- When chromosome is divided into a very small segment at one end and a very large segment on the other end. (iv)Submetacentric- When chromosome have two unequal segments, one of which is slightly larger than other and forms L-shaped chromosome. (v)Metacentric- When two segments are equal and forming $V$-shaped structure.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
In the following questions, a part of the sentence is italicised. Below the sentence, three possible substitutions for the italicised part are given. Choose the one which improves the italicised part. If none of the substitutions improves the italicised part, your answer is (d).
I was delighted to see  < i>him fully recover < /i>.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which type of DNA is found in bacteria?
questions.solution: Bacteria are prokaryotic in nature, in which typical chromosomes are lacking. DNA is circular and naked as it is not surrounded by his tones (basic proteins).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The main organelle involved in modification and routing of newly synthesized proteins to their destinations is
questions.solution: The main organelle involved in modification and routing of newly synthesized proteins to their destinations is endoplasmic reticulum. The endoplasmic reticulum serves many general functions, including the folding of protein molecules in sacs called cisternae and the transport of synthesized proteins in vesicles to the Golgi apparatus. Correct folding of newly made proteins is made possible by several endoplasmic reticulum chaperone proteins.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which organelle is present in higher number in secretory cells?
questions.solution: Dictyosome or Golgi complex is present in higher number in secretory cells. All glandular cells depend upon Golgi complex for concentrating and pouring their secretion to outside.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Protein synthesis in an animal cell occurs
options: only on the ribosomes present in cytosol
on ribosomes present in cytoplasm as well as in mitochondria
only on ribosomes attached to the nuclear envelope and endoplasmic reticulum
on ribosomes present in the nucleolus as well as in cytoplasm
questions.solution: Protein synthesis takes place on ribosomes. In an eukaryotic cell ribosomes are present in cytoplasm, mitochondria and chloroplasts. So, in these places protein synthesis take place.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Match the following column

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: During meiosis-I, the bivalent chromosomes clearly appear as tetrads during
questions.solution: During meiosis-I, in pachytene phase of prophase-I the bivalent chromosomes clearly appear as tetrads for crossing over.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Post mitotic gap phase is characterised by all, except
questions.solution: Post mitotic gap phase is characterised by all except synthesis of histone proteins. Histone proteins are synthesised in S-phase. S-phase is called invisible phase of the cell cycle as replicated chromosomes are not visible at this stage.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Above diagram represents
questions.solution: The given figure is showing anaphase-I of meiosis. It begins when the two chromosomes of each bivalent separate and start moving towards opposite poles of the cell. Unlike the anaphase of mitosis, the sister chromatids remain attached at their centromeres and move together toward the poles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match the following column I with column II.
$\begin{array}{ll|ll}
\hline & \text{Column I} & & \text{Column II} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Carcinogen} & \text{1.} & \text{Cancerous tumour} \\
\text{B.} & \text{Anaphase-I} & \text{2.} & \text{Disjunction} \\
\text{C.} & \text{Mitosis} & \text{3.} & \text{Synapse} \\
\text{D.} & \text{Zygotene} & \text{4.} & \text{Plectonemic coiling} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
Codes
questions.solution:
Carcinogen causes cancerous tumour disjunction of chromosomes takes place in anaphase-I and II when chromosomes more to the opposite poles of the cell.
Plectonemic coiling refers to inter-twining of a double helix DNA molecule in such a way that for its correction unwinding of DNA helix molecule is required, this take place in mitosis in which sister chromatids are tightly coiled upon each other. In zygotene stage, homologs login to synapse by coming to approximate alignment.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The non-disjunction, in meiosis may result is extra copy of a chromosome in a sperm cell. During which phase the above phenomenon may occur?
questions.solution: In anaphase-I and II chromosomes become shorter and thicker and migrate towards opposite poles of the cell. In case of non-disjunction of chromosomes an extra copy of a chromosome will be present in the cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What will be the DNA content and number of chromosomes in a cell of after S-phase compared to the gamete of the same organism?
options: Same DNA content but double chromosome number
Four times DNA content but double chromosome number
Same DNA content but half chromosome number
Half DNA content but double chromosome number
questions.solution:
The DNA content will be four times and the chromosomes will be double in number in a cell after S - phase as compared to the gamete of the same organism.
