Gametes are haploid(n) cells that contain one set of chromosomes and half amount of DNA content in it.
During S- phase of cell division, only the DNA content of cell gets doubled from 2 n to 4 n while number of chromosome remain same in the dividing cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In some organisms, karyokinesis is not followed by cytokinesis as a result of which, multinucleate condition arises leading to the formation of syncytium. The perfect example for this is
options: appearance of a furrow in cell membrane
questions.solution: Coconut endosperm is unique because of its early liquid syncytical stages which form the hard matured kernel at later stages of fruit development.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Meiosis is best observed in dividing
questions.solution:
Meiosis is best observed in dividing microsporocytes. Microsporocytes or microspore mother cell after meiosis give rise to microspore.
Other cell do not divide by meiosis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Polyploidy can be induced by the application of
questions.solution: Colchicine is a poisonous chemical, isolated from seeds and bulbs of autumn crocus  (Colchicum autumnale). It blocks spindle formation and thus, inhibits the movement of sister chromatids to the opposite poles. The resulting restitution nucleus includes all the chromatids. As a result, the chromosome number of the cell is doubled, which leads to polyploidy.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following precedes re-formation of the nuclear envelope during M-phase of the cell cycle?
options: Decondensation from chromosome and reassembly of the nuclear lamina
Transcription from chromosome and reassembly of the nuclear lamina
Formation of the contractile ring and formation of the phragmoplast
Formaltion of the contractile ring and transcription from chromosomes
questions.solution: At telophase stage, nuclear membrane vesicles associate with the surface of individual chromosomes and fuse to reform the nuclear membranes, which partially enclose clusters of chromosomes before coalescing to reform the complete nuclear envelope. During this process, the nuclear pores reassemble and (the dephosphorylated reassociate to form the nuclear lamina.) One of the lamina proteins (lamina-B) remains with the nuclear membrane fragments throughout mitosis and may help nucleate ressembly. After the nucleus reforms, the pores pump in nuclear proteins, the chromosome decondense and RNA synthesis resumes, causing the nucleolus to reappear.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: DNA replication occurs in
questions.solution: The S-phase of cell cycle is also called 'synthesis phase', in which the cell synthesizes the replica of its genome, i.e., DNA replication occurs during this stage, which ultimately results to the duplication of chromosomal material.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: During the meiotic division, the
questions.solution: Meiotic division occurs in reproductive or germ cells. Four haploid cells are produced after meiosis, which are not identical to their parent cell. In this division, homologous chromosomes form bivalent by pairing, during zygotene stage of prophase-I and during anaphase-I. Homologous chromosomes of each bivalent start migrating towards opposite poles of the spindle, because of this separation of homologous chromosomes, each daughter cell receives a haploid group of segregated chromosomes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In cell cycle, during which phase, chromosomes are arranged in equatorial plate?
questions.solution: Metaphase in both mitosis and meiosis is characterized by the orientation of chromosomes themselves on the spindle fibres at the equatorial plate.Prophase is the first phage and the longest of all phages. Chromatin of the nucleus become condensed forming chromosomes.Anaphase is characterized by the separation of chromatids. Chromatids move in opposite direction towards the pole. Contraction of spindle fibre occurs. It is the shortest of four phases in duration.Telophase in a sense is reversible process of prophase. Chromatin network are reformed from chromosomes. The nuclear membrane and nucleolus (which disappeared at late prophase) reappears.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A bacterium divides every 35 minutes. If a culture containing \(10^5\) cells \(/ \mathrm{ml}\) is grown in 175 minutes, what will be cell concentration after that period
questions.solution:
As we know that bacterium divided after every 35 minutes through simple mitotic division therefore, number of divisions are $\frac{175}{35}=5$
Since, one bacterium on division produces two cells so, cell concentration after 175 minutes will be
$\begin{aligned}
& =10^5 \times(2)^5 \\
& =32 \times 10^5
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Centromere is required for
questions.solution: Chromosomes are responsible for the transmission of the hereditary information from one generation to the next. The arms of chromosome is known as chromatids. These arms are held together at a point called the centromere (or primary constriction). Centromere occurs any where along the length of chromosome. During cell division spindle fibres are attached to centromere and help in the movement of chromosomes towards the poles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
choose the alternative which is opposite in meaning to the word given in italics in the sentence.
I am somewhat  < i>sceptical < /i> about his claim.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: At what stage of the cell cycle are histone proteins synthesized in a eukaryotic cell ?
questions.solution: Histone proteins are basic proteins and are used in packing of eukaryotic (absent in prokaryotes) DNA. DNA and histones together comprise chromatin, forming bulk of the eukaryotic chromosomes. Histones are of five major kinds $H_1, H_{2 A}, H_{2 B}, H_3$ and $H_4 . H_1$ histones link neighbouring nucleosomes (fundamental packing units of an eukaryotic chromosome), while other are elements of nucleosome structure. During S-phase of cell cycle synthesis of histone proteins take place because at this stage number of chromosomes become double to that of somatic number.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In meiosis division is
questions.solution:
Meiosis I is reductional and Meisois II is equational
In meiosis I, the division is reductional, while in meiosis II, the division is equational. In reduction division, the chromosome number is reduced from diploid (46 chromosomes) to haploid (23 chromosomes) state. Also known as first meiotic division or first meiosis. The second meiotic division is an equational division because it does not reduce chromosome numbers.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Colchicine prevents the mitosis of the cells at which of the following stage?
questions.solution: If a cell is treated with drugs such as Colchicine, vincistrine, the assembly of the microtubules is inhibited, while the dis-assembly continues leading to continues disorganization of microtubules. Colchicine is an alkaloid. It can be extracted from corms of plant Colchicum autumnale and C. luteum. Colchicines prevent spindle formation during mitotic metaphase. Thus, it prevents the separation of chromosomes during anaphase stage. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The chromosome of cell duplicates during the
questions.solution: S-phase (synthesis phase) is the part of the interphase of cell cycle occurring between $\mathrm{G}_1$ and $\mathrm{G}_2$-phase. This is the phase in which DNA replication takes place, i.e. chromosomes are replicated.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Synaptonemal complex is formed
questions.solution: Symptonemol complex is formed during process of meiosis. This is a complex zipper-like structure formed between homologous chromosomes during prophase-I stage of meiosis. It functions as mediator in chromosome pairing, synapsis and recombination.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Above diagram represents
questions.solution: The given diagram represents metaphase-I of meiosis. This stage is more or less similar to metaphase stage of mitosis. The only difference is appearance of paired chromosomes at metaphasic plate.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The mitotic cell cycle is divided typically into four phases: $\mathrm{G}_1, \mathrm{~S}, \mathrm{G}_2$ and M . Considering a mitotic cycle time of 18 hrs ; the distribution of period of time (in hrs) for each of these phases will be
questions.solution:
The longest phase of cell cycle is $\mathrm{G}_1$ where these rapid growth and metabolic activity, followed by S phase for DNA synthesis and replication then $\mathrm{G}_2$ phase for preparation of cell division and lastly mitotic phase.
$\therefore$ Ratio is $9: 5: 3: 1$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Independent assortment of gene occurs due to the orientation of chromosomes at
options: any phase of the cell division
questions.solution: The independent assortment of gene occurs due to the orientation of chromosomes at late metaphase-I of meiotic cell division at the time of gamete formation.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chromosomes are arranged along the equator during
questions.solution: In plant cells, during metaphase chromosomes line up around the equator of the spindle and attached by their centromere to the spindle fibres (microtubules). In animal cells, during metaphase, smaller chromosomes are usually central in position with larger ones peripheral in position.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: During the $\mathrm{G}_1$-phase of cell division
questions.solution: Cell division has two phase : (a) Karyokinesis : Division of nucleus (b) Cytokinesis : Division of cytoplasm. Karyokinesis also consists of two phase : (i) Interphase is a resting phase and consists of $\mathrm{G}_1, \mathrm{~S}$ and $\mathrm{G}_2$-phase. $\begin{array}{|c|l|} \hline \text{Phase} & \text{Activities} \\ \hline \mathrm{G}_1 & \text{RNA and proteins synthesis takes place} \\ \hline \mathrm{S} & \text{DNA replication takes place} \\ \hline \mathrm{G}_2 & \begin{array}{l} \text{Damaged DNA is repaired and division of} \\ \text{centrosomes takes place} \end{array} \\ \hline \end{array}$ (ii)M-phase represents the phase of actual division.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The protein products of tumour suppressor gene may
questions.solution: A tumour suppressor gene or anti oncogene, is a gene that protects a cell from one step on the path to a cancer. It regulates the cell cycle, cause signal cell death and present is non-cancerous cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Benthic organisms are found in
questions.solution: Benthic organism are found at the bottom of the sea. Surface of marine water ecosystem is occupied by planktons white nekton are found on surface and middle of sea ecosystem.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ten percent law of energy transfer in a food chain is given by
questions.solution: According to Lindeman's law of trophic efficiency, the efficiency of energy transfer from one trophic level to next is about $10 \%$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Overlapping region between two ecosystems is called
questions.solution: The zone of transition between two different communities presenting a situation of overlapping is known as ecotone.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Stability of ecosystem depends upon
questions.solution: Network of food chains inter connected at various levels (food web) is meant for increasing the stability of an ecosystem by providing alternative source of food.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The ultimate source of energy for living being is
questions.solution: Sunlight is the ultimate source of energy as it is the only inexhaustible resource, which is utilised by green plants and is passed on to higher tropic levels through food chain.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: From which of the following plant is a medicine for respiratory disorders obtained?
questions.solution: Ephidrine is obtained from several species of Ephedra of family-Ephideraceae. It is used in the treatment of respiratory disorders like cold, asthma, bronchial congestion.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Lichen is the pioneer vegetation on which succession?
questions.solution: The occurrence of relatively definite sequence of communities over a period of time in the same area is known as ecological succession. Lithosere is a type of xerosere originating on bare rock surfaces. The original substratum is deficient in water and lacks any organic matter having only minerals in disintegrated unweathered state. The pioneer vetegation is, therefore, lichens.Hydrosere originating in a pond starts with the colonization of some phytoplanktons which form the pioneer plant community.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The presence of diversity at the junction of territories of two different habitats is known as
questions.solution: The presence of diversity at the junction of terretories of two different habitats is known as edge effect. In other words, the potential for the ecotone to act as habitat for species found in neither major community is called edge effect. In actual, the transition zone between two or more diverse communities is called the ecotone, the population of which becomes more adapted than both the major communities (communities of neighbouring habitats) which are too simple. In addition to this, in this region the density of most of the species is higher than that in the neighbouring.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Decomposers are
questions.solution: Decomposers are organotrophs, which feed on dead bodies of organisms and organic wastes of living organisms.Producers are photosynthetic or autotrophic plants which are able to synthesize organic food from inorganic raw materials with the help of solar radiations.Heterotrophs are some times referred as organotrophs.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following pairs is mismatched?
questions.solution:
Prairies contain tall grasses and shrubs:
Savanna-Acacia trees
Tundra-Permafrost
Coniferous forest-Evergreen trees.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Green plants constitute :
questions.solution: Green plants constitute the first trophic level at these store energy by trapping the light and forming the food through photosynthesis and then passed to other organisms from the plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: If decomposers are removed what will happen to the ecosystem?
options: Rate of decompostion of mineral increases
questions.solution: Decomposers are heterotropic organisms mostly bacteria and fungi which live on dead organic matter or detritus. They release different enzymes from their bodies into the dead and decaying plant and animal remains. The extra cellular digestion of the dead remains, lead to the release of simple inorganic substances.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In terrestrial ecosystem such as forest, maximum energy is found in which trophic level?
questions.solution: In all ecosystem the maximum amount of energy is found in trophic level $1-r$ $\mathrm{t}_1$ (i.e. the producers) which keeps on decreasing along the higher trophic level.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following workers are related as ecologist, palaeobotanist and embryologist respectively?
options: B. Sahni, R. Mishra, P. Maheshwari
R Mishra, B. Sahni, P. Maheshwari
B. Sahni, P. Maheshwari, R Mishra
P. Maheshwari, R Mishra, B. Sahni
questions.solution: Prof. Ramdeo Misra is known as the "father of Ecology in India". Prof. R. Misra obtained Ph.D in Ecology 1937 under Prof. W.H. Pearsall. Prof Misra formulated the first post graduate course in India. Prof. Birbal Sahni is called "father of Indian Palaeobotany". Prof. P. Maheshwari called "father of Indian embryology“. In India for the Ist time he did haploid plant culture (anther and pollen culture). Guha and Maheshwari 1964 successfully cultured the Anther of Datura innoxia on basal medium supplimented with kinetin, coconut milk and fruit juices.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following supports a dense population of plankton and littoral vegetation?
questions.solution: Eutrophication means nutrient enrichment. The main factor that caused eutrophication is the release of large amount of phosphate into water body.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Intermediate community between pioneer and climax communities is called
questions.solution: The first biotic community which develops in a bare area is called pioneer community. Climax community is the stable, self perpetuating and final biotic community that develops at the end of biotic succession. The various biotic communities that develop during biotic succession are termed as seral or transitional communities.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The direction of energy flow is
questions.solution: In an ecosystem, the direction of flow of energy is always in this pattern.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Maximum green house gases are released by
questions.solution: According to Homes etall 1933, USA is responsible for the largest portion of man made contribution to the green house effect $21 \%$ followed by Russia $14 \%$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A lake with an inflow of domestic sewage rich in organic waste may result in
options: drying of the lake very soon due to algal bloom
an increased production of fish due to lot of nutrients
death of fish due to lack of oxygen
increased population of aquatic food web organisms
questions.solution: Oxygen level goes down due to algel bloom. These bloom also release some toxic chemical which kill fishes and other animals.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Carbon monoxide is a pollutant because it
questions.solution: Carbon monoxide, when inhaled, combines with blood haemoglobin to form carboxyhaemoglobin at a rate 210 times faster than the rate of oxygen forms oxyhaemoglobin. Thus, respiration is impaired.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which insecticide is more hazardous to human health?
questions.solution: DDT is the most hazardous, non-biodegradable insecticide, which is fat soluble but insoluble in water. It persists in the environment for a very long period. Being fat soluble, it accumulates in the animal tissues and gets concentrated at different trophic levels of food chain. It each step, DDT is more concentrated. This is also called biomagnification.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Soil salinity is measured by
questions.solution: Conductivity meter measures soil salinity. Porometer is an apparatus for knowing the relative sizes of stomata. Potometer is used for measuring the rate of transpiration.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Eutrophication is caused due to
questions.solution: Eutrophication is caused due to accumulation of minerals in water body. It is the process of natural ageing of lakes by the biological enrichment of its water. In a young lake, the water is clear, supporting little life with time due to the introduction of nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus, growth of microorganism which deteriorate the water quality take place depleting the dissolved $\mathrm{O}_2$ in water.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The Montreal Protocol refers to
options: substances that deplete the ozone layer
questions.solution:
World's major producers and consumers of pollutants met at Montreal (Canada) on September 16,1987 to solve the problem of ozone thining in stratosphere. 24 countries signed the protocol that came into force on january 1, 1989.
This protocol refers to the substances such as CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons), methane that deplete the ozone layer.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ozone depletion in stratosphere would result in
questions.solution: In the atmosphere (stratosphere), about 30 km above the surface of the earth, the ozone molecules $\left(\mathrm{O}_3\right)$ form an umbrella. It prevents the penetration of harmful ultra violet radiation from the sun and thus protects the life of the earth. It is now feared that there is danger of depletion of the ozone layer. The prime suspects of ozone thinning or depletion are chlorofluoro carbons (CFCs). Because it is odourless, invisible, that is why, it is widely used in domestic refrigerators and cold storage facilities. It is calculated that the depletion of ozone layer by $1 \%$ results in an increase in the incidence of skin cancer by $5 \%$ to $7 \%$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is always present in photochemical smog?
questions.solution: Photochemical smog occurs at high temperature over cities and towns due to stull air, emission of nitrogen oxides and hydrocarbons from automobile exhausts and solar energy. Nitrogen dioxide splits into nitric oxide and nascent oxygen. Nascent oxygen combines with molecular oxygen to form ozone.Ozone reacts with hydrocarbons to form aldehydes and ketones.Nitrogen oxide,oxygen andketones combines to formPeroxy Acyl Nitrates (PAN) which is responsible for the eye irritation. This smog was first observed in Los Angeles, so called asLos Angeles smog.Classical (London) Smog was reported in 1952 in London. It occurs at low temperaure, containssulphur gases (hydrogen sulphide, sulphur dioxide), smoke and dust particles.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The soil which is transported by wind is known as
questions.solution: Soil transportation by wind is common in dry regions where soil is chiefly sandy and the vegetation is very poor. Transported soils are those where the weathered material is taken away at other places. Depending on the nature of these transporting agents the transported soil may be (i) Glacial transportation by glaciers (large mass of snow ice) (ii)Eolian Transportation by wind (iii)Alluvial Transportation by running water (iv)Colluvial Transportation by gravity.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Balbiani rings are the structural features of
questions.solution: Balbiani rings are large RNA puffs reported in the salivary gland chromosomes (polytene chromosome) of Chironomus insect during larval development.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Indian rhinoceros are protected in
questions.solution: Rhino (Rhinoceros unicomis) are protected in Kaziranga National park. This park is situated in Assam.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Repressor protein is produced by
questions.solution: The regulator gene is responsible for the synthesis of a protein called repressor. The repressor may be active or inactive (aporepressor). The active repressor is normally seen in inducible systems while aporepressor is seen in repressible system. The repressor has an affinity for operator gene.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ozone depletion in stratosphere would result in
questions.solution: In the atmosphere (stratosphere), about 30 km above the surface of the earth, the ozone molecules $\left(\mathrm{O}_3\right)$ form an umbrella. It prevents the penetration of harmful ultra violet radiation from the sun and thus protects the life on earth. It is now feared that there is danger of depletion of the ozone layer. The prime suspects of ozone thining or depletion are chlorofluoro carbons (CFCs). Because it is odourless, invisible, that is why it is widely used in domestic refrigerators and cold storage facilities. It is calculated that the depletion of ozone layer by $1 \%$ results in an increase in the incidence of skin cancer by $5 \%$ to $7 \%$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: According to Kyoto protocol the major nations abide to reduce concentration of green house gases by
questions.solution: International conference held in Kyoto, Japan obtained commitments from different, countries for reducing overall green house gas emissions at a level 5% below 1990 level by 2008-2012.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Bio-indicators are used for
questions.solution: Any organism, which can be used to characterize particular environmental properties is called biological indicator, eg, pollution oxygenation.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: At which latitude, heat gain through insolation approximately equals heat loss through terrestrial radiation?
questions.solution: At $40^{\circ}$ North and South the heat gain through insolation approximately equal to the heat loss through terrestrial radiation.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: More than $70 \%$ of world's freshwater is contained in
questions.solution: Three fourth surface of earth (about $71 \%$ of total) is occupied by oceans which contain $97.5 \%$ of total water. This is marine water with about $3.5 \%$ salt contents. Rest water i.e., $2.5 \%$ is freshwater, which occurs on land. Most amount of this water (about $1.97\%$) occurs as frozen ice caps and glaciers and $0.5 \%$ freshwater occurs as ground water.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following pairs is mismatched?
questions.solution: Solar energy is not responsible for green house effect instead it is a source of energy for the plants and animals. Green plants prepare their food by the use of this solar energy. $\mathrm{CO}_2$ gas is mainly responsible for green house effect. Excess of this gas forms a thick layer around the earth and prevents re-radiation of entering sun rays to atmosphere. Thus functions like the glass panels of a green house (or the glass windows of a motor car). This is so called green house effect.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Identify the correctly matched pair
questions.solution: In Kyoto protocol (Dec. 1997), it was determined that the climatic changes due to green house gases will be lower down till 2012.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Carrier of fluorosis is :
questions.solution: Fluorosis is a disease caused by the consumption of water with excess fluoride or fluorine.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which important green house gas, other than carbon dioxide, is being produced from the agricultural fields?
questions.solution: In addition to carbon dioxide some other gases also contribute to green-house effect. These include ozone, CFCs, nitrous oxide.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Biological Oxygen Demand (BOD) is a measure of
options: industrial wastes poured into water bodies
extent to which water is polluted with organic compound
amount of carbon monoxide inseparably combined with haemoglobin
amount of oxygen needed by green plants during night
questions.solution: Biological Oxygen Demand (BOD) is a meaure of polluting organic matter present in a sample of water. BOD is higher in polluted sewage water and is connected with both microbes and organic matters. When larger amount of sewage is dumped in to water, the BOD will increase.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Minamata disease is caused due to presence of..... in water.
questions.solution: In water mercury gets changed into water soluble dimethyl form and enters the food chain accompanied by biological amplification. It develops a crippling deformity called Minamata disease.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: If the threshold for hearing increasing 1000 times, the hearing loss is
questions.solution:
A decibel is a measure of the ratio between two sound stimuli. The decibel level of a sound that a normal human can hear and is calculated using formula.
Intensity (in decibles) $=10 \times \log$ (sound/threshhold)
Therefore, the intensity of a sound that is 1000 times, thresh hold is 30 decibels.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following combinations is incorrect?
questions.solution: The historic convention on biological diversity (The earth summit) held in Rio de Janerio was the Rio Conevention, (in 1992). It called upon all nations to take appropriate measure for sustainable utilisation and conservation of biological diversity.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Increase in toxic concentration from one trophic level to another trophic level is called
questions.solution: The phenomenon through which certain pollutants (toxic substances) get accumulated in trophic level in increasing concentrations along the different trophic level is called biological magnification or ecological magnification.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following statement is correct about DDT ?
options: It is a biomagnifying biodegradable pollutant
It is non biomagnifying biodegradable pollutant
It is biomagnifying non biodegradable pollutant
questions.solution: DDT has been recently banned because it is non-biodegradable and biomagnifying pollutant. Biomagnification means the increase in amount of DDT in the body of organism along with the increase in trophic level. Hence, the amount of DDT in 1st trophic level will be minimum and in top consumer will be maximum.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The gases that produce green house effect are
questions.solution: Green house effect is the $\mathrm{CO}_2$ effect. It is due to $\mathrm{CO}_2$ layer in atmosphere and also by $\mathrm{(CFCs)}, \mathrm{SO}_2, \mathrm{NO}_2, \mathrm{CH}_4$. These gases come from factories, automobile, agriculture, refrigerators, deforestation. These gases entrap the solar heat and prevents the escape of heat from earth to outer space, thus increasing the temperature of earth which further lead to global warming.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Agricultural chemicals include
questions.solution: As growth regulators control the growth of plants, pesticides control the pests and fertilizers enhance productivity of the soil, hence all of these are regarded as agricultural chemicals

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which vitamin should not be stored ?
questions.solution: Vitamin-C (ascorbic acid) has virucidal property and its excess amount is excreted in urine.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A lake with nutrients is called
questions.solution: A lake with nutrients is called eutrophic.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In a coal fired power plant, electrostatic precipitators are installed to control emission of
questions.solution: The electrostatic precipitation controls SPM (Suspended particulate matter) in coal fire power plant.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Nitrogen-fixation in root nodules of  Alnus is brought about by
questions.solution:
Nitrogen is the most critical element. Atmosphere is the greatest reservior of nitrogen. Molecular nitrogen cannot be utilized directly by plants. It has to be fixed or converted into compounds prior to utilization. Symbiotic nitrogen fixation is accomplished by Rhizobium species, which occurs on the roots of leguminous plants. Certain non-leguminous plants also form nodules to fix nitrogen. The best known example in temperate region is alder (Alnus sp.) The bacteria involved in nodule formation is an Actinomycetes, the Frankia.
 Clostridium is anaerobic, saprotrophic, free-living nitrogen fixing bacteria. Bradyrhizobium is symbiont in plants of Parasponia and soyabean.
The  Azorhizobium forms both stem and root nodules in Sesbania (aquatic plant).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Presence of tail in a child is an example of
questions.solution: Atavism is the phenomenon of appearance of ancestral character (not parental) in young ones after many generations, eg, presence of tail in a child.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is responsible for biological nitrogen-fixation?
questions.solution: Azo tobacterk and clostrodium are free livingnitrogen fixing are microorganisms. VAM is symbiotic relationship between fungi and higher plants. Nif gene is responsible for biological nitrogen-fixation, which directs the synthesis of nitrogenase enzyme.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not a micronutrient?
questions.solution: Magnesium (Mg) is a macronutrient not a micronutrient. Such nutrients are required by plants in comparitively large amount ( $1-10 \mathrm{mg}$ per gram of dry matter). Other examples are Phosphorus (P), Potassium (K), Nitrogen (N), etc. Micronutrients are required by plant in traces, less than 0.1 mg per gram of dry matter. Boron (B), Zinc $(\mathrm{Zn})$ and Mangenese $(\mathrm{Mn})$ are micronutrients.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chlorosis is produced in the leaves due to the deficiency of $\mathrm{Fe}, \mathrm{Mg}, \mathrm{Mn}, \mathrm{S}$ or N of these essential elements, those that are exclusive constituents of chlorophyll molecule are
questions.solution: Magnesium is present in the central position of tetrapyrrole ring of the chlorophyll molecule. Porphyrin is a cyclic tetrapyrrole ring made up of four nitrogen containing pyrrole rings arranged in a cyclic fashion in the chlorophyll molecule.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: X -linked recessive gene is
questions.solution: Human males carry single copy of X-chromosome and hence allow the expression of recessive gene; it needs two copies of a X-Linked recessive gene to be expressed in human females. Hence, X-Linked recessive gene is always expressed in male. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which is essential for root hair growth?
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{l|l}
\hline \text{Element} &\text{Essential for} \\
\hline \text{Zinc} & \text{Auxin synthesis} \\
\text{Calcium} & \text{Root hair growth} \\
\text{Molybdenum} & \text{Nitrogen fixation} \\
\text{Sulphur} & \text{Protein synthesis} \\
\hline
\end{array}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Some functions of nutrient element are given below
I. Important constituent of proteins involved in ETS.
II. Activator of catalase.
III. Important constituent of cytochrome.
IV. Essential for chlorophyll synthesis.
The concerned nutrient is
questions.solution: Iron $(\mathrm{Fe})$ is the concerned nutrient associated with the above given functions.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Schizogony occurs in
questions.solution: Schizogony is stage in asexual reproduction of Plasmodium (malarial parasite) in which schizont is formed. From the human blood sporozoites enter the liver cells. The sporozoite grows in size to become a rounded schizont called cryptozoite. The latter divides to form cryptomerozoites ( $=$ merozoites). The merozoites are asexual haploid forms. The host liver cells rupture to liberate the cryptomerozoites into the liver sinusoids.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not caused by deficiency of mineral ?
questions.solution: Chlorosis occurs due to the lack of \(\mathrm{Fe}, \mathrm{Mg}\). Necrosis occurs due to the lack of N, S, Mo. Shortening of internodes occurs due to lack of K. Whereas, etiolation is the phenomena which occur in plants not due to deficiency of any mineral element but when the plant is grown in partial or in the complete absence of light. It results in weak stems and loss of chlorophyll from the leaves. Thus the correct answer is option B.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The mineral present in cell wall is
questions.solution:
Chemical composition of the cell wall is the following :
(a) Matrix water-60%, hemicellulose$5-15 \%$, pectic substance- $2-8 \%$, Lipids $0.5-3.0 \%$, proteins- $1-2 \%$.
(b) Microfibrils Cellulose/fungus cellulose10-15%.
(c) Other Ingredients lignin, cutin, suberin, silica, minerals (eg, iron, calcium, carbonate) waxes, tannins, resins, gum etc

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Insects captured by insectivorous plants fulfil their requirements of
questions.solution: The insectivorous plants grow in nitrogen deficient soil and their nitrogen requirement is fulfilled by capturing and digesting the insects.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Hydroponics is a :
questions.solution: Hydroponics refers to soil-less cultures of plants and growing them on mineral solution.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Nitrifying bacteria are able to
options: convert atmospheric nitrogen into soluble forms
questions.solution: Most of the ammonia produced in the soil is acted upon by nitrifying bacteria. Ammonia is changed to nitrate. The reaction occurs in two steps nitrite formation (eg, Nitrosomonas) and nitrate formation (eg, Nitrobacter).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The deficiencies of micro-nutrients, not only affects growth of plants but also vital functions such as photosynthetic and mitochondrial electron flow. Among the list given below, which group of three elements shall affect most, both photosynthetic and mitochondrial electron transport?
questions.solution: Micronutrients are minerals obtained from the soil and present in plant tissues at concentrations usually less than $3 \mu$ $\mathrm{mol} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$ dry matter. Cu (copper), Mn (manganese) and Fe (iron) are those micronutrients, which affect both photosynthesis and mitochondrial electron transport because they are the main constituents of various electron carriers.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following element plays an important role in nitrogen fixation?
questions.solution: Molybdenum is a component of nitrogenase and thus brings about fixation of gaseous nitrogen. It also act as enzyme activator of nitrate reductase.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Stomatal opening in plants occurs due to influx of
questions.solution: It has been now experimentally proved that opening of stomata is an energy consuming process and not closing. The turgidity of guard cells is usually controalled by $\mathrm{K}^{+}, \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$and $\mathrm{H}^{+}$. The opening of stomata is initiated by excretion of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$by guard cells, intake of $\mathrm{K}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$and closing is controlled by abscisic acid.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Molybdenum causes
questions.solution: Molybdenum causes mottling (patches on green and non-green areas).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The first stable product of fixation of atmospheric nitrogen in leguminous plant is
questions.solution: The fixation of atmospheric nitrogen to ammonia is given as $\mathrm{N}_2+8 \mathrm{e}^{-}+8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+16 \mathrm{ATP} \xrightarrow[\text { Nitrogenase }]{\mathrm{MO}-\mathrm{Fe}}$ $2 \mathrm{NH}_3+\mathrm{H}_2+16 \mathrm{ADP}+16 \mathrm{Pi}$ The process in which atmospheric nitrogen gets concerted into inorganic nitrogenon (nitrate, ammonia) compounds through microorganisms is called biological nitrogen fixing.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The enzyme responsible for atmospheric nitrogen fixation is :
questions.solution:
Enzyme nitrogenase plays a key role in $\mathrm{N}_2$ fixations and remains active only during anaerobic condition. It is made up of two protein subunits :
(i) non-heme iron protein or Fe-protein or dinitrogen reductase.
(ii) Iron molybdenum protein or Mo-Fe protein or dinitrogenase.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is non symbiotic bio-fertilizer?
questions.solution:
1. Azotobacter is a non-symbiotic, free-living nitrogen-fixing bacteria.
2. They are aerobic and free-living soil microbes, which perform an essential function in the nitrogen cycle.
3. They convert atmospheric nitrogen to ammonia, which is further utilized by plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Micro-nutrients are :
options: less important in nutrition than macronutrients
as important in nutrition as macro-nutrients
may be omitted from culture media without any detrimental effect on the plant
called micro because they play only minor role in nutrition
questions.solution: Micro-nutrients are the minerals/elements which are required in less amount by the plants and thus are present in less quantity in the plants but these are as important as the macro-nutrients. Examples of micro-nutrients iron ( Fe ), manganese ( Mn ), copper $(\mathrm{Cu})$, zinc $(\mathrm{Zn})$, molybdenum (Mo), boron (B) and chlorine (Cl).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A plant require magnesium $(\mathrm{Mg})$ for
questions.solution: Magnesium is an important constituent of chlorophyll molecule.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Study the following statements and select the option with correct statements.
I. Pulvinus leaf base is present in some leguminous plants.
II. In Eichhornia the petioles expand, becomes green and synthesise food.
III. Opposite phyllotaxy is seen in guava.
questions.solution:
Most of the leaves have a swollen leaf base known as pulvinus. Its attachment on the stem is not strong. In guava opposite phyllotaxy is found. In this case all the pairs of leaves of a branch arise in same plane, so that two vertical rows are formed.
In Australian Acacia, petiole gets modified into leaf like structure and synthesise food. In Eichhornia, petiole becomes spongy and helps in floating.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Perianth is found in which family?
questions.solution: Perianth is found in Liliaceae family when there is no distinction of sepals and petals. The individual parts of the perianth are known as tepals, e.g. onion, lily, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In which one of the family formation of endosperm doesn't take place?
questions.solution: No endosperm formation takes place in the members of Orchidaceae, Podostemonaceae and Trapaceae families.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Match the following columns
questions.solution:
\begin{array}{c|c}
\hline Column I & Column II \\
\hline Opuntia & Stem thorns \\
Asparagus & Phylloclades \\
Citrus & Cladodes \\
\hline
\end{array}

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Verticillaster inflorescence is found in
questions.solution:
Verticillaster inflorescence is the characteristic feature of tulsi (Ocimum) belonging to family-Lamiaceac or Labiatae. Here, flowers are arranged in two opposite cymose groups on each node.
In mustard, raceme and in sunflower capitulum or head type of racemose inflorescence is found. China rose has solitary axillary flower.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Lady finger belongs to family
questions.solution: Lady finger (Abelmoschus esculents) belongs to family-Malvacae. This family is distinguished by presence of epicalyx, staminal tube, extrose anthers, monothecous, and syncarpous ovary.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match the following with correct combination.
$\begin{array}{ll|ll}
\hline & \text{Column I} & & \text{Column II} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Cuscuta} & \text{1.} & \text{Saprophyte} \\
\text{B.} & \text{Eichhornia} & \text{2.} & \text{Pneumatophare} \\
\text{C.} & \text{Monotropa} & \text{3.} & \text{Insectivorous plant} \\
\text{D.} & \text{Rhizophora} & \text{4.} & \text{Parasite} \\
\text{E.} & \text{Utricularia} & \text{5.} & \text{Root pocket} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{ll|ll}
\hline & \text{Column I} & & \text{Column II} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Cuscuta} & \text{4.} & \text{Parasite} \\
\text{B.} & \text{Eichhornia} & \text{5.} & \text{Root pocket} \\
\text{C.} & \text{Monotropa} & \text{1.} & \text{Saprophyte} \\
\text{D.} & \text{Rhizophora} & \text{2.} & \text{Pneumatophore} \\
\text{E.} & \text{Utricularia} & \text{3.} & \text{Insectivorous plant} \\
\hline
