questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The fleshy receptacle of syconus of fig encloses a number of
questions.solution:
Syconus is a composite fruit develops from hypanthodium inflorescence, e.g., Ficus carica, Ficus benghalensis. The flask-shaped receptacle encloses female flowers that give rise to achene-like fruitlets. This fruit possesses a small pore protected by scaly leaves. The receptacle that becomes fleshy is edible.
Samara is a single seeded, dry indehiscent fruit. Its pericarp becomes membranous and flat-like wings that help in dispersal, e.g. , Ulmus, Holoptelia indica.
A berry is a pulpy-indehiscent, few to multiseeded fruit derived from multicarpellary syncarpous gynoecium. The fleshy pericarp of berry consists of three parts-epicarp, mesocarp and endocarp.
Schizocarpic fruits are simple, dry, multiseeded fruits, which breakup into single seeded parts. The single seeded parts, which further do not dehisce are called mericarps.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Catkin inflorescence is found in
questions.solution: In catkin inflorescence the flowers are sessile and unisexual with pendulous axis, e.g., Morus (mulberry).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Epigynous flowers are present in
questions.solution: In epigynous type of flowers the thalamus enclosed the ovary entirely and fuses. The sepals, petals and stamens arise from the top of ovary. Such flowers known as epigynous and ovary as inferior, e.g., Oenothera, Cucurbita.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Dianthus, placentation is
questions.solution: In free central placentation the syncarpous gynoecium is unilocular and the placentae are borne on a central column formed by extension of base of the gynoecium where the carpels fuse or the suppression of septa. e.g., Stellaria, Dianthus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The term 'keel' is used for special type of
questions.solution: Keel is a special type of petal found in papilionaceous type of corolla. Papilionaceous type is butterfly like, the corolla has one large posterior standard, two lateral wings, two innermost and smaller keels.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Coffee and quinine are obtained from the
questions.solution: Coffea arabica (Rubiaceae) yields the well known beverage coffee. Cinchona sp. (Rubiaceae) yields the quinine which is used in treatment of malaria.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Edible part in 'sorosis', a composite fruit, is
questions.solution: Composite fruits develop from entire inflorescence, eg, syconus develops from hypanthodium, eg, banyan. Sorosis is the composite fruit develops from spike and catkin, eg, in pineapple. Sorosis fruit develops from catkin; and edible part of pineapple fruits is peduncle and perianth. Fleshy thalamus is an edible part of pear, apple fruit, ie, pome fruits. Cotyledons are edible part of cashewnut, ie, nut fruit. Endosperm is an edible part of caryopsis of wheat, rice, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is a true nut?
questions.solution: Nut is a fruit develops from polycarpellary, superior ovary. The pericarp is hard, woody or leathery, eg, edible part of chestnut is cotyledons. Ground nut is lomentum in which edible part is seed. Walnut is a drupe fruit in which edible part is cotyledon. Pistachio is a drupe in which edible part is seed.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Botanical name of 'chana' is
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{l|l|l}
\hline \text{Botanical Name} & \begin{array}{l}\text{Common Name}
\end{array} & \text{Family} \\
\hline \text{Cicer arietnum} & \text{Gram} & \text{Papilionaceae} \\
\text{Phaseolus aureus} & \text{Green gram} & \text{Papilionaceae} \\
\text{Dolichos lablab} & \text{Sem} & \text{Papilionaceae} \\
\hline
\end{array}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The edible dry fruit 'chilgoza' is
questions.solution: Edible dry fruit which is known as chilgoza is seed of Pinus gerardiana. Gymnosperms are plants in of which ovary is absent so, there is production of naked seeds, hence fruits are absent in gymnosperms. Seeds of Cycas are not edible.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Lady finger belongs to family
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}
\hline \text{Member} & \text{Botanical Name} & \text{Family} \\
\hline \text{Lady finger} & \text{Abelmoschus esculentus} & \text{Malvaceae} \\
\hline \text{Cucumber} & \text{Cucumis sativus} & \text{Cucurbitaceae} \\
\hline \text{Onion} & \text{Allium cepa} & \text{Liliaceae} \\
\hline
\end{array}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pappus is modified
questions.solution: The fruits of Asteraceae often possess pappus (which is a modification of persistent hairy calyx) which functions as a parachute for the dispersal of, eg, Sonchus, Tagstes. This dispersal mechanism is known as parachute mechanism. Censer mechanism for seed dispersal is found in poppy.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Trimerous flower, superior ovary and axile placentation is characteristics of
questions.solution: Monocot is characterised by trimerous flowers, fibrous roots, parallel venation of leaves, eg, Family-Liliaceae. Trimerous flower, superior ovary and axile placentation is characteristics of familyLiliaceae, eg, onion. Cucurbitaceae, Solanaceae and Compositae are dicot families characterised by tetra or pentamerous flowers, tap roots and reticulated venation of leaves.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Clinging roots are found in
questions.solution: Clinging roots are found is orchids, these roots grow as epiphytes upon the trunks or branches of trees. They hang freely in the air and absorb atmospheric moisture with the help of special sponge like tissue called velamen. Velamen is the modification of epidermis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Caryopsis is present in
questions.solution: Caryopsis is present in wheat. It is a type of simple dry fruit. It is also called as grain. In such fruits the ovary wall and seed coat fuse making it difficult to separate the two.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pappus helps in dispersal of pollen in
questions.solution: Pappus are modified bracts of Asteraceae family. These hairy structures help in dispersal of pollen by wind.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Expanded green stem of Opuntia is called
questions.solution: Phylloclade is a modified stem or branch which may be flat or circular, fleshy, like green leaf, eg, Opuntia.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Three floral diagrams are given here. Their respective families are assigned in the answer key. Find out the families to which these diagrams belong to
questions.solution:
'Pappus', a hairy structure is a modification of calyx and is the characteristic feature of family-Asteraceae. So,
of family-Asteraceae. Calyx 5, gamosepalous, persistant, corolla 5, gamopetalous. Androecium 5, polyandrous and gynoecium bicarpellary and syncarpous are the diagnositc feature of family-Solanaceae, shown by floral formula Calyx 4, polysepalous and arranged in 2 whorld of 2 each, corolla 4, polypetalous and cruciform. Androecium 6, polyandrous, tetradynamous. Gynoecium, bicarpellary, syncarpous and superior, placentation parietial with a partition calledreplum. These are the diagnostic feature of family-Brassicaceae or Cruciferae, shown by floral formula

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Most of the economically important fibre yielding plants belong to family
questions.solution: The fibre yielding plants fulfil the second requirement of human beings, ie, clothing. Such plants are known to mankind since time immemorial. The flax was cultivated in Europe even in stone age. Botanically fibres are sclerenchymatous occurring in different parts of the plant. The longest fibre of Boehmeria nivea measures 55 cm in length. Most of the economically important fibre yielding plants belong to family-Malvaceae (eg, Gossypium, Hibiscus, Cannabinus, Abutilon theophrasti, Abelmoschus esculentus, Hibiscus subdariffa, Urena lobata etc).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Mitotic spindle have main protein
questions.solution: Microtubules are unbranched, hollow, submicroscopic tubules, which form the structural material of cilia, flagella, basal bodies, centrioles and spindle apparatus. Microtubules are built up of protofilaments formed of two related proteins, $\alpha$ and $\beta$-tubulins.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ginger is an underground stem. It is distinguished from root because
questions.solution: Underground stems can be differentiated from roots by (i) Absence of root cap (ii) Absence of root hair (iii) Presence of terminal bud (iv) Presence of nodes and internodes (v) Occurrence of foliage or scale leaves on the nodes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Ruscus, the stem is a
questions.solution: When the stem becomes green, flattened or fleshy and carry out photosynthesis, this is called phylloclade. The short, green, cylindrical or flattened phylloclade with one or two internode is called clodode, e.g., Ruscus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Floral formula of is characteristic of the family :
questions.solution: The member of sub-family Caesal pinieae have bracteate, zygomorphic, bisexual, hypogynous, pentamerous flowers with 5 -sepals (polysepalous) 5-petals (polypeptalous), $5+5$ stamens (polyandrous) and monocarpellary, superior ovary with marginal placentation.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The number of female flowers found in a cyanthium is :
questions.solution: Cyanthium is a special type of inflorescence (found in Euphorbia sp.) with a cup shaped involucre having nector secreting glands, a centrally placed single large female flower which is reduced to pistil surrounded by many male flowers present in the form of stamens.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The plant that is characteristics component of mangrove vegetation :
questions.solution: Rhizophora mangel is the component of mangrove vegetation and grows in marshes or nitrogen and air deficient soil. These plants possess vivipary and pneumatophores or respiratory roots.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The given formula belong to
questions.solution: The family Malvaceae includes 75 genera and 1000 species they are chiefly distributed in tropical and subtropical region of the world. The floral fomula is of malva plant.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is used as green manure?
questions.solution: Crotolaria juncea, common name sunn hemp. A quick growing crop, is cultivated and ploughed under, to incorporate it into the soil. A green manure crop supplies organic matter and additional nitrogen and also exercises a protective action against erosion and leaching.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The modified stem of Opuntia is
questions.solution: When the stem is flattened and functions as leaf is called phylloclade or cladophyll.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The inflorescence in Ocimum is :
questions.solution: Verticillaster consists of biparous cymes ending in uniparous scorpioid cymes on either side e.g. Ocimum and several members of family Labiatae.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cellulose fibre is obtained from Gossypium _______.
questions.solution: Cotton is the seed surface fibre of Gossypium. Its processing involves ginning, bailing, picking, lapping, carding and twisting. It is used in textile industry.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The ability of the venus flytrap to capture insects is due to
options: a passive process requiring no special ability on the part of the plant
questions.solution: Insectivorous plants grow in water logged swampy soils, deficient in nitrogenous compounds. These plants obtain their nitrogen compounds from the insects. Due to this reason, these plants are called as semi-autotrophic or semi-heterotrophic. Dionea (an insectivorous plant) is commonly called as venus fly trap. Leaves of this plant have winged petiole and lamina modified into two toothed jaws. Each jaw bears several teeth. Upper surface of each jaw bears triangularly arranged long sensitive hairs or bristles and many glands, which are irregularly scattered. These hairs are very sensitive to touch stimulus. When an insect touches these hairs, this stimulus causes rapid turgor pressure changes which closes the leaf.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following represents the edible part of the fruit of litchi?
questions.solution: The edible part of the fruit of litchi is juicy aril. Aril is an accessory seed covering formed by an outgrowth at the base of the ovule.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In turnip, $2 / 3$ part of swollen area is derived from :
questions.solution: In turnip, napiform root is found, the swollen part of which is derived from the hypocotyl.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Dark reaction
questions.solution: In dark reaction, the products formed from light reaction are required. The dark reaction occurs outside of the thylakoids during day in CAM plants and during night in $\mathrm{C}_3$-plants. In this reaction, the energy from ATP and NADPH are used to fix carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of these is incorrect for $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants?
questions.solution: Among the given options, option (4) is incorrect about $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants. The mesophyll cells of $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants do not possess RuBisCO enzyme. $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants show Kranz anatomy. In these plants, the initial fixation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ occurs in mesophyll cells. The primary acceptor of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is Phosphoenol Pyruvate or PEP. It combines with $\mathrm{CO}_2$ in the presence of PEP carboxylase or PEPCase to form oxaloacetic acid or oxaloacetate.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A plant is provided with ideal conditions for photosynthesis and supplied with isotope 14 $\mathrm{CO}_2$. When the products of the process are analysed carefully, what would be the nature of products?
options: Oxygen is labelled, but glucose is normal
Glucose is labelled, but oxygen is normal
questions.solution:
In the process of photosynthesis
$6 \mathrm{CO}_2+12 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{Chl}]{\mathrm{hv}}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_6+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+6 \mathrm{O}_2 \uparrow \\ & \text { (Glucose) }\end{aligned}$
Plants manufacture glucose from $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ and $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and oxygen gas is released. So, due to isotopic carbon of $\mathrm{CO}_2$, only glucose will be labelled, but oxygen will be normal.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Herbicide that blocks electron transport from PS-II to PS-I by inhibiting electron flow between plastoquinone $\rightarrow$ cytochrome is
questions.solution:
DCMU is selective herbicide that acts as a potent inhibitor of PS-II. Paraquat inhibits PS-I by producing super oxides.
DCPIP is a dye used to measure rate of photosynthesis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Which of the following characteristics out of I, II and III are exhibited by $\mathrm{C}_4$ plants?
I. Kranz anatomy.
II. The product of photosynthesis is oxalo acetic acid.
III. Both PEP carboxylase and ribulose-bis phosphate carboxylase act as carboxylating enzyme.
Choose the corrent option.
options: I and II, but not III
II and III, but not I
I and III, but not II
questions.solution: $\mathrm{C}_4$ plants have an alternative $\mathrm{CO}_2$ fixation pathways called Hatch and Slack cycle where OAA, a 4C compound is the first stable product. These plants have Kranz anatomy in leaf.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Molecular formula of chlorophyll-b is
questions.solution: The molecular formula of chlorophyll-b is $\mathrm{C}_{55} \mathrm{H}_{70} \mathrm{O}_6 \mathrm{~N}_4 \mathrm{Mg}$. It helps in photosynthesis by absorbing light energy. It is more soluble in polar solvents than chlorophyll-a because of its carbonyl group. It is of yellow colour and primarily absorbs blue light.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Conditions required for cyclic photophosphorylation are
questions.solution:
The conditions required for cyclic photophosphorylation are aerobic condition and optimum light intensity.
It is a process of photophosphorylation in which an electron expelled by the excited photocentre is returned. This electron passes through a series of electron carriers. Cyclic photophosphorylation is performed by photosystem-I only.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Kranz anatomy is found in
questions.solution:
Kranz anatomy is found in $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants.It refers
to the presence of two types of chloroplasts agranal in bundle sheath cells and grand in mesophyll cells ,e.g. sugarcane, maize, etc. In these plants, the first stable product after the fixation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is 4-c, dicarboxylic acid called Oxaloacetic Acid (OAA).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Which of the following characteristics is are exhibited by $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants?
I. Kranz anatomy.
II. The first product of photosynthesis is oxaloacetic acid.
III. Both PEP carboxylates and ribulose biphosphate carboxylate act as carboxylating enzymes.
The correct option is
options: I and III, but not II
I and II, but not III
II and III, but not I
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{C}_4$-plants have an alternative $\mathrm{CO}_2$ fixation pathway called Hatch and Slack cycle. These plants have Kranz anatomy in leaf, where vascular bundles are surrounded by bundle sheath.
In $\mathrm{C}_4$ cycle light reaction occurs in, mesophyll chloroplast by PEP carboxylaes and the assimilation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ takes place in, i.e., dark phase in bundle sheath chloroplast by Rubisco.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Quantasome are present in
questions.solution: Quantasomes are the photosynthetic units present in the thylakoids of chloroplast. Each of the quantasomes contain about 250-300 chlorophyll molecules.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following cell organelles is associated with photorespiration?
questions.solution: Photorespiration ( $\mathrm{C}_2$-cycle) is a special type of respiration shown by many green plants ( $\mathrm{C}_3$-plants) when they are exposed to light. It is a light dependent process during, which $\mathrm{O}_2$ is used and $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is released. The process of photorespiration takes place only in chlorophyllous tissues of plants. Therefore, the main site for photorespiration is chloroplast. But mitochondria and peroxysomes are also required to complete the process.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Aqueous and vitreous humour are divided by
questions.solution: Lens is a transparent, biconvex, elastic structure that bends light waves as they pass through its surfaces. The lens separates the aqueous and vitreous humours.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Kranz anatomy can be obseved in leaves of
questions.solution: Kranz anatomy is found in $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants, e.g., Sorghum, Zea mays, Saccharum officinarum. It is characterised by connective, undifferentiated mesophyll around vascular bundles with chloroplast containing bundle sheath.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In leaves of $\mathrm{C}_4$ plants malic acid synthesis during $\mathrm{CO}_2$ fixation occurs in
questions.solution: In $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants $\mathrm{CO}_2$ combines with phosphoenol pyruvic acid to form oxaloacetic acid in mesophyll chloroplasts. It is further reduced to malic acid which is transferred to chloroplast in bundle sheath cells.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Light reaction in stroma lamellae of the chloroplast results in the formation of
questions.solution: Photosystem-I is located in non-appressed part of grana and stroma lamellae. It is involved both in cyclic and non-cyclic photophosphorylation. Interrelationship of PS-I and PS-II produces ATP and $\mathrm{NADPH}_2$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Photosynthesis in $\mathrm{C}_4$ plants is relatively less limited by atmospheric $\mathrm{CO}_2$ levels because
options: four carbon acids are the primary initial $\mathrm{CO}_2$ fixation products
the primary fixation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is mediated via PEP carboxylase
effective pumping of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ into bundle sheath cells
RUBISCO in $\mathrm{CO}_4$ plants has higher affinity for $\mathrm{CO}_2$
questions.solution:
The fixation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ in $\mathrm{C}_4$ plants takes place in two places and by two different organic compounds. Phosphoenol pyruvate (PEP) is found in mesophyll cells which primarily fixes atmospheric $\mathrm{CO}_2$ into oxaloacetic acid (4C).
RUBISCO is present in bundle sheath cells where final fixation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ in hexose sugars takes place. $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is primarily fixed by PEP carboxylase because this enzyme has greater affinity to $\mathrm{CO}_2$ than RUBISCO.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In CAM plants, $\mathrm{CO}_2$ acceptor is
questions.solution: The process of photosynthesis in $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants and CAM (Crassulacean Acid Metabolism) is same. But instead of spatial separation of initial PEP case fixation and final Rubisco fixation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$, the two steps occur in same cell but at different times, night and day, eg, Opuntia, pineapple, Vanilla. PEP (Phosphoenol Pyruvate) is the first acceptor of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ in CAM plants like $\mathrm{C}_4$-plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chemiosmotic theory of ATP synthesis in the chloroplasts and mitochondria is based on
questions.solution: Chemiosmotic hypothesis for oxidative phosphorylation (ATP synthesis) was proposed by Peter Mitchell in 1961 for this he was awarded Noble Prize in 1978. This theory is based on proton gradient.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Photosynthetic Active Radiation (PAR) has the following range of wavelengths
questions.solution: The cosmic rays, X-rays, UV-rays, the visible spectrum, infra red rays, radio waves, coming from sun are constituted electromagnetic spectrum. The waves of each of these types have a characteristic range of wavelengths. The visible spectrum, which is also known as Photosynthetic Active Radiation (PAR), has a range of wavelength of 380 nm to 760 nm . This part of electromagnetic spectrum is responsible for photosynthesis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The law of limiting factors for photosynthesis was enunciated by :
questions.solution: Blackman (1905) proposed the law of limiting factor according to which, "when a biological process is controlled as to its rapidity by a number of separate factors, the rate of the process is limited by the pace of the slowest factor.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The light phase of photosynthesis is called :
questions.solution: Hill reaction, also called as light reaction is a photochemical reaction. In this, reduced enzymes and phosphate bond energy (ATP) are produced.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The plant Cuscuta shows maximum photosynthesis in :
questions.solution: Cuscuta is a parasitic plant which gets prepared food from other plants through the haustoria. At this plant lacks chlorophyll, it is unable to perform photosynthesis or to make its own food itself.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In photosythesis action and absorption spectrum were related by
questions.solution: Engelmann studied the effect of different region of the visible spectrum on the rate of photosynthesis of different algae. The amount of oxygen $\left(\mathrm{O}_2\right)$ released was found to be maximum in blue and red absorption bands of chlorophyll.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Energy transfer in photosynthesis occurs as
questions.solution: Photosynthetic pigments mainly occur on thylakoid membranes. These are major pigments like chlorophylls and accessory pigments like phycoerythrin, phycocyanin, and carotenoids. The accessory pigments absorb light energy of various wavelength and get excited and transfer the energy to chlorophyll-a molecule through electron spin resonance. Order of energy transfer in photosynthesis occur as phycocyanin followed by phycoerythrin followed by carotenoids and finally to chlorophylls.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Chl -b differs from chl -a by
questions.solution: Chlorophyll-b structrally differs from chlorophyll-a by the presence of carbonyl (CHO) group at 3rd carbon in the prophyrin ring. chlorophyll-a possess methyl $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)$ group in this place.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In photosynthesis what occurs in PS-II?
options: It takes longer wavelength of light and $e^{-}$ from $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
It takes shorter wavelength of light and $e^{-}$from $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
It takes longer wavelength of light and $e^{-}$ from NADP
It takes shorter wavelength of light and $e^{-}$from NADP
questions.solution: Green plants and algae use two type of photo-system called PS-I and PS -II. In PS-I chlorophyll absorb maximum at 700 nm wavelength of light thus reaction centre is $\mathrm{P}_{700}$ and PS-II absorb maximum 680 nm wavelength of light thus reaction centre is $\mathrm{P}_{680} . \mathrm{P}_{680}$ (PS-II) extracts an electron from water, returning to its unexcited state because hydrolysis of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ occurs in PS-II. So, $\mathrm{O}_2$ evolved in PS-II come from water.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Fixation of one $\mathrm{CO}_2$ molecule through Calvin cycle requires.
questions.solution: 2 ATP are required during conversion of PGA to 1, 3 diphosphoglyceric acid and 1 ATP during conversioin of glyceraldehyde phosphate to ribulose biphosphate. $2 \mathrm{NADPH}_2$ molecules are utilised for converting 1, 3 diphosphoglyceric acid to glyceraldehyde phosphate.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In photosynthesis carbon dioxide is converted to carbohydrates. It is a _______ process.
questions.solution: Photosynthesis is an oxidation reduction process in which water is oxidised and carbon dioxide is reduced to carbohydrates. Hence, it is anabolic and endergonic process.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Hatch and Slack pathway
options: occurs in Kranz anatomy where mesophyll have small chloroplast whereas bundle sheath have granal chloroplast
occurs in Kranz anatomy when mesophyll have small chloroplast where as bundle sheath have larger, agranal chloroplast
Kranz anatomy where mesophyll cell are diffused
questions.solution: $\mathrm{C}_4$ plants have "Kranz type" anatomy. Vascular bundles are surrounded by bundle sheath cells. The chloroplasts are dimorphic. The cells of bundle sheath have very large chloroplasts. They lack grana and contain starch grains. They are centripetally arranged and mesophyll cells have small and agranal chloroplast.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Hatch and Slack type plants $\mathrm{CO}_2$ acceptor is
questions.solution: In Hatch and Slack cycle $\mathrm{CO}_2$ acceptor is phosphoenol pyruvic acid and first product is oxaloacetic acid, (a $4-\mathrm{C}$ compound). Therefore it is popularly termed as $\mathrm{C}_4$ cycle.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In CAM plants
options: stomata open in day and close at night
stomata remain open in day and night both
stomata remain closed in day and night both
stomata open at night and close in day
questions.solution: CAM is found in members of the family Crassulaceae (e.g., Kalanchoe), family Cactaceae (e.g., Opuntia, Cereus), and some members of Liliaceae, Orchidaceae, Bromeliaceae, Euphorbia sp. All CAM plants are not succulent and stomata open at night.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In sea anemone, the symmetry is
questions.solution:
In radial symmetry, body can be divided into similar halves by more than two planes passing through the main axis.
Cnidarians or coelenterates (like Hydra, sea anemone, Aurelia, Obelia) are radially symmetrical animals with cell-tissue grade of body organization.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: DCMU
questions.solution: DCMU (Dichlorophenyl Dimethyl Urea) is a herbicide, which inhibits $\mathrm{O}_2$ evolution and non-cyclic photophosphorylation, $\mathrm{O}_2$ evolution and non-cyclic photophosphorylation both involve in PS-II.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following elements is activator for both ribulose bisphosphate, carboxylase oxygenase and phosphoenol pyruvate carboxylase in photosynthetic carbon fixation?
questions.solution: In photosynthetic carbon fixation magnesium ion $\left(\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}\right)$ is an activator for both ribulose bisphosphate carboxylase oxygenase and phosphoenol pyruvate carboxylase enzymes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Hormone inducing fruit ripening is
questions.solution: Ethylene is a natural gaseous hormone, which is predominantly known for inducing fruit ripening.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following pairs, is not correctly matched?
questions.solution: Gibberellin helps in cell growth of stem, leaves and other aerial parts.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following properties is shown by cytokinins?
questions.solution: Cytokinin is a plant growth hormone, which is mostly synthesised in the roots, cytokinin delays leaf senescence.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Vernalisation is
questions.solution:
Vernalisation is low temperature treatment.
It is the process of shortening of vegetative phase and initiation of reproductive phase by application of low temperature $\left(0-4^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$ to moistened seeds and young plants.
It is used for a number of temperate plants like winter wheat, winter rye, cabbage, henbane, Chrysanthemum and even certain perennials, e.g. apples.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Compare the statements I and II and choose correct option.
Statement (I) Auxin promote apical dominance by suppressing the activity of lateral buds.
Statement (II) In moriculture, periodic prunning of shoot tips is done to make mulberry plants bushy.
Choose the correct options.
options: I is false, but II is true
II is false, but I is true
Both I and II are true
Both I and II are false
questions.solution: Periodic prunning of lateral buds always make a plant bushy.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Compare the statement I and II and choose the correct option.
Statement I In the flowering plants due to higher accumulation of auxins dormancy of lateral buds occurs.
Statement II In Maryland Mammoth (a tobacco variety) flowering occurred at different time at different latitude due to gibberellin concentration.
options: Statement I is true, but II is false
Statement I is false, but II is true
questions.solution:
Auxin is responsible for apical dominance in apical buds, which inhibits the lateral buds to grow.
Plants require a certain day length to flower. This relative length of day and night needed for flowering is called as photoperiod. The effect of photoperiod or daily duration of light hours and dark periods on the growth and development of plants, especially flowering, is called photoperiodism.
This phenomenon was first discovered by Garner and Allard (1920, 1922) on Maryland Mammoth variety of tobacco. They observed that the Maryland Mammoth could be made to flower only by reducing the light hours with artificial darkening. It could be made to remain vegetative in winter by providing extra light.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following generally acts as an antagonist to gibberellins?
questions.solution: Gibberellins and Abscisic Acid (ABA) are antagonistic to each other. Gibberllins promotes seed germination whereas $A B A$ promotes seed dormacy.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not a plant growth inhibitor?
questions.solution: IAA is a plant growth promoter while dormin, abscisic acid and ethylene are plant growth inhibitors. IAA (indole 3-Acetic Acid) is an auxin. It was discovered by Koge et al. (1934). Auxins are synthesised in shoot apices, leaf primordia and developing seeds from amino acid, tryptophan.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ethylene is related with
questions.solution: Ethylene is connected with climecteric phenomenon in which there is 1000 fold increase in the production of basal ethylene level during ripening of climecteric fruits.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The bioassay of auxin is
questions.solution: A bioassay is a measurement of the effect of a known or suspected biologically active substance on living material. Went used Avena sativa (oat) coleoptiles in a technique called the Avena coleoptile curvature test for auxin.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Genetic dwarfism can be overcome by
questions.solution: Genetic dwarfism is overcome by the usage of gibberellins. Ethylene is a gaseous hormone, which induces ripening in unripe fruits. ABA cause ageing and abscission of leaves.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not an auxin?
questions.solution: Zeatin is a cytokinin. IAA or indole acetic acid is a natural auxin. IBA or indole butyric acid and NAA or nepthalene acetic acid are synthetic auxin.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Induction of flowering by low temperature treatment is
questions.solution: Vernalization is chilling or cold treatment of the young plants or seeds to induce flowering. It is a process of shortening of the juvenile or vegetative phase and fastening the flowering by a process of cold treatment. It was first reported by a Russion worker, Lysenko (1928) while working on cold requiring biennial plants. Common examples of plants requiring vernalization are winter rye, winter wheat, winter barley, pea, Chrysanthemum etc.Cryobiology is the study of life at low temperature, usually at liquid nitrogen (ie $-196^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ) and preservation of organisms in liquid by chemical methods.Photoperiodism is the effect of photoperiods or daily duration of light hours (and dark periods) on the growth and development mainly flowering in plants. It was first studied by 'Garner and Allard' (1920) on 'Maryland Mammoth' variety of tobacco.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The bioassay of auxin is
questions.solution: Bioassay is the testing of biological activity of a substance by employing living material. Went used Avena sativa (oat) coleoptiles in a technique called Avena coleoptile curvature test. ABA or abscisic acid induces dormancy in buds, seeds and underground storage organs. Unorganised and undifferentiated mass of cells forms during culture of cells known as callus. In callus culture, shoot and root regenerations are controlled, generally by auxin and cytokinin balance.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Solarisation is
questions.solution: Photosynthesis starts at quite low intensity of light and very high intensity causes oxidation and destruction of pigments, enzymes and intermediates. This destruction known as solarisation. So, solarisation causes reduction in photosynthetic yields. Photoperiodism is the effect of duration of sunlight on plants for many processes like flowering.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Solenocytes are associated with
questions.solution: Solenocytes or flame cells are the excretory organs of phylum-Platyhelminthes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Compound squamous epithelium is found in
questions.solution: In the structure of compound squamous epithelium, several layers of cells, deep layers are cuboidal to columnar, surface layers flat and scale-like. This epithelium founds in mouth,oesophagus, part of epiglottis (pharynx) and vagina. The main function of this epithelium is protection.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Phytochrome is present in
questions.solution: Phytochrome is chromoprotein (photosensitive, photoreceptor, blue pigment protein complex. Found in almost all flowering plants (angiosperms).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Ageing is retarted by
questions.solution: Richmond and Lang 1967 observed that degradation of protein and chlorophyll was delayed in the detached leaves of Xanthium, if there was cytokinin in the medium.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The form of pigment which inhibits germination is
questions.solution:
The seed germination is promoted by red wavelength (650 nm) and inhibited by far red $(730 \mathrm{~nm}$), this is due to the pigment phytochrome behaving in the following manner.
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Red } \lambda \longrightarrow \text { No germination } \\ & \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Auxin in plant means for
questions.solution: Auxins induce cell elongation. IAA is true auxin hormone. Auxins are generally acidic in nature.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is a day neutral plant?
questions.solution: Day neutral plants do not need a specific photoperiod to produce flowers. They are also called intermediates or photoneutrals. Their photoperiod varies from a few hours to 24 hours of uninterrupted light e.g. tomato, cucumber, sunflower, Helianthus annuus, maize and cotton etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The dormancy of seed is regulated by
questions.solution: Dormancy is the condition of a seed when it fails to germinate even when the environmental conditions usually considered necessary for germination are available. It is regulated by ABA (Absicsic acid)

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is an antigibberellin?
questions.solution: The ABA inhibits gibberellin induced growth activities on account of this antagonistic behaviour, it is also called antigibberellin.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Mesozoic era is Golden period of
questions.solution: The Mesozoic era is known as "Golden age of reptiles".

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following theory gives the latest explanation for the closure of stomata?
questions.solution:
As per 'Active $\mathrm{K}^{+}$transport mechanism' accumulation of $\mathrm{K}^{+}$ions occurs in the guard cell during the day in response to light. It increases the turgidity of guard cells, consequently stomata open.
During night, ions move out of the guard cells into surrounding epidermal cells consequently guard cells become flaccid and stomata close.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following is a natural growth inhibitor ?
questions.solution: ABA (Abscisic Acid) is a naturally occurring growth inhibitor in plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Gibberellin was first extracted from :
questions.solution: Yabuta and Hayashi (1939) isolated an active substance from a fungus, Gibberella fujikuroi and called it gibberellin-A. It causes backane disease in rice seedlings.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following plant is LDP ?
questions.solution: Long Day Plants (LDP) require a photoperiod of more than a critical length, which may vary from 4 to 18 hours. The best flowering of long day plants usually occurs in continuous light. For flowering they require either no dark period or a very short dark period. Example of long day plants are wheat, maize, oats, spinach etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Apical dominance is caused by
questions.solution: In many plants, as the apical bud grows, the growth of lower axillary buds is suppressed. Removal of apical bud results in the growth of lower buds. The auxin (IAA) of the terminal bud inhibits the growth of lateral buds. This phenomenon is known as apical dominance.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one is the sweetest sugar ?
questions.solution: Fructose is the sweetest sugar. It is found in sweet fruits and in honey.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Zooidogamy is found in
questions.solution: Zooidogamy is found in Cycas. It is a type of plant reproduction in which male gametes, called antherozoids, reach the female gametes, called archegonium, by swimming in a path of water. It is also found in algae, bryophytes, pteridophytes and some gymnosperms.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Match the storage products listed under column I with the organism given under column II, choose the appropriate option from the given options
questions.solution: The correct matching are

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one is a parasitic alga?
questions.solution: Cephaleuros is a parasitic thalloid green alga. Its common name is red rust. This alga is parasitic on some important economic plants of the tropics and subtropics, e.g. tea, guava, mango, coffee, etc. It damages the area of algal growth on leaves or kills the new shoots or disfigure fruit.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Palmella stage is present in
questions.solution: Palmella stage is present in Chlamydomonas. In adverse conditions, the daughter protoplasts formed by division, do not develop neuromotor apparatus and become motile. However, the parent cell wall gelatinises and forms a matrix around the daughter protoplasts. All cells of palmella stage develop flagella and become motile. They escape from the gelatinous matrix on the arrival of favourable conditions.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Heterotrichous thallus is shown by which organism?
questions.solution: The heterotrichous thallus is shown by Ectocarpus. It is a filamentous marine brown alga which has both upright and prostrate regions. Such a growth is called heterotrichous.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Eyespot is seen in
questions.solution: Eyespot is seen in Chlamydomonas. It is a microscopic, eukaryotic, unicellular, pyriform, biflagellate alga. The alga possesses a cup-shaped chloroplast with a red eyespot or stigma and a pyrenoid for storing starch.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Identify the wrong combination
questions.solution:
Marchantia is a bryophyte belonging to the class-Hepaticopsida, the class of liverworts.
They have elasters which are hygroscopic structures that develops inside the capsule along with spores. Elasters help in dehiscence of capsule and liberation of spores.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The plant whose seeds are known to leave the longest viability period is
questions.solution: Viability is the ability of seed to possess power of germination over a period of time Nelumbo nucifera seed have maximum viability of seed of about 1000 yrs.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Read the following statements and choose the correct option.
I. Leptosporangiate development of sporangium is found in all members Pteropsida.
II. Seed habit is shown by Sellaginella.
III. Gnetum leaves are monomorphic and pinnately compound.
IV. Sporic meiosis is found in Volvox, Chlamydomonas and Ulothrix.
Choose the correct option.
questions.solution:
I, II and IV are correct statements.
Pinus leaves are polymorphic and pinnately compound. In Gnetum, leaves are evergreen, opposite 8-20 cm long and 3-10 cm broad, enter at maturity glossy dark.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cycas revoluta is popularly known as
questions.solution: A starch called sago is obtained from the pith of Cycas revoluta. That is why it is called sago palm.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following pairs of plants are not seed producers?
questions.solution: Seed producing plants belong to spermatophyta. It includes gymnosperms and angiosperms. Seed orginated in gymnosperms. Fern and Funaria belong to pteridophytes and bryophytes respectively. So, they do not reproduce by producing seeds.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: First vascular plant is
questions.solution: Pteridophyte are called vascular cryptogams. They are seedless vascular plant as they possess water conducting xylem and food translocating phloem but do not produce seed (instead spare formation takes place).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Agar-agar is produced by
questions.solution:
Agar-agar is obtained from members of Rhodophyceae algae collectively called agarophytes. These include Gelidium, Gracillaria, etc.
Agar is a jelly, like substance used for culturing processes. It is used as laxatives in bacteriological and mycological media.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The plant of  Triticum aestivum is
questions.solution: The common bread wheat  (Triticum aestivum) is an allohexaploid, which has two copies of each of the genomes A, B and D. Its somatic complement is represented by $A A B B D D$.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Fern spores are usually
questions.solution: In pteridophytes, spore is a haploid structure, which develops after meiosis in the spore mother cell. On germination, it gives rise to a green haploid prothallus (gametophyte) which is monoecious, i.e., has both antheridia (male sex organs) and archegonia (female sex organs).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following plant cells is not surrounded by a cell wall?
questions.solution: Gamete is the haploid reproductive cell that fuse with another gamete to form a diploid zygote. These are not surrounded by the cell wall. On the other hand, root hair cell, stem hair cell and bacterial cell, all possess a well defined cell wall.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Calyptra develops from
questions.solution: Calyptra is a covering developed from the ventre of archegonium in bryophytes and pteridophytes. It acts as a transpiration shield around the immature capsule and provides protection to the young capsule.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Protonema is the stage in the life cycle of
questions.solution: Protonema is the juvenile stage of moss resulting from the germinating meiospore and consists of a slender, green, branching system of filaments. In  Funaria, the protonema stage is only vegetative and transitory, which precedes the upright, leafy gametophyte.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A fern differs from a moss in having
questions.solution: A fern (pteridophyte) differs from a moss (bryophyte) in having independent gametophyte, while in moss the sporophyte is simpler than the gametophyte and remains attached to the parent gametophyte throughout its life. This sporophyte is dependent upon gametophyte partially or wholly for its nutrition.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Female cone of Pinus is a
questions.solution: Female cone of Pinus is a modified long. It occurs on the main stem singly or in pairs. It is homologous to inflorescence of angiosperms. The female cone consists of numerous megasporophylls or female flowers or carpels, which are arranged around the cone axis in a spiral manner. Each megasporophyll is a modified dwarf shoot since it arises in the axil of scaly leaf which is now termed as bract scale. Hence, option B is correct.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Prothallus of the fern produces
questions.solution: Prothallus is a small, flattened, multicellular structure that represents the independent gametophyte generation of a fern. The prothalli bear antheridia (male sex organs) and archegonia (female sex organs) which produce male and female gametes, respectively.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In gymnosperms, the ovule is naked because
questions.solution: Gymnosperms are commonly known as 'naked seed plants' because the ovary wall is not present and hence, the ovules remain unprotected or naked.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Shape of chloroplast of Ulothrix is
questions.solution: The chloroplast of Ulothrix is girdle-shaped containing one or more pyrenoids. The thallus of Ulothrix is filamentous, long, unbranched and multicellular. It is a freshwater alga growing on substratum like rocks, sand, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one correctly describe reproduction and life cycle of fern?
questions.solution: During favourable month sporangia are produced on the underside or margin of leaves in ferns. After development, it become dry and bursts to release spores. These spores germinate on the suitable substrate. Each spore absorb water, then ruptures and produce green filament which grow into prothallus (heart-shaped). Prothallus develops into young sporophyte, which in turn grows into an adult plant.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A fern prothallus is bisexual. If fertilisation takes place between their gametes than it is known as
questions.solution: Self-fertilisation is the union of male and female gametes produced by the same individual, ie, from a bisexual individual. Cross-fertilisation is the fertilisation of male and female gametes produced by different individuals. Isogamous means fusion of isogametes (ie, of same morphology and physiology). Viviparous means giving birth to young ones.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Most primitive member, in which roots are nc present, is
questions.solution: Psilophytes are the oldest of seedless vascular plants. The plants are characterised by the absence of roots and presence of rhizoids, eg, Rhynia is the most primitive member of pteridophytes. Lycopodium and Selaginella are developed pteridophytes in which plant body is differentiated into roots, stems and leaves.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not the feature of gymnosperms?
questions.solution: Gymnosperms (Gymno $=$ naked; sperma $=$ seed) are naked seed bearing perennial plants. In gymnosperms, there is no fruit formation (because ovary is absent); xylem without vessels and phloem without companion cells and have distinct branches.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Indusium is found in
questions.solution:
Indusium is a membranous structure which covers a group of sporangia below it. It is differentiated, into a kidney-shaped dorsal plate called hood and a short stalk through which it remains attached with the undersurface of the pinnule at a swollen base called placenta. The sporangia attached with a placenta form group called sorus (pl. sori). Different types of sorus (simple, gradate, mixed) are seen to be present in a box like structure called sporocarp formed on the vegetative leaf, $i e$, frond of ferns.
Such spore producing structure, sorus, covered by a membranous structure is not found in any group of plants (Algae, Bryophyta, Gymnosperms) except, ferns of Pteridophyta.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Pinus, male cone bears a large number of
questions.solution: Microsporophylls andmegasporophylls are the leaf-like structures on which microsporangia (pollen sacs) and magasporangia are borne on the same plant of Pinus. The sporophylls are aggregated in form of cones. (a) Male cones are smaller, also known as microstrobili (staminate strobili). (b) Female cones are larger, known as macrostrobili (ovulate strobili).Each male cone consists of an elongated axis, bearing a number of spirally arrangedmicrosporophylls. On the underside of which two microsporangia develop and get filled with microspores (pollen grains).Anther ormicrosporangium is the male reproductive structures ofangiosperms. Microsporangium forms microspores (pollens), through microsporogenesis.Ligule is a tongue-like membranous outgrowth, present on upper surface, near the base of the microphyllous leaf in pteridophytes (eg, Selaginella). The ligule shrinks and becomes inconspicuous in mature leaves.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cleavage polyembryony occurs in
questions.solution: Polyembryony is of common occurrence among the gymnosperms. This is possible because more than one archegonia are fertilized and more than one zygotes are formed. These develops into embryos, but only one of them succeeds in developing into a complete embryo. In the conifers there is a cleavage polyembryony. In this case all the four cells of the young embryo separate and develop into 4 embryos, but only one completely develop and others abort, eg Pinus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Agar-agar is obtained from
questions.solution:
Gelidium, Gracilaria, Pterocladia are red algae having industrial importance. They produce a jelly like substance agar-agar, used as culture medium with a number of different uses.
Chlorella, a green alga contains 50\% protein, vitamins. Antibiotic chlorellin has been extracted from Chlorella, found to be more effective against bacteria.
Spirogyra, a green alga is also known as water-silk.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Cycas, pollination takes place in
questions.solution:
In Cycas, pollination takes place in 3-cellec stage, $i e$, pollen grain divides to form three cells, which are
(i) Prothallial cell
(ii) Generative cell
(iii) Tube cell.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following is heterosporous?
questions.solution: The sporophyte of pteridophyte produces meiospores inside sporangia, which may be homosporous (e.g., Adiantum, Dryopteris, Equisetum, etc.) or heterosporous (e.g., Salvinia, Selaginella, etc.)

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Calyptra is derived from
questions.solution: Calyptra is a small sheath of cells, derived from the archegonia, which covers top of the capsule.Capsule is a sporophytic region of mosses.Antheridia is a small structure that produces sperms in bryophytes or pteridophytes. In fungi the young sporangium is a white spherical structure situated at the free end of the sporangiophore. At maturity it turns black. The black colour is due to the presence of sooty spores. The centre of the sporangium is a large dome-shaped structure called the columella.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is/are grouped under phanerogams?
questions.solution: Eichler (1883) divided plant kingdom into two sub-kingdoms. (i)Cryptogamae- Plants having no flowers such as algae, fungi, bryophytes and pteridophytes (ii)Phanerogamae- Plants having evident reproductive organs like flowers and seeds such as angiosperms and gymnosperms.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: From outer to inside the sequence of three bones present in the middle ear of mammals is
questions.solution: The middle ear cavity in mammals characteristically contains a chain of three little bones or ear ossicles extending between the tympanic membrane and the fenestra ovalis. These are called from outside as the malleus (hammer), incus (anvil) and stapes (stirrup), so named because of their characteristic shapes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Yersinia pestis is responsible for
questions.solution: Bubonic plague (black death) is caused by Yersinia pestis (formerly Pasteurella pestis) bacteria.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In which one of the following, the male and female gametophytes don't have free-living independent existence?
questions.solution:
In  Pteris (also Dryopteris), the spore germinates to produce the prothallus. The prothallus is a small, green, flat, surface loving, thallus-like object. It is monoecious and bears sex organs on the ventral side. The antheridia (male sex organs) arise among the rhizoids towards the posterior side of the prothallus and are emergent. The archegonia develop in central cushion behind the apical notch. In these plants, male and female gametophytes do not have free living independent existence.
All species of Polytrichum are dioecious. The antheridia and archegonia are borne on different gametophore. The plant body is an errect leafy shoot but is not the entire gametophyte. The leafy shoot arise from protonema (the juvenile stage).
The leafy gametophore of Funaria reproduces sexually by formation of antheridia and archegonia. The antheridia are formed at the summit of a relatively small, thin, leafy shoot, which develops first. The female branch arises later as a lateral outgrowth from the base of parent male shoot.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Iodine is obtained from
questions.solution: Brown algae such as Laminaria, Macrocystis, Fucus etc are the main source of iodine.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following is a saprophytic bryophyte ?
questions.solution: Bryophytes usually grow in moist and shady places. Some bryophytes grow in diverse habitats such as aquatic (eg, Riccia fluitans), epiphytic (eg, Dendroceros and many mosses) saprophytic (eg, Buxbaumia aphylla) and in dry habitats such as dry heaths (eg, Polytrichum juniperinum) deserts (eg, Tortula desertorum) and dry rocks (eg, Porella platyphylla).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Top-shaped multiciliate male gametes and the mature seed which bears only one embryo with two cotyledons, are characteristic features of
questions.solution: Cycads is a group of gymnosperms, which have top shaped multiciliated male gametes (largest male gamete) and the each mature seed of these plants contain one embryo and two cotyledons (dicotyledonous) eg, Cycas.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following as a living fossil ?
questions.solution: Linkgo, a member of order linkgoales, is called living fossil because all the others except Ginko biloba are now extinct.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The amphibians of plant kingdom are :
questions.solution: The bryophytes are terrestrial plants, generally growing in moist and shady places but always need water for fertilization. Hence, these are called amphibians of the plant kingdom.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The alga used for quick sewage disposal is :
questions.solution: Certain algae such as Chlorella, Chlamydomonas, Scenedesmus, Oscillatoria etc., are used for the quick disposal of sewage, domestic and some industrial wastes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Tea and coffee are affected by
questions.solution: The green algae Cephaleuros virescens causes red rust of tea. Thus, destroying the tea leaves. Similar disease is caused by the species of Cephaleuros to coffee plant, piper and Citrus species.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Though Cycas has 2 cotyledons, this is not included in dicot because
questions.solution: In Cycas ovules are found without ovary, this condition is called naked ovule. Cycas produces largest ovules in the plant kingdom. The ovules are orthotropous and unitegmic.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Mannitol is reserve food in
questions.solution: Class Phaeophyceae includes brown algae Brown algae are marine plants. Chief pigments found in the members of this class are chloropyll $a$ and $c$, $\beta$-carotene, violaxanthin, fucoxanthin, lutein, diatoxanthin. Reserve food is laminarin, mannitol and oils.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Plants growing on sand and gravel are called
questions.solution: Psammophytes grow in sand and gravel, eremophytes grow in desert and gravel, psilophytes grow in savanna and oxylophytes grow on acidic soil.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The amphibians of plant kingdom are
questions.solution: In bryophytes water is essential for fertilization. It provides a medium of transport for antherozoids to reach archegonia. Hence, they are called amphibians of plant kingdom.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Heterospory is the production of
questions.solution: In some pteridophytes two types of spores are formed which differ significantly in their size and also in function. This phenomenon is called heterospory e.g., Selaginella and Marsilea etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Algal zone is present in
questions.solution: The cortex in coralloid roots of Cycas is divided into inner and outer regions by algal zone. The cells of this zone contain endophytic algal forms particularly Anabaena cycadeae and Nostoc punctiforme.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In Chlorophyceae the mode of sexual reproduction is
questions.solution: In isogamy, gametes are morphologically and physiologically same, in anisogamy gametes are morphologically different but physiologically same and in oogamy, gametes are both morphologically and physiologically different e.g., Ulothrix and Spirogyra members of Chlorophyceae.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not the feature of gymnosperms?
questions.solution: Gymnosperms (Gymno $=$ naked + sperma $=$ seed) are naked seeded plants in which ovule is not covered by ovary. In gymnosperms, xylem contains only tracheids and xylem parenchyma; vessels are absent (exceptionally present in Gnetales)

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Match items in column-I with those in Column-II
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}
\hline & \text{Column - I} & & \text{Column - II} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Peritrichous flagellation} & \text{1.} & \text{Ginkgo} \\
