or $\quad \mathrm{p}_2=2 \mathrm{p}_1$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: 1000 N force is required to lift a hook and 10000 N force is requires to lift a load slowly. Find power required to lift hook with load with speed $\mathrm{v}=0.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.solution:
$\because$ Net force required to lift a hook and load,
$\mathrm{F}_{\text {net }}=1000+10000=11000 \mathrm{~N}$
$\because$ Power required to lift hook, $\mathrm{P}=\frac{\mathrm{W}}{\mathrm{t}}$
$\begin{aligned} \because \quad \mathrm{W} & =\mathrm{F}_{\text {net }} \mathrm{d} \\ \mathrm{P} \times \mathrm{t} & =\mathrm{F}_{\text {net }} \mathrm{d} \\ \mathrm{P} & =\frac{\mathrm{F}_{\text {net }} \mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{t}}=\mathrm{F}_{\text {net }}\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{t}}\right)=\mathrm{F}_{\text {net }} \mathrm{V} \quad\left[\text { Here, } \mathrm{v}=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{t}}\right]\end{aligned}$
or $\mathrm{P}=11000 \times 0.5=5500 \mathrm{~W}=5.5 \mathrm{~kW}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A mass of $M \mathrm{~kg}$ is suspended by a weightless string. The horizontal force that is required to displace it until the string makes an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the initial vertical direction is
questions.solution: Here, the constant horizontal force required to take the body from position 1 to position 2 can be calculated by using work-energy theorem. Let us assume that body is taken slowly so that its speed doesn't change, then $\begin{aligned} \Delta K & =0 \\ & =W_F+W_{M g}+W_{\text {tension }} \end{aligned}$ [symbols have their usual meanings] $\begin{aligned} W_F & =F \times l \sin 45^{\circ}, \\ W_{M g} & =M_g\left(l-l \cos 45^{\circ}\right), W_{\text {tension }}=0 \\ \therefore \quad F & =M g(\sqrt{2}-1) \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: An alpha nucleus of energy $\frac{1}{2} m v^2$ bombards a heavy nuclear target of charge $Z e$. Then the distance of closest approach for the alpha nucleus will be proportional to
questions.solution:
Since, here nuclear target is heavy it can be assumed safely that it will remain stationary and will not move due to the Coulombic interaction force.
At distance of closest approach relative velocity of two particles is $v$. Here target is considered as stationary, so $\alpha$-particle comes to rest instantaneously at distance of closest approach. Let required distance is $r$, then from work energy-theorem.
$\begin{aligned} 0-\frac{m v^2}{2} & =-\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{Z e \times 2 e}{r} \\ \Rightarrow \quad r & \propto \frac{1}{m} \\ & \propto \frac{1}{v^2} \\ & \propto Z e^2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Two blocks of masses $m_1$ and $m_2$ are connected by a spring of spring constant $k$. The block of mass $m_2$ is given a sharp empulse so that it acquires a velocity $v_0$ towards right. Find the maximum elongation that the spring will suffer.
questions.solution:
The velocity of centre of mass
$v_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{m_1 v_1+m_2 v_2}{m_1+m_2}$
When $v_1=0$ and $v_2=v_0$, then
$v_{c m}=\frac{m_2 v_0}{m_1+m_2}$
Now, let $x$ be the elongation in the spring.
Change in potential energy $=$ potential energy stored in spring
$\begin{gathered}
\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{2} m_2 v_0^2-\frac{1}{2}\left(m_1+m_2\right) \\
\left(\frac{m_2 V_0}{m_1+m_2}\right)^2=\frac{1}{2} k x^2
\end{gathered}$
This gives, $x=\left(\frac{m_1 m_2}{m_1+m_2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} v_0$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A particle moves from position $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}_1=3 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+2 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-6 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ to position $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}_2=14 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+13 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+9 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ under action of force $\mathbf{F}=4 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+3 \hat{\mathbf{k}} \mathrm{N}$. The work done will be:
questions.solution:
Using the formula for work done
$W=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{F}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{F}} \cdot\left(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}_2}-\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}_1}\right)$
$\begin{array}{r}W=(4 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+3 \hat{\mathbf{k}}) \cdot(14 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+13 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+9 \hat{\mathbf{k}}) -3 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-2 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+6 \hat{\mathbf{k}})\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & W=(4 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+3 \hat{\mathbf{k}}) \cdot(11 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+11 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+15 \hat{\mathbf{k}}) \\ & W=44+11+45=100 \text { joule }\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: The area of the acceleration-displacement curve of a body gives
options: change in momentum per unit mass
change in KE per unit mass
questions.solution:
Area of acceleration-displacement curve gives change in KE per unit mass.
$\frac{1}{2} m\left(v^2-u^2\right)=F s=\frac{m d v}{d t} \times s$
$\therefore \quad \frac{\text { Change in KE }}{\text { Mass }}=\frac{d v}{d t} \times s$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: The work done in pulling up a block of wood weighing 2 kN for a length of 10 m on a smooth plane inclined at an angle of $15^{\circ}$ with the horizontal is
questions.solution: Here : Weight of block $w=2 \mathrm{kN}$, Distance $d=10 \mathrm{~m}$ Angle of inclination on the plane $\alpha=15^{\circ}$ The block will be pulled up on a smooth plane Hence, force of resistance due to inclination $\begin{aligned} F & =w \sin \alpha=2 \times 10^3 \sin 15^{\circ} \\ & =2 \times 10^3 \times 0.2588 \\ & =0.5176 \mathrm{kN}\end{aligned}$ Now work done, $\begin{aligned}W=F d & =0.5176 \times 10^3 \times 10 \\& =5.17 \mathrm{kJ}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: A force $F$ acting on an object varies with distance $x$ as shown here. The force is in newton and distance is in metre. The work done by the force in moving the object from $x=0$ to $x=6 \mathrm{~m}$ is
questions.solution:
The work done will be the area of the $F$-x graph.
Work done in moving the object from $x=0$ to $x=6 \mathrm{~m}$ is given by
$W=$ area of rectangle + area of triangle
$\begin{aligned} & =3 \times 3+\frac{1}{2} \times 3 \times 3 \\ & =9+4.5=13.5 \mathrm{~J}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: 300 J of work is done in sliding a 2 kg block up an inclined plane of height 10 m . Taking $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$, work done against friction is
questions.solution:
Net work done in sliding a body up to a height $h$ on inclined plane
$\begin{array}{r}=\text { Work done against gravitational force } \\ \quad+\text { Work done against frictional force }\end{array}$
$\Rightarrow \quad W=W_g+W_f$  ...(i)
but $W=300 \mathrm{~J}$
$W_g=m g h=2 \times 10 \times 10=200 \mathrm{~J}$
Putting in Eq. (i), we get
$\begin{gathered} \\ 300=200+W_f \\ \Rightarrow \quad W_f=300-200=100 \mathrm{~J}\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In annealing
options: primers anneal to the DNA template
Taq polymerase adds nucleotides to the annealed primer
two strands of the DNA separate
questions.solution: In annealing, primers anneal to the DNA template, to provide a site for initiation of polymerisation to enzyme Taq polymerase. Before this denaturation of DNA takes place in which two strands of DNA separate from each other at high temperature of $92-95^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Extensions is last stage of PCR, in which Taq polymerase enzyme adds nucleotides to annealed primer and synthesise new strand.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following DNA sequences qualifies to be designated as a palindrome?
questions.solution: Palindromic sequence is the sequence of nucleotides whose reading is same when read from both $5^{\prime} \rightarrow 3^{\prime}$ and $3^{\prime} \rightarrow 5^{\prime}$ direction. It is the mirror image of the sequence in its first half

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Genetic variation cannot be effectively brought about by
questions.solution: Genetic variation cannot be effectively brought about by amplification. It is a technique used in molecular biology, by which multiple copies of gene or DNA sequence is produced by in vivo or in vitro methods. It has no role in introducing genetic variation in organism. It just leads to an increase in the frequency of gene.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following is a recognisation site for restriction enzyme Bam HI?
questions.solution: For restriction enzyme Bam HI the unique recognition site is

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following statements is false regarding SDS-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis?
options: Proteins are separated by molecular weight
SDS is a detergent which gives charge to protein
Large protein move more slowly through gel
SDS is used to maintain the 3-dimentional structure of protein
questions.solution: For protein SDS (Sodium Dodecyl Sulphate) is an anionic detergent applied to protein sample linearize proteins and to impart a negative charge to linearzed proteins.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gene transfer is present in
questions.solution: Gene transfer is present in biolistics. This technique involves firing of microscopic gold particles coated with the foreign DNA at the cells using a compressed air gun. It is designed to overcome the problem of strong cell wall in plant tissues. These particles can penetrate the cell wall, the cell and the nuclear membrane to deliver the DNA to the nucleus.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The vector for T-DNA is
questions.solution:
- Plant transformation mediated by the soil pathogen, Agrobacterium tumefaciens has become the most used method for plant transformation.
- It naturally infects the wound sites in dicots causing the crown gall disease. It is capable to transfer T-DNA of the Tumour inducing (Ti) plasmid into the nucleus of infected cells.
- It will then integrate into the host genome and is transcribed, causing the crown gall disease.
- Thus, the correct answer is option C.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which statement is correct for bacterial transduction?
options: Transfer of some genes from one bacteria to another bacteria through virus
Transfer of genes from one bacteria to another bacteria by conjugation
Bacteria obtain DNA from other external source
questions.solution: Transduction involves the picking up of DNA by bacteriophage from one bacterial cell and carrying it to another where the DNA fragment may get incorporated into the bacterial host's genome.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The mobile genetic element is
questions.solution: The mobile genetic element is broadly any genetic element capable of moving itself with or without duplication from one site in a genome to another.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In recombinant DNA technique, the term vector refers to
options: donor DNA, is identified and picked up through electrophoresis
plasmid, transfers DNA into living cell
collection of entire genome in form of plasmid
enzyme, cuts the DNA at specific sites
questions.solution: Vector is a plasmid or virus DNA used to introduced genes into a host cell, where the genes may be amplified (gene cloning or otherwise manipulated.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Given below is a sample of portion of DNA strand given the base sequence on the opposite strand. What is so special show in it?
$\begin{aligned}& \text { 5'- GAATTC...3' } \\& \text { 3'- CTTAAG ... 5' }\end{aligned}$
questions.solution: Palindromic DNA is base sequence of DNA which reads the same forward and backward. It has similar sequence in both the strands. Different types of palindromic sequences are recognised by restriction endonucleases.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The thermostable enzymes, Taq and Pfu, isolated from thermophilic bacteria are
questions.solution:
'Taq' and 'Pfu' are thermostable enzymes, which are isolated from thermophilic bacteria.
They are DNA polymerase in nature, which are widely used in polymerase chain reaction.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gel electrophoresis is used for
options: separation of DNA fragments according to their size
construction of recombinant DNA by joining with cloning vectors
questions.solution:
When genomic DNA extracted from any tissue of a plant or animal species is digested with a restriction enzyme, it is cleaved into segments. The segments of different sizes can be separated through gel electrophoresis. Gel electrophoresis involves movement of fragments or molecules on the basis if molecule size and shape under the influence of electric field.
Endonucleases are enzymes that produce internal cuts called cleavage in DNA molecules. It cleaves DNA only within or near recognition sites, which have specific base sequences, such endonucleases are known as restriction endonucleases.
The selected vector is cut open with a restriction enzyme that has a unique target site located in the sequence, where the DNA insert should be integrated to facilitate the selection of recombinant DNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Biolistic technique is used in
questions.solution: Biolistics is a technique far introducing genetic material into living cells, especially plant cells in which DNA- coated microscopic particles are fired into the cell using a special gun.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A kind of biotechnology involving manipulation of DNA is
questions.solution: In genetic engineering $r \mathrm{DNA}$ technology is applied to several biotechnological processes for obtaining particular biochemical improvement of genetic make up of an organism and fighting genetic defects.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Hamburger shift is also known as
questions.solution: To maintain electrostatic neutrality of plasma, many chloride ions diffuse from plasma into RBCs and bicarbonate ions pass out. The chloride content of RBCs increases when oxygenated blood becomes deoxygenated. This is termed as chloride shift or Hamburger shift.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is used as a best genetic vector in plants ?
questions.solution: Agrobacterium tumifaciens is used as a best genetic vector in plants.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following hydrolysis internal phosphodiester bonds in a polynucleotide chain?
questions.solution:
DNA ase (or deoxyribonuclease) or simply nuclease is an enzyme which breaks down DNA by hydrolysis of the phosphodiester bonds of its sugar-phosphate back-bone. Depending on the position of hydrolysing phosphodiester bonds, nucleases are of two types:
(i) Endonucleases (ii)Exonucleases.
Endonucleases hydrolyse internal phosphodiester bonds in a polynucleotide chain (i.e., DNA). While exonucleases hydrolyse terminal phosphodiester bonds in a polynucleotide chain (i.e., DNA).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following pairs is correctly matched?
questions.solution: Restriction enzyme recognises specific nucleotide sequences in DNA and then makes a double-stranded cleavage of DNA molecule. Restriction enzymes are called genetic scissors as they slice off genes and is recently being used in gene therapy technique. They are key tool of genetic engineering. Central dogma is related with protein synthesis. Okazaki fragments are found during DNA synthesis and RNA polymerase is used in synthesis of RNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Micro-organism which act as a vehicle for foreign gene transfer in higher plants:
questions.solution: The bacterium-Agrobacterium acts as a vehicle for transfer of DNA or genes from one organism to another organism because it infects all dicot plants.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Restriction enzymes are used in genetic engineering, because they
options: can cut DNA at specific base sequence
are nucleases that cut DNA at variable sites
questions.solution: Restriction enzyme are also known as molecular knifes or molecular scissors. They are isolated from bacteria. They recognize specific base sequence in DNA and cleave both strands of it

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which is also called molecular glue
questions.solution:
Enzyme DNA ligase is also called as molecule or glue, as it works to repair broken DNA by joining two nucleotides in a DNA strand. It is commonly used in genetic engineering to do the reverse of restriction enzyme, i.e,. to join together complementary restriction fragments.
DNA gyrase is an enzyme of class topoisomerase that relieves torque or strain in DNA during DNA replication. DNA helicase unwinds the double helix to make site of DNA synthesis. DNA polymerase catalyses formation of new DNA on template strand.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is the Pribnow box?
questions.solution: The promoters is bacterial and viral genes usually contains a consensus sequence of $5^{\prime}-$ TATAAT-3' forming RNA polymerase binding site or the Pribnow box, after its discoverer. Pribnow box lies with in the promoter about 10 base pairs before the starting point of transcription.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The process which can not take place in the absence of viruses is
questions.solution: Transduction is the transfer of bacterial genes from one bacterium to another through virus.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Product of biotechnology is
questions.solution: Biotechnology may be, simply defined as the use of microorganisms animals or plant's cells or thin components to generate products and services useful to human beings. Now-a-days, biotechnology is very helpful in producing transgenic crops or Genetically Modified (GM) crops, transgenic animals, biofertilizers, antibodies, hormones like humulin (genetically engineered human insulin), antibodies and various other useful products.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The protein $\alpha-1$ antitrypsin is used to treat which of the following disease?
questions.solution: GMO can be created by the introduction of the portion of DNA (or gene) which codes for a particular product such as human protein, $\alpha$-1-antitrypsin. This protein is used to treat emphyseme a respiratory disorder.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Golden ages of reptiles and fishes are respectively
questions.solution: Mesozoic period was golden age for reptiles. In this period reptiles were dominant species, like dinosaurs. Devonian period was golden age for fishes due to their abundance and diversity.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Nucleic acid segment tagged with a radiactive molecule is called
questions.solution: Probe are $15-30$ bases long radioactive labelled oligonucleotides (RNA or DNA) used to detect complementary nucleotide sequence, used for disease diagonsis, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Golden rice is a transgenic crop of the future with the following improved trait
questions.solution: Generally seeds of rice do not have vitamin-A, but golden rice, which is developed through genetic engineering has the high vitamin-A content.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Herbicide resistant gene in plant is
questions.solution: Herbicide resistant gene in plants is $B t$ gene Bacillus thuringiensis is the bacterium that occur naturally in soil. It is now used as herbicide or biopesticide.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following can help in the diagnosis of a genetical disorder?
questions.solution: PCR is a technique in which a small fragment of DNA is rapidly cloned or duplicated to produce multiple DNA copies. Thus, it helps in the diagnosis of a genetical disorder. This technique was conceived by American biochemist Kary B. Mullis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Who received Nobel Prize in 1951 for the development of vaccine for yellow fever?
questions.solution: Max Theiler received Nobel Prize in 1951 for the development of vaccines for yellow fever.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Bacillus thuringiensis $(B t)$ strains have been used for designing novel
questions.solution: Bt strain have been used to design bioinsecticidal plants through genetic engineering.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following bio-engineered bacteria is utilized for cleaning of marine oil slicks?
questions.solution: Pseudomonas putida is a genetically engineered bacterium with many different plasmids to degrade the pollutants. It is developed by Dr. Anand Mohan Chakraborty and is known as superbug or oil eating bug or Chakraborty's superbug. Now-a-days, this genetically engineered bacterium is utilized for cleaning of marine oil slicks.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) strains have been used for designing novel
questions.solution: Bioinsecticides are those biological agents, which are used to control harmful insects. A bacterium, Bacillus thuringiensis is used for this purpose. Spores of this bacterium produce the insecticidal cry-protein. Therefore, spores, of this bacterium kill larvae of certain insects. The commercial preparations of B. thuringiensis contain a mixture of spores, cry-protein and an inert carrier.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A transgenic food crop, which may help in solving the problem of nightblindness in developing countries is
questions.solution:
Vitamin-A deficiency causes nightblindness among children. Vitamin-A deficiency often occurs, where rice is the staple food since, rice grain does not contain $\beta$-carotene. Three transgenes providing phytoene synthase, phytoene desaturase, $\beta$ carotene desaturase and lycopene cyclase activities were transferred into rice by Agrobacterium mediated transformation. The resulting transgenic rice popularly called Golden rice contains good quantities of $\beta$-carotene.
Flavr savr is genetically modified tomato, which remains fresh and retains their flavour much longer than normal tomato due to blocking of synthesis of fruit softening enzyme polygalacturonase.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Purkinje's fibres are found in
questions.solution: Purkinje's fibres are found in heart. These are the part of impulse conducting network of the heart. They rapidly transmit impulses from the atrioventricular node to the ventricles.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Chordae tendineae is found in
questions.solution:
Chordae tendineae is found in heart muscles of mammals.
Chordae tendineae are group of tough, tendinous strands found in the heart of mammals. They are also called as 'heart strings' due to their resemblence with small pieces of string. They play an important role in holding the atrioventricular valves in place while heart is pumping blood.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In cardiac cycle maximum time is taken by
questions.solution: Cardiac cycle is the event during, which one heartbeat or one cycle of contraction (systole) and relaxation (diastole) of cardiac muscle occurs. A cardiac cycle consists of systole and diastole of atria and systole and diastole of ventricles. The time of cardiac cycle is in reverse ratio of the rate of heartbeat. In man, the heart rate is about 72 times $/ \mathrm{min}$, therefore time of a cardiac cycle is $60 / 72=0.8 \mathrm{sec}$ approx .

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Foramen ovale
options: connects the two atria in the foetal heart.
is a condition in which the heart valves do not completely close
is a shallow depression in the inter ventricular septum
is a connection between the pulmonary trunk and the aorta in the foetus
questions.solution:
Foramen ovale is an opening in the interatrial septum of the foetal heart through which both the atria communicate with each other.
In adults, this aperture is closed and is represented by a small oval depression called fossa ovalis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Intrinsic and extrinsic pathways of blood clotting are interlinked at the activation steps of which of the following factors?
questions.solution: Both intrinsic and extrinsic pathway of blood clotting are interlinked at the final common pathway of factor $X$, thrombin and fibrin.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Band 3 protein in RBC has the function of
options: it mediates the exchange of cellular $\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}$with $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$in plasma
it mediates the exchange of cations between cell and plasma
questions.solution: Band 3 protein is a typical polytropic memberane protein that mediates the exchange of cellular bicarbonate ions $\left(\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}\right)$with the chloride $\left(\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right)$ion in plasma, which has been known as the 'chloride shift.' Owing to this, RBC can discriminate the metabolically active cells from inactive cells and deliver oxygen particularly to metabolically active tissue that produce carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: If due to some injury the chordae tendinae of the tricuspid valve of the human heart is partially non-functional, what will be the immediate effect?
options: The flow of blood into the aorta will be slowed down
The blood will tend to flow back into the left atrium
The flow of blood into the pulmonary artery will be reduced
questions.solution: If cordae tendinae of the tricuspid valve become partially non-functional due to injury then flow of blood into the pulmonary artery will be reduced.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A hormone, secreted by the endocrinal cells of duodenal mucosa which influences the release of pancreatic juice is
questions.solution: Cholecystokinin Pancreozymin (CCK-PZ) is the hormone secreted from mucosa of small intestine. It stimulates pancreas to release enzymatic (Pancreatic) juice and gall bladder to eject bile.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Inhibin is composed of
questions.solution: Inhibins are dimeric glycoproteins composed of an $\alpha$-subunit and either a $\beta_{\mathrm{A}}$ (inhibin A) or a $\beta_{\mathrm{B}}$ subunit (inhibin B). They are mainly produced in gonads and provide negative regulation of FSH secretion.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not considered as a secondary messenger?
questions.solution:
Acetylcholine is not considered as a secondary messanger.
It is a common neurotransmitter present in the neuromuscular junctions, voluntary neural synapses, synapses of pre-ganglionic nerve fibres, synapses of post-ganglionic parasympathetic nerve fibres. Cholinergic nerve fibres release acetylcholine. It has excitatory effect on the skeletal muscles and excitatory or inhibitory effect at the other sites.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Match the following Columns.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which hormone produces calorigenic effect?
questions.solution: Calorigenic effect refers to the substance or process that produces heat or energy or that increases the consumption of $\mathrm{O}_2$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Adrenaline and noradrenaline are hormones that act as
questions.solution: There are four neurotransmitters identified in vertebrates acetylcholine, serotonin, adranaline and noradrenaline.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Steroid hormones work as
options: they enter into target cells and binds with specific receptor and activates specific genes to form protein
questions.solution: Steroid hormone are lipid soluble, so easily pass through plasma membrane.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Endemic goitre is a state of
questions.solution:
Goitre is a swelling of the neck due to enlargement of the thyroid gland. This may be due to lack of dietary iodine, which occurs due to decreases thyroid function, which is necessary for the production of thyroxine hormone.
This was the cause of endemic goitre, formerly common in regions, where the people lacked in their diet iodine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gigantism and acromegaly are due to
questions.solution:
The anterior pituitary secretes growth hormone. Often, it is known as somatotropin. Growth hormone hypersecretion produces gigantism in children and acromegaly in adults. A tumor in the thyroid is responsible for the hypersecretion of growth hormone in the pituitary gland (hyperpituitarism).
Acromegaly has many unusual signs, including hand and foot enlargement, joint pain, tighter skin, deepening of the voice, headaches, and vision issues.
Excessive growth of bones and enlargement of internal organs is caused by gigantism. Gigantism contributes to severe linear development, headaches, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Calcitonin is a thyroid hormone which
questions.solution: Calcitonin is a polypeptide hormone which lowers calcium and phosphate levels of plasma by inhibiting bone degradation and stimulating their uptake by bone. Parathyroid hormone elevates calcium level in blood.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Glycogenolysis involves
questions.solution:
Glycogenesis-conversion of sugar to glycogen.
Glycogenolysis-conversion of glycogen to sugar.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Bradykinin is a
questions.solution:
Bradykinin is a peptide hormone. It functions as a potent endothelium-dependent vasodilator and mild diuretic, which may cause lowering of blood pressure.
It also causes contraction of non-vascular smooth muscle in the bronchus and gut, increases vascular permeability. It is also involved in the mechanism of inflammation and pain.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What is incorrect about inhibin?
questions.solution: Statement (1) is incorrect about inhibin. It is a glycoprotein, not lipoprotein. In females, it is secreted by the corpus luteum while in males it is secreted by Sertoli cells. Inhibin inhibits FSH production from the anterior lobe of pituitary gland. Its molecular weight is $10-30 \mathrm{k}$ Dalton.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Function of hypothalamus is
questions.solution: The functions of hypothalamus include thermoregulation, water balance and control of hormone functions. It is located below the thalamus and is a part of limbic system. It synthesises and secretes certain neurohormones which in turn stimulate or inhibit the secretion of pituitary hormones. It controls other activities also like sleep, hunger, body temperature, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: During emergency which of the following hormone is secreted?
questions.solution: Adrenalin (epinephrine) is secreted by Chromaffin cells of adrenal medulla. The secretion of adrenalin is directly controlled by the autonomic nervous system, but not under the control of pituitary. Adrenalin is also called 'emergency hormone' because it contributes the fright, fight or flight reactions' which occur in condition of emergency. Increase in emotional disturbance increases the secretion of adrenalin, accelerating the rate of respiration, dilating respiratory passage, increasing blood pressure, heart beat, sugar level and stimulating cellular metabolism.Aldosterone, is a mineralocorticoid or salt retaining hormone secreted by adrenal cortex. Its basic funtion is in conservation of sodium and water and dimination of potassium.Thyroxine (tri-iodothyronine) is manufactured in thyroid gland and is synthesized from iodine. Its main function is to control metabolism, regulating tissue growth and development.Calcitonin orThyrocalcitonin (TCT) is a non-iodized hormone, secreted by parafollicular cells of thyroid. Its main role is to lower the amount of calcium and phosphate in the blood.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following statement is correct in relation to the endocrine system?
options: Adenohypophysis is under direct neural regulation of the hypothalamus
Organs in the body like gastro-intestinal tract, heart, kidney and liver do not produce any hormones
Non-nutrient chemicals produced by the body in trace amount that act as inter-cellular messanger are known as hormones
Releasing and inhibitory hormones are produced by the pituitary gland
questions.solution: Endocrine cells are present in different parts of the gastro-intestinal tract, e.g. gastrin, secretin, GIP. Atrial wall of our heart secretes a peptide hormone called ANF (Atrial Natriuretic Factor). $\mathrm{RH} / \mathrm{IH}$ are produced by hypothalamus. Adenohypophysis is not directly under neural control, it is under the control of hypothalamic hormones, brought by portal system.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: LH and FSH are collectively called
questions.solution: Trophe $=$ nourishment. Most of the tropic hormones are secreted by anterior lobe of pituitary. Gonadotropins orgonadotropic hormones are those which stimulate the gonads (testes and ovaries),FSH (Follicle Stimulating Hormone) stimulates growth ofovarian follicles and the secretion ofoestrogens in thefemale andspermatogenesis (formation of sperms) in themale . Luteinizing Hormone(LH) stimulatescorpus luteum of the ovary to secreteprogesterone in the females. In male, it activates theLeydig's (interstitial)cells of the testis to secreteandrogens. Somatotropins or somatotropic hormone orgrowth hormone stimulates growth directly and in conjugation with other hormones the target cells of these hormones are various body cells which undergo growth.Luteotropic hormones stimulates the corpus luteum of the ovary to secrete progesterone hormone. It is also called Prolactin Hormone (PRL) orMammotropin Hormone (MTH) because its main physiological effect is to activate growth of breast during pregnancy and secretion of mammary glands after child birth.Oxytocin (OT; pitocin) is secreted by posterior lobe of pituitary. It promotes contraction of the uterine muscles and myoepithelial cells of the lactating breast, ejecting the milk after child birth. Oxytocin is calledbirth hormone andmilk ejecting hormone.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The function of pineal body is to
questions.solution: The pineal body (gland) is small mass of tissues near the centre of the mammalian brain. The pineal secretes the hormone melatonin, a modified amino acid. The pineal contains light sensitive cells and has nervous connections from the eyes and melatonin regulates function related to light. It also regulates sexual behaviour and regulating the period of puberty.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
choose the alternative which is opposite in meaning to the word given in italics in the sentence.
My uncle is very wealthy, but rather  < i>parsimonious < /i> in his habits.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
choose the alternative which is most nearly the same in meaning to the word given in italics in the sentence.
They  < i>venerated < /i> the old teacher.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Enzymes, vitamins and hormones can be classified into a single category of biological chemicals, because all of these
options: are exclusively synthesized in the body of a living organism as at present
questions.solution: Enzymes, vitamins and hormones are classified into a single category of biological chemical because all these help in regulation of metabolism. Enzyme is a proteinaceous catalyst produced by a cell and responsible for the high rate and specificity of one or more intercellular or intracellular biochemical reactions. Vitamin is an organic substance which generally synthesized by plants (exception vitamin-D). Absence of a vitamin from the diet for sufficient time gives symptoms of a resulting deficiency disease. Hormones are chemical messengers which on secretion bring about a specific and adaptive physiological response.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Secretin and cholecystokinin are digestive hormones. They are secreted in
questions.solution: Secretin and Cholecystokinin (CСK) are two main Gastrointestinal (GI) hormones secretes in duodenum of alimentary canal. CCK stimulates gall bladder contraction and thus increases the flow of bile salts into the intestine. Secretin stimulates the release of an alkaline pancreatic fluid that neutralizes stomach acid as it enters the intestine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following hormones is released by posterior lobe of pituitary gland?
questions.solution: Anti Diuretic Hormone $(\mathrm{ADH})$ is released by posterior lobe of pituitary gland which is also called vasopressin. It increase reabsorption of water by kidneys.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which is gonadotropin hormone?
questions.solution:
Gonadotropins or gonadotropic hormones are as follows.
(a) Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) : It stimulates growth of ovarian follicles and their secretion of oestrogen in the female and spermatogenesis in the male.
(b) Interstitial Cell Stimulating Hormone (ICSH) : It activates the Leydig's cells of the testis to secrete androgens. In female it stimulates the corpus luteum of the ovary to secrete progesterone. In female ICSH is termed as LH (Luteinizing Hormone).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following hormone stimulates the gastric secretion?
questions.solution:
Gastrin hormone is secreted by the the gastric glands to release the gastric juice. Enterogasterone is secreted by the duodenal epithelium. It inhibits the secretion of gastric juice and slows gastric contraction.
Cholecystokinin pancreozymin (CCK-PZ) is secreted by entire small intestine. It stimulates the gall bladder to release bile and the pancreas to release enzymes in the pancreatic juice.
Villikinin accelerates the movement of intestinal villi.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one affects liver, muscle and adipose tissue?
questions.solution: Insulin affects liver, muscle and adipose tissue. In the muscular tissue it acts to promote carbohydrate metabolism and storage of glycogen. In liver cells, it favours glycongenesis, glycolysis and increases lipogenesis. In adipose tissue, in enhances the membrane transfer of glucose and promotes lipogenesis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is both exocrine and endocrine gland?
questions.solution: Pancreas are partially exocrine and partially endocrine glands.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Blood calcium level can be increased by the administration of
questions.solution: Blood calcium level is regulated by Parathormone.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Follicle stimulating hormone is secreted by
questions.solution: Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) is a glycoproteins secreted from anterior lobe of pituitary. So, it is secreted in male and female both. In male, it stimulates spermatogenesis and development of seminiferous tubules and in female, it stimulates formation and growth of ovarian follicle in ovary.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following pituitary hormones works indirectly?
questions.solution: Among the given hormones, TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone) secreted by anterior lobe of pituitary gland, works indirectly. This hormone controls the growth and activity of thyroid gland. It influences the uptake of iodine, the synthesis of the hormones; thyroxine and tri-iodothyronine by the thyroid gland. Rest of the hormones, i.e. MSH (Melanocyte Stimulating Hormone), GH (Growth Hormone) and oxytocin work directly on their target organ. They do not stimulate any other gland.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Where is sacculus rotandus located?
questions.solution: Sacculus rotundus is located between ileum and caecum. The junction of ileum and large intestine is seen in the form of an orifice called ileocolic or ostium ileocaecal valve. This portion is well- developed in rabbit in the form of swollen portion called sacculus rotandus.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In gluconeogenesis, which of the following cannot act as substrate?
questions.solution: In gluconeogenesis, fructose cannot act as substrate because it is a metabolic process by which formation of glucose from noncarbohydrate carbon substrates such as amino acid (glycine), fatty acid (glycerol, palmitic acid) etc. takes place.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Peyer's patches are present in
questions.solution: Peyer's patches are present in ileum. They are organised lymphoid follicles. They form an important part of the immune system by monitoring intestinal bacteria populations. They prevent the growth of pathogenic bacteria in the intestine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What is the function of Kupffer's cells?
questions.solution: The main function of Kupffer's cells is phagocytosis. They are specialised macrophages located in the liver, lining the walls of the sinusoids. Kupffer's cell activation is responsible for early ethanol induced liver injury, common in chronic alcoholics.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Daily requirement of vitamin-A for adult women is
questions.solution: The recommended daily amount of vitamin-A for adult women is 700 micrograms. Vitamin-A is a nutrient important for good vision, growth, cell division, reproduction and immunity. It is found in spinach, dairy products, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Pyloric sphincter guards the opening between
questions.solution: Pyloric sphincter guards are the opening between the pylorus of stomach and the duodenum of small intestine. It periodically permits partially digested food to leave the stomach and enter the duodenum.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following hormone secretes a gastric secretion?
questions.solution: Gastrin hormone is secreted by gastric glands to release gastric juice. Enterogastrone is secreted by the duodenal epithelium which inhibits the secretion of gastric juice.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Pellagra is caused by deficiency of vitamins
questions.solution: Pellagra is caused by deficiency of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_5$. Therefore, vitamin- $B_5$ is also known as antipellagra factor. Symptoms of this disease are thick pigmented skin of head, swollen lips and patient feels irritable.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Space in the jaw bone unoccupied by teeth is called
questions.solution:
Diastema is the gap that separates the biing teeth from the grinding teeth in herbivores. It creates a space in which food can be held ready for the grinding action of the teeth.
This space is filled by large canine teeth in carnivores.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Identify the correct set, which shows the name of the enzyme from where it is secreted and substrate upon which it acts.
questions.solution:
Pepsin is released from peptic cells of the gastric gland on the stomach wall and it can digest milk protein caesin.
Chymotrypsin is released from the intestine and digest proteins. Ptyalin is released from salivary glands and acts on starch.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Oxyntic cells secrete
questions.solution: Oxyntic cells or parietal cells are large and rounded cells located upon the surface of gastric gland and secrete HCl and CIF (Castle's Intrinsic Factor). This HCl gives the gastric fluids, a pH of about 2.0.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Deficiency of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_{12}$ causes
questions.solution: The deficiency (hypovitaminosis) of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_{12}$ or cyanocobalamine causes pernicious anaemia, demyelination of nerve fibres and glossitis (inflammation of tongue).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The layer of cells that secrete enamel of tooth is
questions.solution: The pulp cavity contains a mass of dense but soft connective tissue which is called pulp. A single layer of odontoblast cells line the pulp cavity. These cells secrete enamel, which is a bluish-white shiny translucent and the hardest substance of the body

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The contraction of gall bladder is due to
questions.solution: Cholecystokinin (also called pancreozymin) is a hormone of mucosa of small intestine. It is released in response to chyme. It causes pancreas to release pancreatic enzymes and gall bladder to eject bile.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is mismatched?
questions.solution:
Beri-beri is caused by the deficiency of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_1$ (thiamine).
Vitamin- K is also known as antihaemorrhagic factor. The main sources of vitamin-K are green leafy vegetables such as cauliflower, cabbage, spinach, etc. It is also found in animal sources like egg yolk, liver, etc. Vitamin-K is essential for blood clotting and deficiency of it causes haemorrhage.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is the largest gland in an adult man ?
questions.solution: Liver is endodermal in origin and is the largest gland in human body. It is the busiest and largest chemical factory in the body.Liver cells secrete bile directly into bile capillaries, which is used in the emulsification and absorption of fats. Besides, it also functions in manufacture of anticoagulant heparin, plasma protein like albumin, fibrinogen and prothrombin and in deamination of proteins.Thyroid gland (measures 3-7 cm in length and 25 gm in weight in adults) a median endocrine gland located below larynx is the largest endocrine gland in the body. Thyroxine $\left(\mathrm{T}_4\right)$, produced by thyroid gland, function mainly in controlling metabolism, ie, basal metabolic rate of the body.Pancreas is a mixed gland (heterocrine gland) with both exocrine and endocrine portions. The average length of pancreas is $12-15 \mathrm{~cm}$ and weight is $50-70 \mathrm{gm}$. Insulin, glucagon, somatostatin are the hormones secreted by different cells of endocrine portion of pancreas.Thymus gland (at birth weights 10-12 gm, at puberty $20-30 \mathrm{gm}$ and at old age it weighs 3-6 gm ) is mainly concerned with immunological functions but becomes inconspicuous after puberty.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following vitamins is water soluble as well as an antioxidant?

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Kupffer cells are present in
questions.solution: Kupffer cells are the phagocytic cells present over the lining of 'sinusoids' (spaces between the hepatic cords) in liver. These cells destroy wan-out white and red blood cells and bacteria.Small intestine possess paneth cells and Argentaffin cells secreting digestive enzymes. These cells are present on simple tubular pits of intestinal glands known as 'crypts of Lieberkuhn'.Pancreas possess groups of cells, islets of Langerhans which contain four type of cells : Alpha cells ( $32-38 \%)$, Beta cells ( $60-70 \%$ ), Delta and F-cells ( $2 \%-8 \%$ )Thyroid gland possess spherical sacs called thyroid follicles. Wall of follicle is made of two types of cells,Principal cells andParafollicular cells.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched?
questions.solution: Pellagra is caused by the deficiency of Vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_3$ (niacin), while vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_2$ (riboflavin) deficiency cause dermatitis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Length of oesophagus is
questions.solution: The length of oesophagus is nearly 25 cm . Oesophagus is a hollow muscular tube found in vertebrates through which food is transfered from pharynx to stomach. It is about 25 centimetres long in adults. Peristaltic contractions of oesophageal muscle facilitate the passage of food through it.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
rearrange the parts P, Q, R and S to make a proper sentence.
It has been established that
(P) Einstein was
(Q) although a great scientist
(R) weak in arithmetic
(S) right from his school days
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Epithelial cells of the intestine involved in food absorption have on their surface
questions.solution: The mucosa and sub-mucosa of small intestine are thrown into folds. Surfaces of these folds is covered by fine, fingerlike projections of the epithelium. These projections are called villi. In addition, the epithelial cells of the villi are covered on their exposed surface by cytoplasmic projections called microvilli.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Which group of three of the following five statements (A-E) contain is all three correct statements regarding beri-beri?
A. A crippling disease prevalent among the native population of sub-Sahara Africa.
B. A deficiency disease caused by lack of thiamine (vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_1$ ).
C. A nutritional disorder in infants and young children when the diet is persistently deficient in essential protein.
D. Occurs in those countries where the staple diet is polished rice.
E. The symptoms are pain from neuritis, paralysis, muscle wasting, progressive oedema, mental deterioration and finally heart failure.
questions.solution: The deficiency of vitamin- $B_1$ or thiamine is responsible for the disease beri-beri. This disease occurs in those countries where the staple diet is polished rice. The symptoms of this disease are pain from neuritis, paralysis, muscle wasting, progressive oedema, mental deterioration and finally heart failure.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: 'Digestion' word means :
questions.solution: Digestion is a process in which non-diffusible foods are converted into diffusible foods with the help of digestive enzymes. Digestion is mostly carried out by the process of hydrolysis using water molecules for the cleavage. The enzymes which bring about digestion are called hydrolases.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Maximum absorption of water occurs in :
questions.solution: The chief functions of the large intestine are the absorption of water and the elimination of solid wastes. Large intestine is divided into three parts i.e., caecum, colon and rectum. In mammals, colon has essential water absorbing role in preparation of faeces.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: During starvation what will be sequence of ending of food stuffs ?
questions.solution:
During starvation reserve carbohydrate is used up firstly by the body and after carbohydrates, fat is used as energy source and at last, when both carbohydrate and fat are absent, proteins are used as energy source. Therefore, the sequence is
Carbohydrate $\rightarrow$ Fat $\rightarrow$ Protein

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Due to alcohol abuse brain related disease Korsakoff's syndrome occurs. Which nutrient is responsible for this syndrome?
questions.solution: Alcohol interferes with the metabolism of thiamine in the liver. Thus, alcohol intake with deficiency of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_1$ is the basis of brain related diseases called Wernicke's syndrome and Korsakoff's syndrome. Both the syndromes are associated with the destruction of cells in brain with the memory loss even of recent events and mental confusion along with the impairment of speech.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Secretion of gastric juice is stimulated by
questions.solution: The flow of gastric juice is regulated partly by a nervous mechanism and partly by the action of the hormone gastrin, which is produced in the mucosa cells of the stomach. It induces the secretion of gastric juice by gastric glands.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Pepsin is inactivated at pH
questions.solution: Pepsin is inactivated at pH above 5 . Pepsin is a proteolytic or protein splitting enzyme. The pH optimum of pepsin is about 2 and the enzyme, therefore, works well in the acidic gastric juice while it is inactivated in the basic medium.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_{12}$ is absorbed primarily in the
questions.solution: The parietal cells of gastric mucosa secrete certain intrinsic factors. This help in the absorption of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_{12}$. It is propelled along the small intestine to the terminal ileum and is absorbed by an active transport process.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Pellagra is caused by deficiency of vitamin
questions.solution: Pellagra is caused by deficiency of vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_5$. Therefore, vitamin- $\mathrm{B}_5$ is also known as antipellagra factor. The symptoms of this disease are swollen lips, thick pigmented skin of hands and legs and patient feels irritable.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Deoxyribonuclease and ribonuclease are secreted by
questions.solution: Deoxyribonuclease and ribonuclease are secreted by pancreas. These enzymes act on DNA and RNA and convert them to deoxyribonucleotides and ribonucleotides, respectively. The optimum pH required by pancreatic juice to secrete these enzymes is $7-8$ (alkaline).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Disease caused by deficiency of protein in children is
questions.solution: Kwashiorkor is caused by the deficiency of proteins in diet. This disease occurs in children of age group 1 to 5 years. Its symptoms are oedema (swelling of abdomen), thining of limbs, diarrhoea and retarded brain.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Bile helps in the digestion of fat through
questions.solution: Bile contains no digestive enzymes, yet it plays an important role in fat digestion. It intensifies the 'mixing contractions' of intestinal wall. The organic salts by bile reduce the surface tension of fats globules and suspend them in water of digestive juice. It is emulsification of fats. It facilitates easy action of pancreatic juice on fats. They also help in formation of micelles which help to transport of all and products of fat digestion from intestinal lumen to absorptive cells. The inorganic bile salts import alkalinity to chyme and inactivate gastric pepsin.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following represents the dental formula of man?
questions.solution: The number and kinds of teeth in a species of mammals can be represented by a sort of equation, which is called dental formula. The dental formula of $\operatorname{man}$ is $\frac{2 \cdot 1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3}{2 \cdot 1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3}=32$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one is antioxidant vitamin?
questions.solution: Three vitamins $\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{E}$ and $\beta$-carotene (provitamin) are called antioxidant vitamin. Because they inactivate oxygen free radicals. Free radicals are highly reactive particles that carry an unpaired electron $\left[e^{-}\right]$, they damage cell membranes, DNA, other cellular structures and contribute to formation of artery narrowings and atherosclerosis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The true stomach in ruminants where most of digestion takes place is
questions.solution:
In ruminants (e.g., cow, goat and camel) the stomach is 4 chambered as follows
(1) Rumen (cellulose is digested)
(2) Reticulum (cellulose is digested)
(3) Omasum (absorb water)
(4) Abomasum (true stomach)

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A patient is generally advised to specially, consume more meat, lentils, milk and eggs in diet only when he suffers from
questions.solution: The deficiency of proteins within the body is responsible for a disease, known as kwashiorkor. So, a kwashiorkor diseased patient is generally advised to specially, consume more meat, lentils, milk and eggs because these are rich sources of protein.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In the wall of alimentary canal, what is the actual sequence from outer to inner
options: serosa, long muscle, circular, submucosa, mucosa
questions.solution: The wall of alimentary canal is made up of four basic layers. Ist of all there is serosa which is a thin membranous covering around oesophagus. Then there is muscle layer-outer layer of longitudinal muscles and inner layer of circular muscles. Last there is submucosa, which contains a few oesophageal glands. Then comes mucosa, which have several layers of flattened cells.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Brunner's gland is located in
questions.solution: The Brunner's glands are located in duodenum only. They secrete a little enzyme and mucus. The mucus protects the duodenal wall from getting digested. The Brunner's glands open into the crypts of Lieberkuhn.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Following are the two statements regarding the origin of life.
I. The earliest organisms that appeared on the earth were non-green and presumably an aerobes.
II. The first autotrophic organisms were the chemoautotrophs that never released oxygen of the above statements which on of the following options is correct?
options: II is correct, but I is false
Both I and II are correct
Both I and II are false
I is correct, but II is false
questions.solution:
The earliest organisms that appeared on earth were anaerobic chemoautotrophs.
Chemoautotrophs were the first autotrophic organisms. They were unable to perform photolysis of water and never released oxygen, e.g. sulphur bacteria.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Variation in gene frequencies within population can occur by chance rather than by natural selection. This is reffered to as
questions.solution: Variation in gene frequencies within populations can occur by chance and is called as genetic drift.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following graphs correctly describes disruptive selection? When studying fitness level associated with body size?
questions.solution: Disruptive selection occurs when an environmental change may produce selection pressure that favours two extreme characteristics. Selection acts on the phenotype, physical, biochemical and behavioural traits of an organism. It indirectly adapts a population to its environment by selecting and maintaining favourable genotypes in their give pool.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The best description of natural selection is
options: the reproductive success of the members of a population best adapted to environment
it acts when the resources are unlimited
a change in the proportion of variations within a population
questions.solution: Natural selection is show by the reproductive success of the members of a population best adapted to the environment. The organisms which adapt useful variation successfully survive in changing environment and reproduce.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Coacervates were discovered by
questions.solution:
Coacervates were discovered by Oparin. They are large organic molecules synthesised abiotically on primitive earth.
They were the precursor of colloidal aggregates formed as a result of intermolecular attraction. Their presence was experimentally proved by Syduly Fox and Oparin.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The term 'prebiotic soup' for organic water containing mixture of simple organic compounds was given by
questions.solution: The term 'prebiotic soup' is used by Haldane for organic water containing mixture of simple organic compounds during early stage of origin of life.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is the most primitive ancestor of man?
questions.solution: Ramapithecus was the most primitive hominid. It is fossils were obtained from Asia and Africa. It's features suggested that they were the beginning of the linkages leading to human.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following human ancestors is known as tool maker?
questions.solution: Homo habilis was also known as tool maker because it was the first human who made weapons and tools from rocks.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: During which geological period of evolution did the greatest diversification of life occurred on the earth?
questions.solution:
The Cambrian explosion in Cambrian period marks the occurrence of greatest diversification on the earth.
Most major phyla appeared (indicated by fossil study), divergence of modern Metazoan phyla occurred and major diversification of other organisms.
Before this period, most organisms were simple however, this changed after Cambrian period, diversification rate accelerated and the diversity of life began to resemble that of today.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: About how long ago was the earth formed?
questions.solution: On the basis of geological, chemical and physical data, the scientist have come to the conclusion that solar system came into being about 4.57 billion years ago.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The most recent and direct pre-historic ancestor is
questions.solution: The cro-magnon man (Homo sapiens fossalis) is the most recent and direct prehistoric ancestor of present man. It arose about $3,40,000$ years ago.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A connecting link between plant and animal kingdom is
questions.solution: Euglena can be considered as both plants as well as an animal. It can perform photosynthesis in presence of light, but in the absence of light it opts holozoic mode of nutrition, which is the feature of animals. Thus, Euglena is considered as connecting link between plant and animal kingdom.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Darwins finches represent
questions.solution: There are thirteen types of finches described by Darwin, they are geographically isolated and found in galapagos islands of South Pacific.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium is known to be affected by gene flow, genetic drift, mutation, genetic recombination and
questions.solution:
- The Hardy-Weinberg principle states that allele and genotype frequencies in a population will remain constant from generation to generation in the absence of other evolutionary influences.
- When mating is random in a large population with no disruptive circumstances, both genotype and allele frequencies will remain constant because they are in equilibrium.
- Any factor, which can disturb the alleles in a population is likely to affect the Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium.
- The alleles in a population can be altered by mutation, migration, genetic drift, recombination, and natural selection. Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium is not effected by evolution, limiting factors, saltation and over prediction
So, the correct answer is 'Natural selection'.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Darwinism explains all the following except
options: within each species, there are variations
organisms tend to produce more number of offspring that can survive
offspring with better traits that overcome competition are best suited for the environment
variations are inherited from parents to offspring through genes
questions.solution: Variation is one of the main postulates of Darwinism. Darwin recognised two types of variations-continous and dis-continous variation, but he could not explain the inheritance of variations.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Theory of pangenesis was given by
questions.solution: Darwin proposed the theory of pangenesis to explain the inheritance of characteristics from parents to offsprings. According to this theory every somatic cells produces gemmules and the actual germ cells are the sites of collection of gemmules coming from different somatic cells.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following in birds, indicates their reptilian ancestry?
options: Two special chambers crop and gizzard in their digestive tract
questions.solution: Reptiles too are egg-laying organisms. But in aves, the eggs have a calcareous shell around them. In aves, the heart is four-chambered, while in reptiles the heart is 3 chambered and is incompletely divided to form a four-chambered heart. They have two special chambers called crop and gizzard in their digestive tract. Gizzard is modified for grinding food and crop for storing food on a temporary basis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Ancestor of man who first stood erect was
questions.solution: Australopithecus are considered as connecting link between ape and man. They were the ancestors of man who first stood erect. Their cranial capacity was 300-500 cc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The most primitive vertebrates are
questions.solution: Class-Ostracodermi of vertebrata includes most primitive vertebrates, e.g.,  Cephalaspis and Drepanaspis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Change in the number of body parts is called
questions.solution: Meristic variations involve any change in the number of body parts. In other words, it may be defined as the numerical variation in the taxonomic characters, e.g., number of spots, vertebrae, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Appearance of antibiotic resistant bacteria is an example of
questions.solution:
When a bacterial population encounters a particular antibiotic, those sensitive to it die. But some bacteria having mutations become resistant to antibiotics. Such resistant bacteria survive and multiply quickly as the competing bacteria have died. Soon the resistance providing genes become wide spread and entire bacterial population become resistant.
Appearence of antibiotic resistant bacteria is example of pre-existing variation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Origin of life occurred in
questions.solution: The first living form is named as protocell or eobiont or protobiont, which evolved into prokaryotic cell. These were originated about 3900-3500 million years ago, during Precambrian era.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Match the following Columns

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one is the oldest in following?
questions.solution:
Bryophytes are the oldest (most primitive) plant type in terms of evolution.
These are the simplest and first among land inhabiting plants occupying an intermediate, position between algae and pteridophyta. They are non- vascular, terristrial, autotrophic, non-flowering and seedless plants.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Darwin's finches are an excellent example of
questions.solution:
Adaptive radiation represents evolution of new forms in several directions from the common ancestral type. In 1831, Darwin got an opportunity to travel by HMS Beagle for a voyage of world exploration. Beagle sailed to the Galapagos Islands, here Darwin found a living laboratory of evolution. The common birds of Galapagos Islands, the finches were markedly different from the finches of main land. The closely related species of finches had beaks of different shapes and sizes and adapted for feeding on completely different diet showing adaptive radiation.
The transitional fossil forms, which show characteristic of two different groups of living animals are called connecting links, e.g.,  Archaeopteryx, Seymouria, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Theory of continuity of germplasm was propounded by
questions.solution: Theory of continuity of germplasm was proposed by August Weismann (1892), a German Biologist, he suggested that the changes occurring in germplasm are inherited by offsprings, whereas in somatoplasm are not transmitted to next generation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The diversity in the type of beaks of finches adapted to different feeding habits on the Galapagos islands, as observed by Darwin, provides evidence for
options: origin of species by natural selection
questions.solution: Birds of Galapagos islands (Darwin's finches) are believed to have evolved from ancestors on the South American mainland as a result of natural selection due to different feeding niches available to them.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Human ancestors who left cave paintings were

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Connecting link between annelids and molluscs is
questions.solution: Neopilina (mollusc) is considered as a connecting link between Annelida and Mollusca.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The presence of diversity at the junction of territories to two different habitats is known as
questions.solution: When well developed ecotonal communities may contain organisms characteristic of the overlapping communities plus species living only in the ecotone region, this condition is known as edge effect. The yearly or seasonal phenomenon of cyclic fluctuation in population density causing periodic squeezing of some of the genes in a gene pool in random fashion is called bottleneck phenomenon. Pasteur effect is reduction in the amount of breakdown of respiratory substrate and evolution of carbon dioxide when an anaerobically respiring material is brought in oxygen containing environment.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following provides most evident proof of evolution?
questions.solution: Fossils provide the direct evidences of organic evolution. Fossils may be entire organisms buried in sediment or snow, small part of ancient organisms or impression of extinct organisms, ancient leaf or stem.Morphology is the study of form and structure in organisms. Comparative study of homologous and analogous organs provide evidence of evolution under functional anatomy (tectology). Comparativeembryology (study of embryo) provides evidence of organic evolution which is based on basic laws or principles of embryonic development (the biogenetic law or recapitulation theory).Vestigial organs are degenerate, non-functional and rudimentary organs correspond to fully developed and functional organs of related organisms. They also afford to provide evidences for organic evolution. But, among them most evident and direct proof of evolution is provided by fossils.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not vestigial in man?
questions.solution: Vestigial organs are imperfectly developed, non-functional organs which were fully developed and functional in related and ancestral forms Example : Nictitating membrane, tail vertebrae, vermiform appendix.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Biological concept of species is mainly based on
questions.solution: A species can be defined as a group of closely related organisms which are capable of inbreeding to produce fertile offspring. Thus biological concept of species is mainly based on reproductive isolation, which preserve the integrity of the species by checking hybridization.

questions.q:
rearrange the parts P, Q, R and S to make a proper sentence.
The statement
(P) therefore you must listen carefully
(Q) what the speaker has said
(R) in order to understand
(S) will be made just once

choose the alternative which is opposite in meaning to the word given in italics in the sentence.
His writings have been much  < i>eulogised < /i> by Indian scholars.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions given below it. Journalism combines writing with news gathering and interpretation. While the journalist's work obviously varies from newspaper to newspaper and from magazine to magazine, all journalists are as much research workers, as they are writers. They cannot write their news or feature stories, unless they locate it and research them first. They must be able to read the in-between lines of the main source-news and interpret that. A great many stories hunt the journalist, who rejects most of them. He carefully sorts and sifts those, taking only a relatively very small proportion of news, may be, coming from an unexpected source. A successful journalist may gather news to the tune of 100 percent but he can use them for his profession only 3-5 percent. He must be able to see or forecast to himself, the news of tomorrow or the day after, from the news of today; because newspapers want advance news or advance warning to give a good, exclusive and exhaustive coverage to anything of interest. But a real 'scoop' is a very rare event. And bogus scoops also bring disrepute to the newspaper.Question: According to the passage, a journalist's work includes
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions given below it. Journalism combines writing with news gathering and interpretation. While the journalist's work obviously varies from newspaper to newspaper and from magazine to magazine, all journalists are as much research workers, as they are writers. They cannot write their news or feature stories, unless they locate it and research them first. They must be able to read the in-between lines of the main source-news and interpret that. A great many stories hunt the journalist, who rejects most of them. He carefully sorts and sifts those, taking only a relatively very small proportion of news, may be, coming from an unexpected source. A successful journalist may gather news to the tune of 100 percent but he can use them for his profession only 3-5 percent. He must be able to see or forecast to himself, the news of tomorrow or the day after, from the news of today; because newspapers want advance news or advance warning to give a good, exclusive and exhaustive coverage to anything of interest. But a real 'scoop' is a very rare event. And bogus scoops also bring disrepute to the newspaper.Question: What is the basic requisite for exclusive and exhaustive coverage of newspapers?
options: Forecast of tomorrow's news from today's news
Collecting the news of a 'real scoop'
Selection of a small portion of the gathered news
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: There are two opposing views about origin of modern man. According to one view Homo erectus in Asia were the ancestors of modern man. A study of variations of DNA however suggested African origin of modern man. What kind of observation on DNA variation could suggest this?
options: Greater variation in Africa than in Asia
Variation only in Asia and no variation in Africa
Greater variation in Asia than in Africa
Similar variation in Africa and Asia
questions.solution:
There are two models about origin of modern man (Homo sapiens sapiens) i.e.
(i) Multiregional model : According to this view, Modern humans evolved in many parts of the world from regional descendants of Homo erectus who dispersed from Africa between 1 and 2 million years ago.
(ii) Monogenesis model: According to this view only African descendents of Homo erectus gave rise to modern humans. In late 1980s Rebecca Cann and other geneticists supported this view on the basis of DNA of living humans. They compared the mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) of a multiathenic sample of more than 100 people representing four continents. The greater the difference between the mtDNAs of two peoples, the longer ago that mtDNAs diverged from a common source. By using bioinformatics, they concluded that the divergence of $m t$ DNA of Africans from common source began just 200,000 years ago, much to late to represent the dispersal of Homo erectus. Thus there are greater variation in Asia than in Africa.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following phenomena supports Darwin's concept of natural selection in organic evolution?
options: Production of ‘Dolly’ the sheep by cloning
Development of organs from ‘stem cells’ for organ transplantation
questions.solution: According to Darwin's concept of natural selection, the organisms which are provided with favourable variations would survive because, they are the fittest to face their surrounding while the organisms, which are unfit for surrounding variations are destroyed. Prevalence of pesticide resistant insects is due to adaptability of these insects for the changes in environment (due to use of pesticides).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: de Vries gave his mutation theory on organic evolution while working on
questions.solution: Hugo de Vries (1848-1935) proposed mutation theory for the formation of new species. According to him, new species are not formed by continuous variations but by sudden appearance of variations which he assigned as mutations. He stated that these mutations are heritable and persist in successive generations. For proposing this theory he performed experiments on Oenothera lamarckiana (evening primrose).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not true for a species?
options: Members of a species can interbreed
Variations occurs among members of a species
Each species is reproductively isolated from every other species
Gene flow does not occur between the populations of a species
questions.solution: Gene flow means the spread of genes through populations as affected by movements of individual and their propagules e.g., spores, seeds etc. Gene flow ensures that all populations of a given species share a common gene pool i.e., it reduces difference between populations. The interruption of gene flow between populations is a pre-requisite for the formation of new species.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Using imprints from a plate with complete medium and carrying bacterial colonies, you can select streptomycin resistant mutants and prove that such mutations do not originate as adaptation. These imprints need to be used
options: on plates with and without streptomycin
questions.solution: Plates having streptomycin allow to propagate only those bacteria, which are resistant to the antibiotic.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following experiments suggests that simplest living organisms could not have originated spontaneously from non-living matter?
options: Microbes did not appear in stored meat
Larvae could appear in decaying organic matter
Microbes appeared from unsterilized organic matter
Meat was not spoiled, when heated and kept sealed in a vessel
questions.solution:
Francesco Redi, an Italian physician, took the flesh and cooked it so that no organisms were left alive. Then he placed flesh in three jars, of which, one was uncovered, the second was covered with parchment and the third one was covered with fine muslin. He kept these jar for a few days and observed that maggots developed only in the uncovered jar through the flies also visited other jars.
So, the correct answer is 'Meat was not spoiled, when heated and kept sealed in a vessel'.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which have not left any evidences of organic evolution?
questions.solution:
The organisms which possess the characters of two different groups are called connecting links. They provide the evidence of organic evolution.
Examples :
(1) Archaeopteryx is connecting link between reptiles and birds.
(2) Neopilina is connecting link between Annelida and Mollusca.
(3) Peripatus is connecting link between Annelida and Arthropoda.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Set which includes only analogous organs is :
options: wings of butterfly, housefly and bat
hindlegs of horse, grasshopper and bat
wings of butterfly and wingspread of bat and birds
mandibles of cockroach, mosquito and honey bee
questions.solution:
Analogous organs have similar functions but are different in their structure and origin.
Examples :
(1) Wings of butterfly, birds and bat have similar functions.
(2) Stings of honey bee and scorpion are analogous structures.
(3) Fins of fishes and flippers of whales are analogous organs.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Organs which differ in origin but perform similar functions :
questions.solution:
The organs which have similar function but are different in their structure and origin are called analogous organs.
The organs which have same fundamental structure and origin but function may be similar or different, are called homologous organs.
The organs which are present in reduced form and do not correspond to the fully developed functional organs to related animals are called vestigial organs.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: 'Convergent Evolution' is shown by :
questions.solution: Convergent evolution or adaptive convergence or parallel evolution is shown by analogous organs, whereas divergent evolution or evolutionary divergence or adaptive radiation are shown by homologous organs.
