questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Age of fishes is also known as
questions.solution: The Devonian Period is known as the age of fishes'. It is famous for the thousands of species of fish that developed in Devonian sea. The Devonian Period of Palaeozonic Era lasted from 417 million years ago to 354 million years ago.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Example of analogous organs is :
questions.solution: Wings of bird and insects are analogous organs.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Dinosaurs become extinct in
questions.solution: Dinosaurs become extinct in cretaceous period.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following is vestigeal organ of human?
questions.solution: Vestigeal organs are present in reduced form and are of no use to the animal in which they are present. Man has, 180 vestigeal organs. e.g., nictitating membrane, vermiform appendix, wisdom tooth, muscle of pinna and forhead, mammary glands in male etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Stanley Miller performed an experiment to prove the origin of life. They took gases $\mathrm{NH}_3$ and $\mathrm{H}_2$ along with
questions.solution: Stanley Miller using $\mathrm{CH}_4: \mathrm{NH}_3: \mathrm{H}_2$ gases in the ratio of $2: 2: 1$ and water vapours synthesised many simple organic compounds including amino acids, glycine, alanine and aspartic acid. According to him life originated from $\mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{CH}_4, \mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following is not a vestigial structural in Homo sapiens?
questions.solution:
Vestigial organs are degenerate, non-functional and rudimentary organs correspond to fully developed and functional organs of related organism. Third molar (wisdom teeth), plica semilunaris and pyramidalis muscles are vestigial organs.
Epiglottis is not a vestigial organ in man. Epiglottis is the structure that prevents the entry of food into respiratory tract during swallowing in man.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which compound has very important role in prebiotic evolution?
questions.solution: JBS Haldane (1920) used the term"Prebiotic soup" or"hot dilute soup of organic substances" for oceanic water containing mixture of simple organic compounds. Methane $\left(\mathrm{CH}_4\right)$ was probably the first organic compound and hydrogen cyanide was formed later.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Carcinogen present in cigarette smoke is
questions.solution: Benzopyrene is a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon formed as a result of incomplete combustion of organic matter such as coal tar, tobacco, etc. It is a potential carcinogenic compound listed as Group 1 carcinogen by the IARC International Agency for Research on Cancer. It reacts and binds to DNA, resulting in mutation and eventually leads to cancer.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Note the following features and choose the ones applicable to Wuchereria bancrofti.
I. Coelozoic parasite
II. Histozoic parasite
III. Monogenetic parasite
IV. Digenetic parasite
V. Monomorphic, acoelomate parasite
VI. Dimorphic, pseudocoelomate parasite
questions.solution: Statements II, IV, VI are correct Wuchereria bancrofti commonly called filarial worm is a digenetic, dimorphic, pseudocoelomate, histozoic and pathogenic parasite. It is digenetic because it has two stages of multiplication in two different hosts, one sexual in the mature form, the other asexual in larval stages. It is a histozoic parasite as it lives in the tissue outside of a cell body.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one is an anti-allergic antibody?
questions.solution: IgE is an anti-allergic antibody. It plays a pivotal role in response to allergens such as anaphylactic drugs, bee stings, etc. They are synthesised by plasma cells. It is found in only mammals.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Choose the correct regarding antibodies.
questions.solution:
IgG antibody can cross placenta and provide natural passive immunity to the developing foetus. Other options are corrected as follows IgA is a dimer found in body secretions such as sweat, tears, saliva, mucus, colostrum, etc. It provide localised protections from pathogens.
IgE is involved in hypersensitive reactions and are present in mast cells and basophil cells.
IgM is found in pentamer form not as dimer.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Traditional anesthesia is obtained from
questions.solution: Traditionally, erythroxylum (cocaine) was used to obtain anesthesia. It has an anesthetic and analgesic property which can alleviate the pain of headache, rheumatism, wounds and sores, etc. It was also used for broken bones, childbirth and during performing trepanning operations on the skull.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which type of immunoglobin is/are abundantly found in foetus?
questions.solution: IgG is the most abundant antibody in foetus as it is the only one which can cross placental barrier from mother to reach child and provides immune protection to it.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Monoclonal antibodies and polyclonal antibodies are produced by
questions.solution: Antibodies are produced by B-lymphocytes. Monoclonal and polyclonal antibodies are particularly produced by plasma cells of B-lymphocytes when combined with myleoma cells on specific medium. These cells play a key role in specific immunological reactions.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Who received Nobel Prize in 2008 for the discovery of HIV?
questions.solution: Luc Montagnier received Nobel Prize in 2008 for the discovery of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) which is the cause for AIDS.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following is not correctly matched?
questions.solution: Plague (black death) is caused by Pasteurella bacteria. Diphtheria is caused by Corynebacterium diphtheriae bacteria and tetanus (lockjaw) is caused by Clostridium tetani bacteria, while filariasis (elephantiasis) is caused by Wuchereria bancrofti.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following antibodies plays an important role as mediator in allergic response?
questions.solution: Antibody $\lg E$ acts as mediator in allergic response.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Source of commercial chewing gum latex is
questions.solution:
The latex of some plants is commercially important such as
(i) Source of commercial rubber is latex of Hevea brasiliensis, Ficus elastica, Cryptostegia, Manihot glaziovii.
(ii) Source of chewing or chickle gum is latex of Achras sapota.
(iii) Source of enzyme papain is latex of Carica рарауа.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following is a matching pair of vector and the disease?
questions.solution: Culex mosquito is responsible for carrying pathogen of filariasis as vector. Housefly is vector for amoebiasis, typhoid, etc. Aedes aegypti mosquito is vector for dengue virus. Sandfly is vector for kala azar.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following is not correct pair of type of cancer and origin place?
questions.solution: Sarcomas cancer originate in lymphatic tissues, connective tissues, muscles and bones.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Diphtheria is caused by
options: poison released by living bacterial cell into the host
poison released from dead bacterial cell into the host
poison released by virus into the host
excessive immune response by the body of host
questions.solution: Diphtheria is caused by poison released by living bacterial cell of Corynebacterium diphtheriae into host body.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not correctly matched?
questions.solution:
Trichomonas vaginalis is a flagellate found in vagina of female (humans) causing vaginalis. It leads to burning sensation, itching and frothy vaginal discharge.
Leishmaniasis or kala-azar is caused by Leishmania donovani. It is characterised by the presence of cutaneous oriental roses along with fever.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following T-cells are destroyed by HIV?
questions.solution: HIV attaches to $\mathrm{CD}_4$ receptor site of helper T-cells by the help of GP-120 on the protein coat of the virus.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Continuous exposer to vinyl chloride may cause cancer of the
questions.solution: Vinyl chloride is used to make Poly Venyl Chloride (PVC) plastic and vinyl products. Long term exposer to vinyl chloride through inhalation and oral exposer in human result in liver damage or cancer.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following act as an antigen, but do not induce antibody production?
questions.solution: The hapten is a low weight molecular that can be made immunogenic conjugation to suitable carrier.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: An autoimmune disease is
questions.solution: Myasthenia gravis is an autoimmune disease characterised by the chronic fatigability and weakness of muscles, specially in the face and throat, as a result of defect in the conduction of nerve impulses at the myoneural junction.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The effect of cigarette smoking and radon in combination on lungs is
questions.solution: The effect of cigarette smoking and radon in combination on lungs is synergistic.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Typhoid is caused by
questions.solution:
Typhoid or Enteric fever is a bacteria borne disease caused by bacillus Salmonella typhii. It spreads through consumption of contaminated food and water. Main symptoms are high fever, headache, vomiting, loose stools, etc.
Pneumonia is caused by three different strains of bacterium named Streptococcus pneumoniae, Pneumococcus pneumoniae and Diplococcus pneumoniae. Filarial worm cause filarial disease which is also called elephantiasis. The scientific name of worm is Wuchereria bancroft.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Choose correct option for larva of housefly.
questions.solution: Larva of housefly is called maggot. Larva of certain beetles and other insects are called grub. Cypris is a larval form of class-Crustacea. Wriggler is a larval form of mosquitoes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is involved in passive immunity?
questions.solution: IgA antibody is involved in passive immunity. This type of immunity is produced by a donor other than the infected person or an animal. It is short lived. Human colostrum (Mother's first milk) is rich in IgA antibodies. It provides natural passive immunity to the infant till the age of three months.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Genetic material found in Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) is
questions.solution: Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) is the causal organism of AIDS (Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome). It is a retrovirus belonging to the family-Retroviridae. The core of HIV contains two molecules of single stranded RNA (as genetic material) and reverse transcriptase.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Cells obtained from cancerous tumours are known as
questions.solution: Myeloma is a type of cancer that develops from plasma cells occurring in the bone marrow. It may develop, wherever the plasma cells occur.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Addiction to alcohol causes
questions.solution: In alcoholic person, some part of alcohol is changed into acetaldehyde, which stimulates the formation of fat. This fat is deposited over the wall of arteries and also accumulates in liver cells. Due to which liver gets failed and there is very little regulation of carbohydrate, protein and fat metabolism. This disorder is known as fatty liver syndrome. Later on, the liver gets turned into solid, hard, fibrous organ causing liver cirrhosis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: AIDS virus cotnains
questions.solution: AIDS is caused by Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV). It is a lentivirus belonging to the family-Retroviridae. The core of this virus contains two single strands of RNA molecules (genetic material) proteins and the enzyme reverse transcriptase.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Sir Godfrey Hounsfield developed the diagonostic technique of
questions.solution: Sir Godfrey Newbold Hounsfield, an English electrical engineer who shared th 1979 Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine with Allan MacLeod Cormak for his part in developing diagnostic technique of X-ray computed tomogrpahy (CT) scanning. He died in August 2004.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Humoral immunity is mediated by
questions.solution: Plasma cells plays a key role in Humoral Imunity (HMI). It activates secretion of various antibodies (B-cells) against specific antigens entering the body.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Sporogony of malarial parasite occurs in
questions.solution: Sporogony is a sexual phase starting with ookinete in stomach wall of mosquito to form haploid sporozoites in oocyst. Sporozoites enter in salivary glands of mosquito. When mosquito sucks human blood, it injects sporozoites along with saliva containing anticoagulant.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Alcoholism may leads to
questions.solution:
Alcoholism is a chronic, often progressive disease in which a person craves alcohol and drinks despite repeated alcohol related problems. It involves a physical dependence on alcohol. It may lead to liver cirrhosis. Cirrhosis is build up of scar tissue that changes the structure of the liver and blocks blood flow.
Cirrhosis can cause varicose veins, which can rupture and potentially triggering internal bleeding.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The intermediate host of Schistosoma is
questions.solution: Schistosoma mansoni is the common human blood fluke . It belongs to class-Trematoda of Platyhelminthes. Blood fluke is digenetic, primary host is man and secondary host is snail.Sheep is the primary host of Fasciola hepatica (sheep liver fluke), causing 'liver rot'. Its secondary host is also the snail. Mosquito and housefly do not found to be the intermediate host of any animal.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The idiom/phrase of To wash one's dirty linen in public
options: To criticise one's nature in public
To do some ugly work in public
To discuss dirty and scandalous matters of personal nature in the presence of strangers
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
choose the alternative which can be substituted for the given words/sentence.
Poem in short stanzas narrating a popular story
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Action potential is generated by
questions.solution: Action potential refers to the electrical potential difference across an axon membrane, that is stimulated (action). Action potential is generated by $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Damage to thymus in a child may lead to
options: a reduction in haemoglobin content of blood
a reduction in stem cell production
questions.solution: Thymus gland is found in the upper part of thorax near the heart. It is a bilobed pinkish gland. At the time of birth, it is a prominent gland but in the adult it gradually atrophies. This gland secretes thymosin hormone, thymic humoral factor, thymic factor and thymopoietin. Proliferation of lymphocytes and differentiation of these lymphocytes into a variety of clones are induced by these factors. These clones are differentially specialized to destroy different specific category of antigens and pathogens. Thus thymus gland brings fourth T-Lymphocytes for cell mediated immunity.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: AIDS is caused by HIV that principally infects
questions.solution: AIDS (Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome was described first time in 1981 (Africa). It is the result of an infection by the Human Immuno Deficiency Virus (HIV). It is a lentivirus within the family of Retroviride. Principal target cells for HIV are $\mathrm{T}_4$ lymphocytes because these cells have $\mathrm{CD}_4$ receptor protein for interaction of HIV.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following depresses brain activity and produces feelings of calmness, relaxation and drowsiness?
questions.solution: Valium depresses brain activity and produces feeling of calmness, relaxation and drowsiness.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Lathyrism is caused by :
questions.solution: Lathyrism is caused due to consumption of khesari dal (Lathyrus odoratus).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: X-rays are used in
questions.solution: $X$-rays are used in CT-scan (computed tomographic scanning). It uses $X$-rays but employs a computer for reconstructing the image instead of directly recording it on a photographic film. This technique is used in the diagnosis of diseases of brain, spinal cord, chest, etc. It is also useful in detecting tumors.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Abnormal growth of the tumor in cancer is due to
questions.solution: Sometimes certain cells, which have differentiated and stopped dividing, suddenly start multiplying in an uncontrolled way to form a malignant growth called tumor. All cells carry certain cancer producing oncogenes. Under certain conditions these oncogenes are triggered to form masses of cells (tumor).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Antibody is produced by
questions.solution: Antibody is produced by B-lymphocyte. Lymphocytes are critical for immune reactions and of two types B-lymphocytes and T-lymphocytes. B-lymphocytes, upon contact with appropriate antigens, are transformed into plasma cells, which are responsible for the production of antibody. They produce, 20 trillion antibodies per day.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Morphine, which is used as an analgesic is obtained from
questions.solution: Morphine is the principle opium alkaloid. It is a strong analgesic. Opium is dried latex of unripe capsular fruits of poppy plant, Papaver somniferum. It is eaten or smoked.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one has no intermediate host?
questions.solution: Ascaris lumbricoides (the common roundworm) has no intermediate host. Man acquires infection by directly ingesting Ascaris eggs having the infective second stage-rhabditoid larva, with contaminated food or water.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The larvicidial fish used in biocontrol of mosquitoes
questions.solution: Gambusia is an exotic fish feeds on the larvae of mosquito. Now-a-days it is widely used to eradicate mosquito.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The infective stage of Plasmodium is
questions.solution: Infective stage of Plasmodium is a sporozoite inoculated into human blood by female Anopheles. Sporozoite are produced due to gamogony (sexual cycle in the stomach of mosquito) followed by sporogony (asexual cycle in the stomach wall of mosquito). Sporozoites are sickle or spindle-shaped.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Peyer's patches produce
questions.solution: Peyer's patches are found in ileum and made up of lymph nodes. These are aggregates of lymphocytes where B cells form a central follicle and are surrounded by T-cells and macrophages which help the T-cells to recognise Antigen Mucosa Associated Lymphoid Tissue (MALT) is made up of Peyer's patches.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Active immunity means
questions.solution: Immunity is described as an 'active immunity' when an organism's own body manufactures its own antibodies. It may be natural (developing when organism is exposed to an infectious agent) or artificial (achieved by injecting small amount of antigen called vaccine into the body of the individual).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Vaccine for tuberculosis is known as
questions.solution: Baccillus Calmette Guerin (BCG) is the vaccine for tuberculosis. It was first used in 1921. BCG is the only vaccine available today for protection against tuberculosis. It is most effective in protecting children from the disease. BCG contains a live attenuated (weakened) strain of Mycobacterium bovis. This vaccine was developed by Calmette and Guerin at Pasteur Institute, Paris.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Retrovirus have genetic material
questions.solution: Retrovirus is an RNA virus that infects animal cells and replicates by first being converted to double stranded DNA, with the enzyme reverse transcriptase. Retroviruses are enveloped Viruses. The HIV III (Human Immunodeficiency virus), which causes AIDS, is a retrovirus.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the labelled part utilises fructose as a source of energy?
questions.solution:
The middle piece of the sperm utilises fructose as a source of energy. It is packed with numerous mitochondria which utilise fructose as a source of energy to provide motility to sperm to reach out ovum (egg cell)
Head is comprised of acrosome and nucleus. It has hyaluronidase enzyme in acrosome and genetic material in nucleus.
Tail it is comprised of tubulin proteins which provide motility to sperm cell.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Number of chromosomes present in secondary spermatocyte is
questions.solution:
The number of chromosomes present in secondary spermatocyte is 23 . It is because the secondary spermatocytes are formed after the lst meiotic division (reductional division) of diploid primary spermatocytes, hence they have haploid set of chromosomes,
i.e. 23 in count.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Number of chromosomes present in spermatogonium is
questions.solution:
The number of chromosomes present is spermatogonium is 46 .
The undifferentiated, germ cells present on the inside wall of seminiferous tubules of testes are called spermatogonium (or sperm mother cells). Each spermatogonium is diploid and contains 46 chromosomes ( $44+\mathrm{XY}$ ).
At sexual maturity, these cells divide several times by mitosis to produce a large number of spermatogonia, some of which serve as stem cells for spermatogenic lineage while other act as precursor of sperms and differentiate into primary spermatocyte.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one is present in the urine of pregnant woman?
questions.solution: hCG (human Chorionic Gonadotropin) is present in the urine of pregnant woman. It is secreted by placenta. It stimulates the corpus luteum that continues to secrete progesterone.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gastrulation means
options: a phase in which organogenesis takes places
a phase characterised by inner cell mass
questions.solution:
Gastrulation is a process by which blastocyst transforms into three layered (germ layers) structure called gastrula. These three germ layers are inner endoderm, middle mesoderm and outermost ectoderm. These three layers give rise to all tissues and organs of the developing foetus.
Conversion of morula into blastula is called blastulation. In this stage, inner cell mass is found inside the blastula. Organogenesis occur after gastrula stage.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Formation of corpus luteum is induced by
questions.solution: The formation of corpus luteum is induced by LH (Luteinising Hormone).The LH causes ovulation and remaining cells of the ovarian follicle develop corpus luteum. It secretes large amount of progesterone which is essential for the maintenance of uterine endometrium.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Fimbriae are associated with which organ?
questions.solution: Fimbriae are associated with Fallopian tubes. Each Fallopian tube is about $10-12 \mathrm{~cm}$ long and consists of four parts viz., infundibulum, ampulla, isthmus and the uterine part. Fimbriae are finger-like projections found on the ends of infundibulum. They help in the collection of ovum after ovulation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gastrula is the embryonic stage in which
questions.solution: Transformation of the blastocyst into gastrula with primary germ layers by rearrangement of the cells is called gastrulation. In all the triploblastic animals three germ layers-ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm are formed by characteristic morphogenetic movements.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What is acrosomal reaction?
options: Contact of acrosome and nucleus of egg
questions.solution: Acrosomal reaction is digestion of zona pellucida. This involves the progressive fusion of the acrosomal membrane with the plasma membrane of the sperm. It creates pores through which the acrosomal enzymes can be released by exocytosis. These enzymes include a protein digesting enzyme, corona penetrating enzyme, zona lysin (acrosin) and hyaluronidase. These enzymes allow the sperm to digest a path through the zona pellucida to the oocyte.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The secretory phase in the human menstrual cycle is also called as
options: luteal phase and last for about 6 days
follicular phase lasting for about 6 days
luteal phase and last for about 13 days
follicular phase and last for about 13 days
questions.solution: The first phase of human menstrual cycle is called proliferative phase (follicular phase), while second phase of human menstrual cycle is called as secretory phase or luteal phase and it lasts for about 13 days. Here, the LH is secreted by anterior lobe of pituitary leading to ovulation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gonads are derived from
questions.solution: Gonads are derived from the mesodermal germinal ridges of the embryo which contain mesodermal epithelium and large spherical primitive germs cells.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The number of chromosomes in a primary spermatocyte is
questions.solution: Both spermatogonium and primary spermatocyte in human testes are diploid cells (2n).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The portion of the endometrium that covers the embryo and is located between the embryo and the uterine cavity is the
questions.solution:
Desidua basalis is the portion of endometrium between chorion and stratum basalis of the uterus.
Desidua parietalis is the part of the endometrium not involved in the implantation.
Decidua umbilicus no such structure exists in endometrium.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In human during fertilisation a sperm firstly comes in contact with which layer of ovum
questions.solution: Zona radiata is cellular layer around a mature ovum. Sperm has to penetrate through this by using hyaluronidase enzyme to reach upto zone pellucide membrane, which is acellular.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Bartholin's gland is found in
questions.solution: Bartholin's gland is found in vagina. Bartholin glands are a pair of small glands, which occur on each side of the vaginal opening. These glands correspond to cowper's gland of male.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following statements best characterise the testis?
options: The seminiferous epithelium contains only proliferative cells
Functional compartmentalisation of the seminiferous epithelium depends on tight junctions
The interstitial tissue contains few capillaries
The seminiferous epithelium contains numerous capillaries
questions.solution: Each testis contains seminiferous tubules for spermatogenesis and interstitial (Intertubullar) tissue rich in Leyding cells for steroidgenesis. Seminiferous epithelium contains a continuous layer of Sertoli cells, which are joined by tight intercellular junctions. These junctions divide the epithelium into a basal compartment that contains spermatogonia and an abdominal compartment.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Drugs that cause malformation in developing embryo during pregancy are called
questions.solution: Teratogens is an agent that can cause malformations of an embryo on foetus. This can be a chemical substance, a virus or ionising radiation. Mostly, during first three months of pregnancy the pregnant lady should avoid all contact with teratogen.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Structure analogous to the placenta in mammals.
questions.solution: Chorion act as structure analogous to placenta in mammals. It completely encloses the developing embryo and extra embryonic tissues like placenta, it is also highly specialised to facilitate the transfer of nutrients, gases and waste between the embryo and mothers body. This is accomplised by chorionic villi that emerge from the chorion and invade the endometrium.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Hormone responsible for the secretion of milk after parturition is
questions.solution: Prolactin or luteotrophic hormone or luteotrophin is a hormone, synthesised and stored in the anterior pituitary gland, that stimulates milk production after child birth and also stimulates production of progesterone by the corpus luteum in the ovary.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The correct sequence is
questions.solution:
The correct sequence is
Zygote $\rightarrow$ Cleavage $\rightarrow$ Morula $\rightarrow$ Blastula $\rightarrow$ Gastrula.
At about $30-36 \mathrm{hrs}$ after fertilisation, the zygote divides into smaller cells by a process called cleavage. It is division of cells. It results in a solid ball of cells, called morula. It is a 16 -celled stage. It forms blastula which is a 64 -celled stage. It contains a fluid-filled cavity called blastocoel. It ultimately leads to gastrula stage. It is a multilayered structure which is followed by organogenesis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which hormone is secreted in a woman if pregnancy has occurred?
questions.solution: If fertilization occurs and the foetus is implanted in the endometrium, the trophoblast cells of the developing placenta secrete a hormone called Human Crionic Gonadtrophic hormone (HCG). This hormone like LH maintains the corpus luteum and secretion of progesterone and estradiol by it. These two hormone check the breakdown of the endometrium of the uterus. The absence of menstrual bleeding is the earliest sign of pregnancy.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Spermatogenesis is under the regulatory influence of
questions.solution: Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) is produced by basophilic cells of adenohypophysis of pituitary gland. In females, this hormone is responsible for the growth of ovarian follicles up to ovulation, while in males, its functions are development of seminiferous tubules and maintenance of spermatogenesis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Menstruation is due to sudden
questions.solution: The corpus luteum plays an important role in the preparation of endometrium for the implantation of fertilized egg by secreting estrogen and progesterone hormones. But if the egg is not fertilized then the corpus luteum begins to degenerate and it stops the production of progesterone and estrogen hormones, which causes shedding of the endometrium lining with menstrual bleeding.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which is regarded as urinary bladder of embryo?
questions.solution: Allantois is an extraembryonic membrane developed as an outgrowth from hindgut. In the eggs of reptiles and birds, it functions as a urinary bladder and stores the waste excretory products. It also provides oxygen (in reptiles, birds and mammals) and food (in mammals) to the embryo.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which extraembryonic membrane in humans prevents desiccation of the embryo inside the uterus?
questions.solution:
Amnion is an extraembryonic membrane that surrounds embryo in reptiles, birds and mammals. It provides a kind of private aquarium to the embryo and protects it from mechanical shock and desiccation.
Chorion (serosa) is the outermost extra embryonic membrane in reptiles, birds and mammals. It surrounds the whole embryonic system of embryo.
Yolk sac contains yolk in reptiles and birds. In mammals, yolk sac is also known as umbilical vesicle. It is connected to enteron of embryo by a slender yolk stalk.
Allantois functions as an embryonic urinary bladder or as a respiratory extension of hind gut in reptiles and birds. In mammals, it is modified to carry blood vessels to and from the placenta.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The middle piece of the sperm contains
questions.solution: The middle piece of human sperm contains mitochondria, which are coiled around an axial filament called as mitochondrial spiral. These provide energy for the movement of sperms.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The extra structure that provides nutrition to the embryo is
questions.solution: Placenta is a connection between foetus and mother formed from fusion of allantois, chorion and uterine endometrium. Functions of placenta : (i) Provides nourishment (ii) Helps in respiration (iii) Helps in excretion Amnion and chorion are foetal membranes. Amnion helps in protection and chorion helps in nourishment.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Pick the hormone which is not secreted by human placenta.
questions.solution: Prolactin is secreted by pituitary gland, whereas the hCG, hPL and oestrogen are secreted by human placenta.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The embryo at 16 celled stage is known as
questions.solution: The embryo at the 16 -celled stage is called the morula. It is the mass of cells resulting from the cleavage of the ovum before the formation of a blastula.Blastomere is any cells produced during cleavage. Blastula is the early development stage of a metazoan, following the morula stage and consisting of a single, spherical layer of cells that encloses a hollow, central cavity.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Our flight was......from Jaipur to Agra airport.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In rabbit, head of epididymis present at the head of the testis is called
questions.solution: A compact, some what flattened and whitish mass, called epididymis is closely abutted against the dorsal aspect of each testis. In rabbit head of epididymis present at the head of the testis is called caput epididymis. Epididymis differentiated into smaller posterior enlarged part called cauda epididymis. Testes of rabbits are about 15 to 20 mm long and 10 mm thick, oval and pinkish structures. Each is held in its position within its scrotal sac and is supported from posterior scrotal wall by means of a small cord of fibro-muscular mesodermal tissue called gubernaculum. From the tip of cauda epididymis, the Wolffian duct continues forwards as an uncoiled and some what thicker male genital duct orvas deferens.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions given below it. Journalism combines writing with news gathering and interpretation. While the journalist's work obviously varies from newspaper to newspaper and from magazine to magazine, all journalists are as much research workers, as they are writers. They cannot write their news or feature stories, unless they locate it and research them first. They must be able to read the in-between lines of the main source-news and interpret that. A great many stories hunt the journalist, who rejects most of them. He carefully sorts and sifts those, taking only a relatively very small proportion of news, may be, coming from an unexpected source. A successful journalist may gather news to the tune of 100 percent but he can use them for his profession only 3-5 percent. He must be able to see or forecast to himself, the news of tomorrow or the day after, from the news of today; because newspapers want advance news or advance warning to give a good, exclusive and exhaustive coverage to anything of interest. But a real 'scoop' is a very rare event. And bogus scoops also bring disrepute to the newspaper.Question: What, according to the passage, is the common characteristic of all journalists?
options: They all write for the newspapers
They all have to first probe into the news they gather
They all make an adventure to find a worthy news
They all can forecast future news
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Grey crescent is the area
options: at the point of entry of sperm into ovum
just opposite to the site of entry of sperm into ovum
questions.solution: The grey crescent area is the area of just opposite to the entry of sperm into ovum.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In case of pregnancy the heartbeat of embryo starts at
questions.solution: On the 5th week of pregnancy a large tube (premitive heart) starts to beat and pump blood in embryo.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: If mammalian ovum fails to get fertilized, which one of the following is unlikely?
questions.solution: If mammalian ovum fails to get fertilized, the oestrogen secretion does not decrease further while corpus luteum will disintegrate. Primary follicle starts developing and progesterone secretion rapidly declines.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: How many sperms are formed by four primary spermatocytes ?
questions.solution: Spermatogenesis is the formation of sperm from a germ cell. Four spermatozoa are produced from a primary spermatocyte, therefore, 16 spermatozoa will be formed from four primary spermatocytes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Acrosome is formed by :
questions.solution: Acrosome is the penetrating tip of spermatozoan which is formed of Golgi apparatus. It contains hyaluronidase and several lytic enzymes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Incomplete division of egg during cleavage is known as
questions.solution: In meroblastic cleavage, division occurs only in small amount of metabolically active cytoplasm, confined to the animal pole or peripheral region of egg. The yolk remains undivided. It is found in macrolecithal eggs. e.g. birds

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Progesterone is secreted by
questions.solution: Progesterone is a principle female sex hormone. It is a steroid. It is secreted during the later half of the menstrual cycle in human females by temporary endocrine tissue, the corpus luteum. The luteinizing hormone (LH) of anterior pituitary brings about a rupture of the Graafian folllicle to release the ovum and changes the ruptured follicle into a yellow structure called corpus luteum, which secretes progesterone.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The immediate cause of induction of ovulation in female is the large plasma surge of
questions.solution: Ovulation takes place on 14th day of cycle, concentration of LH is highest on 14th day because it has to break the Graafian follicle so as to release the ovum later if the concentration of estrogen and progesterone decrease there is lose of internal lining of uterus and menstural bleeding start.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: How many sperm cells are present in an average $(3 \mathrm{~mL}$ ) ejaculation?
questions.solution: Ejaculation or seminal emission is the forceful expulsion of semen during sexual intercourse. On an average ( 3 mL ) ejaculation of semen contains about 300 million spermatozoa.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Level of LH is maximum
questions.solution: The level of LH is maximum at the time of ovulation (i.e. ovulatory phase) in the menstrual cycle. It is called as LH surge. It induces rupture of Graafian follicles and thereby the release of ovum, i.e. ovulation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is non-symbiotic nitrogen fixer organism?
questions.solution: Anabaena, Nostoc and Aulosira are symbiotic Rhizobium $\mathrm{N}_2$-fixing microbes, while.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following can be controlled by using biopesticides?
questions.solution: Biopesticides are pesticides of biological origin, which may be of various types depending upon the types of pests killed or controlled by them, e.g., algicides, fungicides, bacteriocides, herbicides or weedicides, insecticides, nematicides and rodenticides, etc. These were initially employed to protect crop plants against pests but they are non-equally important for destroying or controlling vectors for various animals and human pathogens, thus, can be used for controlling various diseases also.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Streptomycin is obtained from
questions.solution:
Streptomycin is obtained from Streptomyces griseus.
Auromycin (tetracyclin) is obtained from Streptomyces aureofaciens.
Chloromycetin is obtained from Streptomyces venezuelae.
Terramycin is obtained from Streptomyces ramosus.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Besides dung, the weed that can be used in biogas production is
questions.solution: An aquatic weed-like water hyacinth is used as a source of biogas through harvesting, chopping, crushing and digesting.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following microbes is the source for vitamin-C?
questions.solution: Acetobacter sp. is a source of Vitamin-C.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Roquefort cheese is ripened by using a
questions.solution: 'Roquefort cheese' is a sheep milk blue cheese, which comes from the South of France. It is ripened by growing a specific fungi, i.e. Penicillium roqueforti which gives them a particular flavour.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Autonomic nervous system affects
questions.solution:
Autonomic nervous system controls and coordinates the involuntary activites of various internal organs. This system is divisible into two parts :
(a) Sympathetic nervous system
(b) Para-sympathetic nervous system.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In the treatment of waste water discharge, which treatment stage involves biological treatment?
questions.solution:
During waste water treatment :
(a) Primary treatment involves physical process of removing the suspended matters.
(b) Secondary treatment involves biological treatment.
(c) Tertiary treatment involves the removal of phosphate, nitrate and sulphate.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Lactic acid is produced by Rhizopus species :
questions.solution:
Rhizopus species causing fermentation are :
(i) R. oryzae - Alcoholic fermentation
(ii) R. nodosus - Lactic acid fermentation
(iii) R. stolonifer (R. nigricans) - Fumaric acid fermentation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What happened when we inoculated Rhizobium in wheat field?
options: No increase in production (nitrogen content of soil remains same)
A lot of increase in production (nitrogen content of soil increases)
questions.solution: Rhizobium species is symbiotic nitrogen-fixing bacteria, which occurs in root nodules of legumes. When we inoculated Rhizobium in wheat field, there is no increase in production. Because wheat has no root nodules for fixing nitrogen content. Therefore, nitrogen content of soil will remain same.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The non-photosynthetic, non-symbiotic $\mathrm{N}_2$ fixing bacteria are
questions.solution: Azotobacter, Beijerinkia are non photosynthetic, non symbiotic and aerobic nitrogen fixing bacteria.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gas gangrene is caused by
questions.solution: Gas gangrene is caused by Gram-positive, spore forming bacteria called Clostridium perfringens. This develops when blood flow ceases to a part of a body, usually as a result of blockage of dead tissue.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Biological treatment of water pollution is done with the help of
questions.solution: Water hyacinth (phytoplankton), can purify water polluted by biological and chemical wastes-water hyacinth has remarkable capacity to accumulate poisonous metals including radioactive substances.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Chromosome walking is
options: to see position of ribosomes on nuclear membrane
to check the expression of mutant genes
questions.solution: Chromosome walking is used in genetic mapping. It is a method of positional cloning used to find, isolate and clone a particular allele in a gene library. It is DNA based method of genetic mapping in which the chromosome is analysed by extending one tip to reach the other. This involves systematically moving along the chromosome from a known location to an unknown location.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: RNA binds to tRNA through
questions.solution:
tRNA binds to in RNA through anticodon loop, as it bears the based complementary to those on mRNA being translated.
The amino acid loop of tRNA is involved in binding the respective amino acid. The $\mathrm{T} \psi \mathrm{C}$ loop helps in binding the amino acid, while the D loop of tRNA is the binding site for aminoacyl synthetase.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In DNA $20 \%$ bases are adenine. What percentage of bases are pyrimidines?
questions.solution:
According to chargaff's rule
$\mathrm{A}+\mathrm{G}=\mathrm{C}+\mathrm{T}=50 \%$
$\therefore \quad$ If $\mathrm{A}=20 \%$ then $\mathrm{T}=20 \%$
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{C}+\mathrm{T} & =50 \% \\
\mathrm{C}=50 \%-20 \% & =30 \%
\end{aligned}$
Total percentage of pyrimidine bases
$(\mathrm{T}+\mathrm{Q}) \text { are } 20+30=50 \%$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not a stop codon?
questions.solution:
UAC is not a stop codon. It codes for tyrocine amino acid.
The last codon of a functional gene which helps in termination of the polypeptide chain, is termed as stop or termination codon. These are UAA, UAG and UGA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: During DNA replication, supercoilling is relaxed by
questions.solution:
During DNA replication, super coiling is relaxed by DNA topoisomerase. These enzyme participate in the overwinding or underwinding of DNA. SSBPs are single strand binding proteins which stabilises the unwound parental DNA.
Primase this enzyme synthesise RNA primer on DNA template to initiate DNA synthesis.
RNA Polymerase enzyme helps in synthesis of new DNA strand during replication.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: To which of the following, repressor protein is attached?
questions.solution: Repressor protein is attached to operator gene. It is a regulatory protein synthesised by regulator gene. Repressor is meant for blocking the operator gene so that the structural genes are unable to form mRNAs.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following mRNA can be transcripted as
questions.solution: Among the given options, only first one can be transcripted because it possesses an initiation codon-AUG which codes for methionine. Rest of the mRNA sequences start with termination codons, i.e. UAA (Ochre), UAG (Amber) and UGA (Opal). Therefore, these cannot be transcripted.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Minisatellites or VNTR’s are used in
questions.solution: Minisatellites or Variable Number of Tandem Repeats (VNTR) are used in DNA fingerprinting technique. In VNTR, satellite DNA acts as a probe, that shows very high degree of polymorphism.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which is present at $5^{\prime}$ end of eukaryotic mRNA?
questions.solution: At the 5' end of eukaryotic mRNA, 7 mG (7-methyl guanosine) is present. In eukaryotes, primary transcript is often larger than the functional RNAs. Therefore, post-transcription processing is required to convert primary transcript of all types of RNAs into functional RNAs. It is of four types; cleavage, splicing, terminal additional and nucleotide modification. The terminal additions include capping and tailing. In capping, 7 mG is added to $5^{\prime}$ end of mRNA. In tailing, polyadenylation occurs which is addition of poly (A) tail to $3^{\prime}$ end of mRNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: hnRNA undergoes two additional process. Out of them in one process an unusual nucleotide (methyl GPT) is added to the 5 ' end of molecule. What would you called this?
questions.solution:
The process of addition of methyl GPT at the $5^{\prime}$ end of hnRNA is known as capping. Enzyme guanly transferase catalyses this process.
Cap is essential for the formation of mRNA-Ribosome complex. Translation is not possible if cap is lacking, because cap is identified by 18 SrRNA of ribosome unity.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In the lac operon model, lactose molecules function as
options: inducers, which bind with the operator gene
repressors, which bind with the operator gene
inducers, which bind with the repressor protein
corepressors, which bind with repressor protein
questions.solution:
Lactose or lac operon of E. coli is an example of inducible operon. Lactose is known to be the inducer and substrate for the enzyme $\beta$-galactosidase.
It binds with the repressor protein and changes it into a non-DNA binding state thus activating the operon.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A point mutation where guanine is replaced by cytosine is also called
questions.solution:
The base pair mutations are most common type of mutations. These can be further categorised into two types
(a) Transitions, i.e. in which a purine base is replaced by another purine and pyrimidine is replaced by another pyrimidine.
(b) Transversion, i.e. those in which a purine (guanine) is replaced by a pyrimidine (cytosine) and vice-versa.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Consider these sentances regarding to the structure and nature of DNA.
I. DNA has two pyrimidine bases which contain single ring structure.
II. In DNA, composition of bases should be $\frac{A+T}{G+C}=1$.
III. Each base pair of DNA is $3.4 Å$ apart from othes base pair.
IV. The nucleosome model DNA packaging was proposed by Kornberg and Thomas.
Choose the correct statements from given options.
questions.solution:
Erwin Chargaff (1950) made quantitative analysis of DNA and showed that the composition of DNA molecules varies in complex ways depending upon the source of DNA. He proposed Base Equivalence rule or Chargaff's rule, which states that, for double-stranded DNA, the proportion of adenine always equals to that of thymine and proportion of guanine always equals to that of cytosine,
i.e.
$\mathrm{A}=\mathrm{T}$ and $\mathrm{G}=\mathrm{C}$.
It follows that, an equal proportion of purines and pyrimidines is always seen in a double-stranded DNA, i.e.
$A+G=T+C$ or $\frac{A+G}{T+C}=1$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The fact that DNA is a genetic material was established by the experiment of
questions.solution: The experiment of Hershey and Chase by bacteriophage first time proved that DNA is a genetic material.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Bacteriophage $\phi \times 174$ is different from other bacteriophage due to the presence of
questions.solution: Bacteriophage $\phi \times 174$ has single-stranded DNA (ssDNA).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Specific radioacitve identification of ribosomal RNA can be achieved by using $\mathrm{C}^{14}$ labelled
questions.solution: The pyrimidine, thymine and the sugar deoxyribose are incorporated into DNA, not RNA. Adenine, cytocine and guanine are incorporated into DNA, not RNA. Adenine, cytosine and guanine are incorported into both RNA and DNA. Ribose sugar and the base uracil are specific to all the forms of RNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The genome of Caenorhabditis elegans consists of
questions.solution: The Caenorhabditis elegans genome sequence is now complete, fully contiguous telomere to telomere and totaling 97 million base pairs. The sequence has catalyzed the collection of systematic data sets and analyses, including protein-coding genes, giving a total number of 18000 genes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In a mutational event, when adenine is replaced by guanine, it is a case of
questions.solution:
Transition are substitution gene mutation in which a purine (adenine) is replaced by another purine (guanine) or a pyrimidine (thymine is replaced by another pyrimidine cytosine). Change of codon ATC to GTC or ATT or ACC is an example of transition.
Transversion in substitution gene mutation in which a purine $(\mathrm{A}$ or G$)$ is replaced by a pyrimidine (T or C) or vice-versa.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The part of the bacterial chromosome sharing homology with genome fragment transferred from the recipients to cell during merozygote formation is known as
questions.solution:
Endogenate is the native DNA of the recipient bacterial cell which shares homology with the DNA fragment inserted into it.
Exogenate is the DNA fragment that has been transferred into a recipient cell.
Eugenic is the study or practice of attempting to improve the human gene pool by encouraging reproduction among people with desirable traits. Dysgenics is the study of factors causing propagation of undesirable or disadvantageous genes and traits.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Linker-DNA is attached to
questions.solution: Linker-DNA is attached to $\mathrm{H}_1$ histone length. Linker DNA connects two adjacent nucleosomes. Its length is varied (about $145 Å$ with 70 bp). Nucleosome and linker DNA together constitute chromatosome. Chromatosome contains a histone octamer and DNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not true?
options: Southern hybridisation - related to identification of RNA sequence
questions.solution: Option (d) is not correctly matched because Southern hybridisations technique is related to identification of DNA sequences. RNA sequences are indentified by Northern hybridisation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In 1944, Avery, McCarty and MacLeod isolated a substance from heat killed virulent form of bacteria and added to non-virulent form of bacteria which changed the non-virulent to virulent from this substance can be distroyed by
questions.solution: Avery, MacLeod and McCarty carried out an experiment from which they reported that DNA is a substance that causes bacterial transformation from non-virulent to virulent bacteria, but DNAase acts as a waste management endonucleases responsible for DNA fragmentation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Probes, used in DNA fingerprinting, are initially
questions.solution: Probes used for DNA finger printing are usually prepared from mini-satellite or micro-satellite DNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Satellite DNA is useful tool in
questions.solution: All eukaryotes except yeast contain satellite DNA. Satellite DNA regions like NTR/RFLP are the basis of DNA fingerprinting (forensic science).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Allelic sequence variation where more than one variant allele at a locus in a human population with a frequency greater than 0.01 is referred to as
questions.solution: Allelic sequence variation is described as DNA polymorphism if more than one variant (allele) at a locus occurs in human population with a frequency greater than 0.01 . In simple terms if an inheritable mutation is observed in a population at high frequency, it is referred to as DNA polymorphism.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which set is similar?
questions.solution: A mature ovarian follicle is called Graafian follicle. After ovulation, the empty Graafian follicle shows deposition of luetin and forms corpus luteum that ultimately degenerates.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In DNA, when AGCT occurs, their association is as per which of the following pair
questions.solution: DNA molecule has four bases-adenine guanine cytosine and thymine. Adenine always pairs with thymine and guanine pairs with cytosine. Their association is A-T and G-C.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A segment of DNA has 120 adenine and 120 cytosine bases. The total number of nucleotides present in the segment is
questions.solution:
According to Chargaff's rules, the amount of adenine is always equal to that of thymine, and the amount of guanine is always equal to that of cytosine.
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{A} & =\mathrm{T}(120) \\ \mathrm{G} & =\mathrm{C}(120)\end{aligned}$
The total number of nucleotides would be $120 \times 4=480$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which is common to kidney and skeleton in mammals?
questions.solution: The term 'pelvis' is common to both kidney and skeleton in mammals. In relation to kidney, it can be described as a chamber in the kidney into which the urine drains from renal tubules before passing to the ureter. For skeleton, it is related with pelvic girdle or hip girdle.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Width of the DNA molecule is
questions.solution: The 3-D structure of DNA is represented by a double helix, in which each turn has a diameter of $34 Å$ and contains 10 base pairs at a distance of $3.4 Å$. The width of DNA molecule is $20 Å$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The codon for anticodon $3^{\prime}$-UUUA- $5^{\prime}$ is
questions.solution: In RNA the thymine of DNA is replaced by uracil. Codon and anticodon both found on RNA and are complementary to each other so, the codon for anticodon $3^{\prime}$-UUUA-5' will be $5^{\prime}$-AAUU- $3^{\prime}$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: One gene-one enzyme relationship was established for the first time in
questions.solution: One gene-one enzyme relationship was initially proposed by Beadle and Tatum and based on the experiments conducted on Neurospora crassa. They were awarded by Nobel Prize in 1958 for this achievement.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: DNA element with ability to change position iS called
questions.solution: Transposon is aDNA segment or genetic element moving from one chromosome to another. The 'term', was introduced by R W Hedges and A E Jacob in 1974, to'controlling elements' orjumping genes', discovered by Barbara McClintock (1950) in maize.Cistron is the functional unit of DNA molecules, it codes for a particular gene product. $\square$ Recon is the cross-over unit or it is segment of DNA which participates in crossing over. 'Introns are the interrupted genes or those regions of a gene which do not form the part of $m R N A$ and are removed from primary $m \mathrm{RNA}$ during gene expression in eukaryotes. These are non-coding sequences which get removed during splicing and processing.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Initiation codon is
questions.solution: AUG which codes for methionine (Met) is used as start signal in protein synthesis. It is the most common chain initiation codon. In prokaryotes, the codons GUG and UUG are also employed in instances as start codons. Three triplets,UAA (ochre),UAG (amber) andUGA (opal) do not code for any amino acid and are callednon-sense codons. Their function is to terminate the message for gene controlling protein synthesis. Non-sense codons are also calledchain termination codons. UUU (uracil) codes for phenylalanine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: DNA multiplication is called
questions.solution: DNA multiplication or duplication of DNA takes place by replication. It takes place during S-phase of interphase during cell-cycle. DNA replication is semi-conservative was strongly evidenced byMathew Meselson andFranklin Stahl in 1958 in E. coli.Transcription is the mode by which DNA passes its genetic information to mRNA. It is the first step in protein synthesis which takes place in nucleus of eukaryotic cells.Translation is the synthesis of a chain of polypeptide by mRNA. It is the process when protein synthesis takes place at the site of ribosomes. It involves 3 steps initiation, elongation and termination.Transduction is the transfer of genes from one bacterial cell to another by means of a virus. Transduction was discovered byZinder andLederberg in 1952.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A eukaryotic gene contains two kinds of base sequences. Which of these plays an important role in protein synthesis?
questions.solution: In eukaryotic genes some non-coding parts are interspersed between coding parts. These noncoding parts are called introns and coding sequence are known as exons, ie, exons play significant role in protein synthesis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Consider these following sentences and choose the correct ones.
I. Each gene contains a specific promoter region and a leader sequence for guiding the beginning of transcription.
II. Only one strand of DNA, called template strand is copied by RNA polymerase this strand runs in $3^{\prime} \rightarrow 5^{\prime}$ direction.
III. RNA polymerase adds complementary nucleotides forming single strand $m$ RNA in $3^{\prime} \rightarrow 5^{\prime}$ direction.
IV. Section of DNA that has been transcribed is rewound into its original configuration.
questions.solution: All four statements are correct.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
choose the alternative which can be substituted for the given words/sentence.
A person who enters without any invitation
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: "Bundle of His" are
questions.solution: Bundle of His is a network of muscle fibres found in between two ventricles.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A man of 'A' blood group marries a woman of 'AB' blood group. Which type of progeny would indicate that man is heterozygous ?
questions.solution: From the above cross, it is clear that the occurence of B blood group progeny indicates that man is heterozygous for the allele \(A\). If the man is homozygous for the allele \(A\), the progeny will have either an \(A\) or \(A B\) type blood group.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Stop codons are
questions.solution:
Three codons UAG, UAA and UGA are stop codons or non-sense codons. They do not code for any amino acid.
AUG works as initiation codon in eukaryotes and prokaryotes. In rare case GUG also serves as initiation codon in bacterial protein synthesis.
UAC coded for tyrosine, UGG coded for tryptophan, AGU coded for serine, AGA coded for argenine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Nucleotide are building blocks of nucleic acids, nucleotide is a composite molecule formed by
questions.solution:
Nucleotides are the building blocks of nucleic acids (DNA and RNA). A single nucleotide is composed of a phosphate molecule, a five carbon sugar (either ribose or deoxyribose) and a purine (adenine or guanine) or a pyrimidine (thymine or cytosine or uracil) nitrogenous base.
Nucleoside $=$ Base + Sugar
Nucleotide $=$ Base + Sugar + Phosphate

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following makes use of RNA as a template to synthesize DNA?
questions.solution:
In 1970 H. Temin and D. Baltimore independently discovered the enzyme reverse transcriptase. This enzyme uses RNA as template for the synthesis of C-DNA (Complementary DNA).
$\begin{gathered}\text { RNA } \\ \downarrow \\ \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{DNA}\end{gathered}$ Reverse transcriptase used RNA as  template

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: During transcription holoenzyme RNA polymerase binds to a DNA sequence and the DNA assumes a saddle like structure at that point. What is that sequence called?
questions.solution: TATA box is present in eukaryotic promoter region. It has a resemblance with pribnow box of prokaryotes. TATA box was identified by Dr. Hogness and so, it is also called as Hogness box. It is a 7 bp long region located 20 bp upstream to the start point. During the process of transcription the RNA polymerase (a holoenzyme which has a core unit and a sigma factor for proper initiation of transcription) binds to TATA box due to which DNA assumes a saddle like structure at this place.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: E. coli cells with a mutated Z gene of the lac operon cannot grow in medium containing only lactose as the source of energy because
options: in the presence of glucose, E. coli cells do not utilize lactose
they cannot transport lactose from the medium into the cell
the lac operon is constitutively active in these cells
questions.solution: Lac operon is a cluster of genes encoding three proteins that bacteria use to obtain energy from the sugar lactose. There are three structural genes in lac operon. Promoter for lac $i$ (i) Lac Z (3063 bp) : This gene codes for the enzyme $\beta$ galactosidase which breaks lactose into glucose and galactose to be utilized in the cell. Therefore, E.coli cells with a mutated Z genes of the lac operon can not grow in medium containing only lactose as the source of energy because they cannot synthesize functional $\beta$-galactosidase. (ii) Lac Y (800 bp) : It codes for $\beta$ galactose permease enzyme which is a membrane bound protein and helps in transport of metabolites. (iii) Lac A (800 bp) : It codes for $\beta$ galactose transacetylase an enzyme that transfers an acetyl group from acetyl Co-A to $\beta$-galactosidase.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Sequence of DNA (non-coding) is known as :
questions.solution: Introns are DNA sequence lying within a coding sequence (exons) but not usually encoding cell product and resulting so called split genes. Cistron is a segment of linear DNA encoding a specific and functional product, usually protein.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which is called soluble RNA ?
questions.solution:
There are following three types of cellular RNA.
(1) Transfer RNA or soluble RNA
(2) Messenger RNA or template RNA
(3) Ribosomal RNA
Transfer RNA ( $t$-RNA) and messenger RNA (m-RNA) are synthesized on DNA template of chromosomes while ribosomal RNA ( $r$-RNA) is derived from nucleolar DNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Non-genetic RNA is of :
questions.solution:
Non-genetic RNA is found in all eukaryotic and prokaryotic organisms. It is of three types i.e.,
(i) Ribosomal RNA ( $r$-RNA)
(ii) Transfer RNA ( $t$-RNA)
(iii) Messenger RNA (m-RNA)

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The largest gene in man is
questions.solution: The largest known gene in human is the dystrophin gene, which has 79 axons spanning at least 2300 kb . The human dystrophin gene requires 16 hours to be transcribed.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What's the difference between RNA and DNA?
questions.solution: DNA has deoxy ribose pentose sugar and four nitrogenous bases i.e. adenine [A], guanine [G], both are purines and cytosine $[\mathrm{C}]$, thymine $[\mathrm{T}]$ are pyrimidines. While RNA has ribose pentose sugar and four nitrogenous bases as in DNA except uracil [U] in place of thymine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Transformation in bacteria was discovered by :
questions.solution: Griffiths (1928), for the first time, reported transformation in bacteria and later on Avery, Mcleod and McCarthy (1944) studied it in detail.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: DNA replication generally proceeds in a
questions.solution: DNA synthesis or replication always proceeds in $\mathrm{C}_5 \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_3$ direction. Both the strands replicate by the addition of nucleotide monomer in $\mathrm{C}_5 \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_3$ direction.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A nucleoside is
questions.solution: Base + sugar form a nucleoside and base + sugar + phosphate group combine together to give a nucleotide.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In split genes the coding sequences are called
questions.solution: Split genes were discovered by Phillip Sharp and Robert Richard. The part of DNA which express itself by making $m$ RNA is called exon. One exon is separated from other by inactive part of DNA called intron. This is called split DNA.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: DNA polymerase enzyme is required for synthesis of :
questions.solution: The enzyme needed for replication of DNA i.e. formation of DNA from DNA is DNA polymerase III. DNA replication is initiated by RNA primer which is later removed by DNA polymerase I.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The Okazaki fragments in DNA chain growth
questions.solution: The Okazaki fragments in DNA chain growth polymerise in the $5^{\prime}$ to $3^{\prime}$ direction. The replicated DNA result in transcription.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: One gene-one enzyme hypothesis of Beadle and Tatum was experimentally proved on
questions.solution: One gene-one enzyme theory was given by Beadle and Tatum in 1958, while they were working on red mould or Neurospora crassa (Ascomycetes fungus), which is also called Drosophila of plant kindgom.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What is ribotide?
questions.solution: Ribotide is a nucleotide containing ribose as its pentose component. It is considered a molecular precursor of nucleic acids. In living organisms, the most common bases for ribotides are adenine, guanine, cytosine or uracil. Therefore, option (1), i.e. ribose + uracil + phosphate is correct.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Predation and parasitism are which type of interactions.
questions.solution: Both predation and parasitism show negative interactions. In negative interaction one species is harmed ( - ) while the other is benefitted.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following plant keeps its stomata open during night and closed during the day?
questions.solution: Scotoactive mechanism of opening of stomata is seen in fleshy xerophytes like Opuntia, cactus, etc. They keep their stomata open at night and closed during daytimes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Choose the wrong pair
questions.solution: Tectona grandis (Teak) is found in tropical moist deciduous forests.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: An interaction in which one organism is benefitted and other is unaffected
questions.solution:
Commensalism is a type of interaction where one organism is benefitted and other remain unaffected. In predation, one organism feeds on another organism.
In parasitism, one organism (parasite) gets the benefit at the expense of the host.
Mutualism is a type of positive interaction in which all individuals are benefitted by one another.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Viscum album grown on trees. This is an example of
questions.solution: Viscum album is a partial stem parasite that grows on poplar, apple, walnut, oats, etc. The parasite send primary haustorium into the host for deriving food from them.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is a total root parasite?
questions.solution:
Rafflesia is a total root parasite, which remains attached to the roots of the host plants and derives all of its nutritions from them.
Cuscuta (dodder or amarbel) is a total stem parasite, while Santalum album (sandal wood) is a partial root parasite.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The permanent decrease in population number occurs due to
questions.solution: Mortality is the death rate per thousand individuals. Its rate decreases population size and population density. It is due to improved personal hygiene, sanitation and modern medicines. $\therefore \quad \text { Mortality }=\left(100 \times \frac{\text { Births }}{\text { Deaths }}\right)$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Animals having a built in thermostat to maintain constant body temperature are known as
questions.solution: Homeothermic are the animals having a nearly uniform or constant body temperature. These animals are known as warm blooded animals, eg, birds, man.Poikilothermic are those, having a variable temperature which fluctuates with that of environment. They are called the cold blooded animals, eg, reptiles, amphibians.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Animals undergoes inactive stage during winter, is known as
questions.solution: Hibernation is the inactive stage during winter or the dormancy during winter. It is known as winter sleep.Aestivation in zoology is the dormancy during summer or dry weather. In botany, it is the arrangement of parts in flower bud.Adaptation is the sum total of environmental condition achieved by the organisms in a habitat.Acclimatization is gradual physiological adaptation in response to enviromental changes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Small fish get stuck near the bottom of a shark and derives its nutrition from it. This kind of association is called as
questions.solution:
In commensalism, association between members of different species is made in the way that one is benefitted and neither is harmed, eg, small fish (sucker fish) gets stuck near the bottom of a shark with the help of its hold fast (modified dorsal fin) and is dispersed to distant areas. It also gets protection (due to association with shark) and derive its nutrition also. However, the shark does not get any benefit or harm form the sucker fish.
Antibiosis refers to the complete or partial inhibition of one organism by another through the production of some substance or environmental conditions as a result of metabolic pathways.
In parasitism the parasite organisms derives food and sometimes shelter also, from the host without killing it.
In predation, predatory organisms are free living which catch and kill another species for food.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Crossing over occurs in
questions.solution: Crossing over ie, exchange of segments between non-sister chromatids of homologous chromosome occurs in pachytene stage of prophase-I (meiosis-I).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Study of Ecology of population is called
questions.solution: The study of ecology of population is called Demecology.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Animals have the innate ability to escape from predation. Examples for the same are given below. Select the incorrect example
options: enlargement of body size by swallowing air in puffer fish
questions.solution:
Animals resist predation by cryptic colouration, deceptive marking, behavioural defenses and the possession of mechanical or chemical defenses.
Example : A. enlargement of body size by swallowing air in puffer fish.
B. Melanism in moths.
C. Colour change in chameleon.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: On high mountains, difficulty in breathing is due to :
questions.solution: On high mountains, difficulty in breathing is due to decrease in partial pressure.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Biotic factors are :
options: chemical factors of soil which affect life
physical factors of soil which affect life
all living organisms which influence other organisms
factors of atmosphere which affect life
questions.solution: Biotic factors include all the living organisms which influence the other organisms.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The effect of intraspecific competition on niches is to make them
questions.solution: The intraspecific competition makes the size of niche smaller due to keen competition among the members.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The Orobanche plant is
questions.solution: Total root parasites have no chlorophyll. These are common on the roots of Cruciferae and Solanaceae e.g., Balanophora and Orobanche etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Zero growth means
questions.solution: Zero growth rate means natality (i.e., birth rate), balances the mortality (i.e., death rate)

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: 'Sun basket' is
options: the device to utilise sun rays directly to meet the requirement of heat energy
the sufficient amount of sunlight stored in a cell
questions.solution: Green plants are called sun basket because they store solar energy by converting it into chemical energy with the help of chloroplast by the process calledphotosynthesis.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The part of earth in which life exist is known as ?
questions.solution: Biosphere is the part of earth where life exists.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Biotic potential or potential natality means
options: natural increase of population under ideal/optimum conditions
potential of organism in a biome
number of organisms in a biome
species of maximum number in a population
questions.solution: Biotic potential is natality under optimum condition. The actual birth rate under existing conditions is called realised natality.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Green potatoes are toxic due to
questions.solution: Solanin is a glycoalkaloid poison or natural nerve toxin produced in the green part of the potato. It is a bitter poisonous crystalline alkaloid, which helps the plant to defend against predators, insects, diseases, etc. Ingestion of it may cause vomiting, diarrhoea, headache and even paralysis of central nervous system.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Benthic organisms are found in
questions.solution: Organisms confined to bottom are called benthos and their habitat is called benthic.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: $\mathrm{XYY} \rightarrow$ composition
questions.solution: The individuals having XYY chromosomes are known as super males. They have an additional Y chromosome. They suffer from poly Y male syndrome. These individuals are characterised by abnormal height, mental retardation, antisocial and criminal bent of mind.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Karyotype of Down's syndrome has how many chromosomes?
questions.solution: Karyotype of people suffering from Down's syndrome has 47 chromosomes ( $45+\mathrm{XY}$ in males, $45+\mathrm{XX}$ in females) instead of 46 . It is caused due to the presence of an extra chromosome number 21 , thus it is also known as 21 trisomy.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Gynaecomastia is present in which syndrome?
questions.solution: Gynaecomastia is present in klinefelter's syndrome (XXY). It is swelling of the breast tissue in boys or men, caused by an imbalance of the hormones oestrogen and testosterone. It is commonly found in males affected with Klinefelter's syndroms. Klinefelter's syndrome is the most common sex chromosomal disorder in males. It also characterised by low testosterone and reduced muscle mass, facial and body hair.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: AB blood group shows
questions.solution: In humans, AB blood group shows codominance. Here, allele $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{A}}$ for A-type blood is codominant with allele $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{B}}$ for B-type blood. In heterozygotes, $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{A}} \mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{B}}$ expresses the characteristics of both A and B antigens, i.e. $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{A}} \mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{B}}$ possess RBCs that carry both A and B antigens at their surface.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A normal man and a normal woman both having one of their parent albino, if get married then what is the probability of their child of being albino?
questions.solution:
Albinism is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern that means both parents contribute a mutated gene for the person to have albinism. So, there is a $25 \%$ chance that any of their children will have albinism. Then $50 \%$ of the children will carry the mutated gene, but will not have albinism.
The last possibility is to neither have albinism or carry the mutated gene, that's the other $25 \%$ chance.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Non-disjunction in meiosis results in
questions.solution: Non-disjunction in meiosis results in trisomy, i.e. the presence of an extra chromosome. Such individuals have 47 chromosomes instead of 46 . Some examples of such abnormalities include Down's syndrome (21-trisomy), Patau's syndrome (13-trisomy) and Edward's syndrome (18-trisomy).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
If two persons with 'AB' blood group marry and have suffeciently large number of children, these children could be classified as 'A' blood group. 'AB blood group 'B' blood group in $1: 2: 1$ ratio.
Modern technique of protein electrophoresis reveals presence of both ' $A$ ' and ' $B$ ' type proteins in ' $A B$ ' blood group individuals. This is an example of
