questions.solution: AB blood group is characterised by the presence of both antigen A and B i.e. $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{A}}$ and $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{B}}$ over the surface of RBCs. Here both alleles are able to express themselves forming antigens A and B. This is called as codominance.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The phenotypic ratio of trihybrid cross in $\mathrm{F}_2$ - generation is
questions.solution: The phenotypic ratio of trihybrid cross in $\mathrm{F}_2$-generation will be $27: 9: 9: 9: 3: 3: 3: 1$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following pair is not correct?
questions.solution: Cri-Du-Chat (Cat Cry) Syndrome was first described by Lejeune in 1963 in France. The affected newborn cries-like mewing of a cat. This condition is due to a deletion in the short arm of the chromosome number 5 .

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In pea plants, yellow seeds are dominant to green. If a heterozygous yellow seeded plant is crossed with a green seeded plant, what ratio of yellow and green seeded plants could you expect in $\mathrm{F}_1$-generation.
questions.solution: Yellow seeds are dominant is green (Y). So, a heterozygous yellow seeded plant will have the genotype of $(\mathrm{Y}, \mathrm{y})$ and a green seeded plant will have genotype (yy). When these plants are crossed, the $\mathrm{F}_1$-generation will have the ratio of yellow : green as 50 : 50 .

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Snapdragon flower is an exception of Mendel's laws. It is a good example of
questions.solution: 4 O'clock and snapdragon flower are best examples of incomplete dominance where in heterozyous condition pink flowers are produced from white and red parents. It is an exception to Mendel's law of dominance.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Match the following Column I with Column II.
$\begin{array}{ll|ll}
\hline & \text{Column I} & & \text{Column II} \\
\hline \text{A.} & \text{Complementary ratio} & 1. & 9: 7 \\
\text{B.} & \text{Supplementary ratio} & 2. & 9: 3: 4 \\
\text{C.} & \text{Epistatic ratio (Masking)} & 3. & 12: 3: 1 \\
\text{D.} & \text{Inhibitory ratio} & 4. & 13: 3 \\
\hline
\end{array}$
Codes
questions.solution:
Epistasis is interaction between two or more genes to control a single phenotype. Dominant epistasis also called masking has a ratio $12: 3: 1$ in the $\mathrm{F}_2$ phenotype. Supplementary genes which is recessive epistasis has ratio $9: 3: 4$.
Complementary gene where both dominant alleles are needed for phenotype has ratio 9:7. Inhibitor gene where one gene inhibits the expression of another gene has ratio $13: 3$ in $\mathrm{F}_2$ progeny.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Select the correct statement from the one's given below with respect to dihybrid cross.
options: tightly linked gene on the same chromosome show higher recombinations
genes for apert on same chromosomes show very few recombination
genes loosely linked on the same chromosomes show similar recombination as lightly linked ones
tightly linked genes on the same chromosomes show very few recombination
questions.solution: When two genes are close together on the same chromosome, they do not assort independently and are said to be linked and shows less frequency of recombination. Whereas genes located on different chromosomes assort independently and have a recombination frequency of \(50 \%\) called as less closely linked genes. So, the correct answer is 'Tightly linked genes on the same chromosome show very few recombinations'.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The year 1900 AD is highly significant for geneticists due to
questions.solution: In the year 1900 Hugo de Vries, Karl Correns and Erich Tschermark independently rediscovered the research carried out by Mendel, his experiments on heredity and variations and laid the basic of modern genetics.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Mendelism
$\mathrm{F}_1$-generation means
questions.solution: The 1st generation obtained from crossing two parents is called as first filial generation or $F_1$-generation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Skin colour is controlled by
options: 2 pairs of genes with an intragene
questions.solution: Skin colour in humans is controlled by three pair of genes $\mathrm{Aa}, \mathrm{Bb}, \mathrm{Cc}$.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The dominant epistasis ratio is
questions.solution: In dominant epistatis, a dominant gene (epistatic) marks the expression of another dominant or recessive gene (hypostatic), such interactions give the modified $\mathrm{F}_2$ ratio as 12:3:1.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following cross will produce terminal flower in garden pea?
questions.solution: Axillary position $(A)$ is dominant over terminal (a) position. When $\mathrm{Aa} \times \mathrm{Aa}$ is crossed we get $3: 1$ ratio. of axillary and terminal flowers.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: If a child is of O blood group and his father is of $B$ blood group, the genotype of father is
questions.solution: Genotype of a person with blood group 'B' may be $I^B I^B$ or $I^O I^B$, person with genotype $I^B I^B$ can not produce offsprings with blood group ' O ' in any case but if the person's genotype is $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{O}} \mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{B}}$, then its offsprings may have blood group 'O'. e.g.,

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which trait is shown by given pedigree analysis?
questions.solution: The given pedigree analysis is showing autosomal dominant trait. If such a trait is present in pedigree, it seldom skips a generation. An autosomal recessive trait may skip a generation. A sex-linked dominant trait is more common in females while a sex-linked recessive trait is more common in males.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of them is odd with respect to human male?
questions.solution: Option (a) is incorrect/odd with respect to human male because the male karyotype has $44+\mathrm{XY}$ chromosomes. This means single X -chromosome is present with one Y-chromosomes. Barr body is an additional X-chromosome found in female somatic cells.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which has an additional X-chromosome?
questions.solution: Klinefelter's syndrome is caused by one or more extra X-chromosome (i.e., XXY, XXXY). The man with such a syndrome is sterile, has small testes, long legs and feminine characters like breasts.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A hereditary disease which is never passed on from father to son is
questions.solution: Man has only one X-chromosome that is inherited to his daughter. Therefore, a hereditary disease, which is X-chromosomal linked, is never passed on from father to son.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A polygenic inheritance in human beings is
questions.solution: Genes that when acting individually have a small effect but that collectively produce a significant phenotypic expression are called polygenes, e.g., inheritance of kernel colour in wheat and skin colour in human.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A man of blood group-A marries a woman of blood group-AB, which type of progeny would indicate that man is heterozygous?
questions.solution: In this case, progeny with blood group- B is produced only when man is heterozygous, ie, $\mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{A}} \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
Pure tall plants are crossed with pure dwarf plants. In the $\mathrm{F}_1$ generation, all plants were tall. These tall plants of $F_1$ generation were selfed and the ratio of tall to dwarf plants obtained was
$3: 1$. This is called
questions.solution: The phenotype produced by heterozygotes is identical with that produced by homozygous for the concerned dominant allele. The dominant allele in such a situation is known as completely dominant, eg, when pure tall plant is crossed with pure dwarf plant. In $F_1$ generation, all plants were tall. These tall plants of $\mathrm{F}_1$ generation were selfed than the ratio of tall to dwarf plants obtained was $3: 1$. So, allele of tallness is dominant to allele of dwarfness.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In Pisum sativum, there are 14 chromosomes. How many types of homologous pairs can be prepared?
questions.solution: Pisum sativum is a angiospermic plant. In angiosperms, diploid chromosomes are present in body cells, ie, plants body belongs to sporophytic generation ( $2 n$ ). In Pisum sativum, $2 n=14$. So, seven types of homologous pairs are present in Pisum sativum.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Chromosome complement with $2 n-1$ is called as
questions.solution: Aneuploidy is the deletion or addition of one or a few chromosomes from the original genomes. (i)Monosomy is $(2 n-1)$, eg, Tuner's syndrome. (ii)Nullisomy is $(2 n-2)$. (iii)Trisomy is $(2 n+1), \quad e g, \quad K l i n e f e l t e r ' s$ syndrome. (iv)Tetrasomy $(2 n+2)$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A woman with two genes (one on each X -chromosome) for haemophilia and one gene for colour blindness on the X-chromosome marries a normal man. How will the progeny be?
options: All sons and daughters are haemophilic and colourblind
$50 \%$ haemophilic colourblind sons and $50 \%$ haemophilic sons
questions.solution: Haemophilia and colour blindness both are recessive X-linked traits. They express in males when present in single copy (heterozygous) but in females, they express only when present in homozygous condition. Results (a) $50 \%$ sons are colourblind and haemophilic. (b) $50 \%$ sons are haemophilic only. (c) $50 \%$ daughters are carrier for colour blindness and haemophilia. (d) $50 \%$ daughters are carrier for haemophilia only.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Chromosome complement with $2 n-1$ is called
questions.solution:
When a change in the chromosome number does not involve entire sets of chromosome and restrict to only a few of chromosome, the situation is called aneuploidy. Aneuploidy may be of following types
(a) Monosomic $(2 n-1)$
(b) Trisomic $(2 n+1)$
(c) Nullisomic $(2 n-2)$
(d) Tetrasomic $(2 n+2)$
(e) Double momosomic $(2 n-1-1)$
(f) Double trisomic $(2 n+1+1)$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The trisomy for $21^{\text {st }}$ chromosome is called

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Jumping genes in maize were discovered by
questions.solution: 'Jumping genes' ormovable genetic elements were discovered by 'Barbara McClintock' (1902-92) in maize. These 'controlling elements' could move from one location to another on the chromosome. Barbara McClintock got the Nobel Prize for these genetic elements (Ds=Dissociation gene, $\mathrm{Ac}=$ Association gene) which can jump to any chromosome from their original location on chromosome. The term'transposon' was introduced by R W Hedges and A E Jacob (1974) to these genetic element (jumping gene)Hugo de Vries introduced the term'mutation', in connection with the studies on Oenothera lamarckiana (evening primrose). His theory is known as mutation theory.T H Morgan proposed the 'gene theory' (linkage of genes) and crossing over.Gregor Johann Mendel (1822-1884) is known as the 'father of genetics'. The first scientific study leading to formulation of laws of inheritance was carried out by Mendel.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Number of Barr body which will found in case of Turner's syndrome will be
options: Can't be determine by given data
questions.solution:
The number of barr body in human $=$ one less than the number of $X$ Chromosome.
In case of Turner syndrome $45(44+X)$ chromosomes are found. So number of Barr bodies will be zero.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Connecting link between annelids and molluscs is
questions.solution: Neopilina (mollusc) is considered connecting link between Annelida and Mollusca.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is a test cross ?
questions.solution: A test cross is a cross between heterozygous $\mathrm{F}_1$ hybrid and double recessive homozygous eg, $\mathrm{Tt} \times$ tt to show whether $\mathrm{F}_1$ is homozygous or heterozygous.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Two genes $R$ and $y$ are located very close on the chromosome linkage map on maize plant. When RRYY and rryy genotypes are hybridised, then $\mathrm{F}_2$ segregation will show
options: higher number of the recombinant types
higher number of the parental types
questions.solution: Law of independent assortment does not applicable when the gene of different character occupy on the same homologous chromosomes, i.e., linked genes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: $1: 2: 1$ phenotypic and genotypic ratio is found in
questions.solution: Incomplete dominance or blending inheritance was first seen in Mirabilis jalapa (4'O clock plant). Here, when red flowers are crossed with white flowers variety, the $F_1$ hybrid is pink and $\mathrm{F}_2$ ratio is 1 red : 2 pink : 1 white.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A man and a woman, who do not show any apparent signs of a certain inherited disease, have seven children (2 daughters and 5 sons). Three of the sons suffer from the given disease but none of the daughters are affected. Which of the following mode of inheritance do you suggest for this disease?
questions.solution: A genetic characteristic that is determined by genes located on the sex chromosomes ( X or Y ) are called sex linked characteristic and the condition where expression of sex linked genes is limited to one sex is called sex limited characteristics. In given problem, disease is the result of sex-linked recessive genes. As neither man nor woman shows signs of disease it means woman would be carrier for disease causing gene. In their children none of the daughter is suffering from disease, while the sons are suffering, it means daughters are also carrier, i.e, X-linked recessive. Suppose, genotype of man $=\mathrm{XY}$ Genotype of woman $=\mathrm{X}^{\mathrm{d}} \mathrm{X}$ (d-disease causing gene) For each delivery the probability for each combination is $25 \%$. So, among seven children 2 normal daughter, 3 diseased sons and 2 normal sons are possible.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A woman with normal vision, but whose father was colourblind, marries a colourblind man. Suppose that the fourth child of this couple was a boy. This boy
options: will be partially colourblind since he is heterozygous for the colourblind mutant allele
may be colourblind or may be of normal vision
questions.solution: Colourblindness is a sex linked (X-linked) trait. It always transfers from mother to son. A woman, whose father was colourblind, will cross be carrier for colourblind trait. Marriage of this woman with a colourblind man will result of the following possibilities.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is not a hereditary disease?
questions.solution: Cretinism is a disorder which is caused by deficiency of thyroid hormones in infants. Symptoms of this disorder are slow body growth and mental development, slow heart beat, low blood pressure, stunted growth, retarded sexual development etc. This disease can be treated by an early administration of thyroid hormones.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Haemophilia is more commonly seen in human males than in human females because
options: this disease is due to an X-linked dominant mutation
a greater proportion of girls die in infancy
this disease is due to an X-linked recessive mutation
this disease is due to a Y-linked recessive mutation
questions.solution: Haemophilia, a hereditary (recessive X-linked) disease is caused due to fault in genes, which controlling the factor VIII and IX, located on X-chromosome. The male carries only one X-chromosome, his other sex chromosome, which carries no genes for blood clotting, so the condition is usually seen only in males where only one faulty chromosome is needed. While a female with one faulty X-chromosome will be carrier. So, in females two faulty X-chromosomes are needed to cause the disease.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In order to find out the different types of gametes produced by a pea plant having the genotype AaBb, it should be crossed to a plant with the genotype
questions.solution: Scientists perform test cross to find out the different types of gametes or the genotype of an unknown individual. Test cross is performed always between the $\mathrm{F}_1$ heterozygous plants and pure recessive (homozygous) parent plant. So, in the given case AaBb should be crossed with aabb.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A woman with 47 chromosomes due to three copies of chromosome 21 is characterized by
questions.solution: Down's syndrome is due to an extra chromosome, which combines to the chromosome pair 21. Down syndrome is caused due to trisomy, in which the fusion of a normal $n$ gamete (haploid) takes place with an $n+1$ gamete (diploid). J. Langdon Down first described the developmental defect produced by trisomy in 1866. For this reason it is called Down's syndrome. Persons having Down's syndrome generally have a short, stocky build short hands, flattened facial features and poor muscle tone. In addition they are mentally retarded.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A haemophilic woman marries a normal man then :
options: all the children will be normal
all the sons will be haemophilic
all the girls will be haemophilic

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The arrangement of genes on chromosomes in :
questions.solution: According to chromosome theory, proposed by Morgan and Castle (1912), the genes showing linkage are situated on the same pair of chromosome and are arranged in a linear fashion.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is generally used for induced mutagenesis in crop plants?
questions.solution: Gamma rays (from cobalt 60) is generally used for induced mutation in crop plants.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Ischihara chart is used to detect
questions.solution: Ischihara chart is used to detect colourblindness.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Kappa particles indicate :
questions.solution: Kappa particles are found in Paramecium and possess cytoplasmic inheritance, also called extra-chromosomal inheritance.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A man of blood group 'A', marries a woman of blood group 'B', both of them are heterozygous for blood group, chances of their first child having blood group AB will be
questions.solution: Man Blood group Women Blood group There is $25 \%$ chances of first offspring having blood group AB.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: $2 n-1$ condition represent
questions.solution: When changes involve only a few chromosome say one or two and not the entire genome, the phenomenon is called aneuploidy. Monosomy is a case of aneuploidy, when there is loss of one chromosome from a pair of homologue.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The children of a haemophilic man and a normal woman are
options: neither sons nor daughters are haemophilic
questions.solution: The children of a haemophilic man and normal woman will be all normal. Neither sons nor daughters (only carrier) will be haemophilic.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: A person who is trisomic for twenty first pair of chromosome suffers from
questions.solution: Down's syndrome $(45+\mathrm{XX}$ or $45+\mathrm{XY})$ is the case when there is one extra chromosome in 21st pair of autosome. It is also called trisomy 21 or trisomy-G. It is most common in children of over aged mothers (above 41 years). It is also called Mongolian syndrome.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: If a plant having yellow or round seeds was crossed with another plant having green and wrinkled seeds then $F_1$ progeny are in the ratio
questions.solution: Mendel crossed a dominant homozygous yellow, round seeded plant with a recessive homozygous green and wrinkled seeded plant. The $\mathrm{F}_1$ offsprings produced are heterozygous yellow, round seeded plants parents : yellow round $\times$ Green wrinkled.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q:
In the following questions, a part of the sentence is italicised. Below the sentence, three possible substitutions for the italicised part are given. Choose the one which improves the italicised part. If none of the substitutions improves the italicised part, your answer is (d).
 
Tell your leader that I grant him permission  < i> of stay < /i> in my kingdom.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Shell of molluscs is derived from
questions.solution: Molluscs are the soft bodied, unsegmented animals covered by a shell. In between the shell and body wall is a covering called mantle, which secretes the shell.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of these is used to control human population?
questions.solution: To control the human population many birth control methods can be used, such as hormonal method, i.e., use of contraceptive pills (estrogen and progesterone are main constituents), mechanical prevention method, i.e., use of IUCDs (Intrauterine Contraceptive Device), surgical sterilization methods,like tubectomy (surgical removal of fallopian tubules) or vasectomy (surgical removal of vas deferens).

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which one of the following options is not a sexually transmitted disease?
questions.solution: Pertussis (whooping cough) is not a Sexually Transmitted Disease (STD), while other diseases are transmitted by sexual relationships.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The most important component of the oral contraceptive pills is
questions.solution: Use of contraceptive pills is a widespread form of birth control. Contraceptive pills contain oestrogen and progesterone. The production of the pituitary hormones FSH and LH in the normal sexual cycle of a female is shut down by these hormones. In the absence of FSH, the ovarian follicles do not ripen and ovulation does not occur in the absence of LH .

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: 'GIFT' refers to
questions.solution: Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer (GIFT) is an Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART). In this technique, eggs are removed from the woman and placed in one of the Fallopian tubes, alongwith the man's sperm. This allows fertilisation to take place inside the woman's body. Therefore, this variation is actually an in vivo fertilisation, not an in vitro fertilisation.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The method of directly injecting a sperm into ovum in assisted reproductive technology is called
questions.solution: ICSI is intra cytoplasmic sperm injection. It is one of the techniques of Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART) that help couples to overcome their infertility. In ICSI, sperm is directly injected into ovum, in vitro to form zygote. The embryo, formed is later implanted through ZIFT into woman.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: 'Treponema pallidum' pathogen is a cause of
questions.solution: Syphilis is a Sexually Transmitted Disease (STD) which is caused by Treponema pallidum bacteria.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Depo-provera refers to
questions.solution: Depo-provera injections contains the active ingredient medroxy progesterone acetate, which is a synthetic form of naturally occurring female sex hormone progesterone.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Parbhani Kranti, a variety of bhindi (lady finger) is resistant to
questions.solution:
Prabhani Kranti (example of mutation breeding) is a variety of bhindi which is made resistant to yellow mosaic, virus.
In this Abelmoschus esculentus was transferred from a wild species to produce this new variety.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: When two unrelated individuals or lines are crossed, the performance of $\mathrm{F}_1$ hybrid in often superior to both of its parents. This phenomenon is called
questions.solution: Superiority of hybrids over either of the parent is called heterosis orhybrid.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: The material, which arrests cell division, is obtained from
questions.solution: Colchicine is an antibiotic drug (alkloid), which is obtained from Colchicum (family-Liliacae). It binds to one tubulin molecule and prevents its polymerisation. The depolymerisation of tubulin result in disappearance of mitotic spindle blocking the cell's mitotic chromosomal division at metaphase and anaphase.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Which of the following is not true for a species?
options: Members of a species can interbreed
Variations occur among members of species
Each species is reproductively isolated from every other species
Gene flow does not occur between the populations of a species
questions.solution: Gene flow means the spread of genes through population as affected by movements of individuals and other propagules, e.g., spores, seeds, etc.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Which one is used in tissue culture?
questions.solution: Explant is used in plant tissue culture. It is an excised plant part which has the potential to give rise to whole new plant. The plants obtained through this technique are morphologically and genetically similar to each other and to their parent plant.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: One of the breeding techniques useful to eliminate harmful recessive genes by selection is
questions.solution: When breeding is done between animals of the same breed for 4-6 generations, it is called inbreeding. Inbreeding is necessary if we want to develop a pure line of any animal. It exposes harmful recessive genes that are eliminated by selection. It also helps in accumulation of superior genes and elimination of less desirable genes.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Pebrine is a disease of
questions.solution: Pebrine is a disease of silkworm caused by a small parasite. Nosema, which has a devastating effect on silk industry.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: The term 'aquaculture' means
questions.solution: The term 'aquaculture' refers to the systematic method of cultivation of aquatic organisms to obtain maximum yield of best quality. It includes 'piseiculture' (fish farming) both inland and marine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: In callus culture, roots can be induced by the supply of
questions.solution: Tissue culture is the technique of maintaining and growing cells, tissue, etc. and the differentiation on artificial medium under aseptic condition. Callus is a irregular, unorganised and undifferentiated mass of actively dividing cell. Differentiation of callus is under control of two hormones, ie, auxin and cytokinin. $\begin{array}{l|l} \hline \text{Development of} & \text{Condition} \\ \hline \text{Root} & \frac{\text { Auxin }}{\text { Cytokinin }}= \text{High} \\ \text{Shoot} & \frac{\text { Auxin }}{\text { Cytokinin }}= \text{Low} \\ \text{Both root and shoot} & \frac{\text { Auxin }}{\text { Cytokinin }}= \text{Intermediate} \\ \hline \end{array}$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Which is correctly matched?
questions.solution: Apiculture is the rearing of bee or bee keeping for the production of honey and wax.Pisciculture (fish culture) is the rearing and breeding of fishes in ponds, artificial water reservoirs.Sericulture is the rearing of silkworm or mulberry silkworm (Bombyx mori) for commercial production of silk. Caterpillar feeds on mulberry leaves, its salivary gland secretes liquid silk.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: The name of Norman Borlaug is associated with
questions.solution: Norman Borlaug is associated with green revolution. The green revolution means an increase in the production of crops particularly cereal crops. Such as wheat, rice and maize.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: The world's highly prized wool yielding 'Pashmina' breed is
questions.solution: The world's highly prized wool yielding 'Pashmina' breed is goat.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Which one is not produced by aquaculture?
questions.solution: Silkworm is an insect. The rearing of silk worm on large scale is called sericulture not aquaculture. The other three being found in water their rearing can be grouped under aquaculture.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: The Triticale is an intergeneric hybrid between
questions.solution:
The Triticale is the first man made cereal. It is formed by a cross between wheat (Triticum) and rye (Secale cerale) followed by allopolyploidy (multiplication of none homologous chromosomes).
$\text{Triticum sp} \times \text{Secale cerale} \rightarrow \text{Diploid} \xrightarrow{\text{Allopolyploidy}} \text{Triticale}$

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: The world's highly prized wool yielding 'Pashmina' breed is
questions.solution: Pashmina wool is obtained from Kashmiri goat.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
questions.q: Three crops that contribute maximum to global food grain production are
questions.solution: Wheat, rice and maize belongs to family Poaceae or Graminae. The main fruit type of these crops is caryopsis in which fruit wall is fused with seed coat. These crops are cultivated in all over the world and are contributed maximum to global food grain production.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Six highly chitinous plate-like teeth are found in $\qquad$ of cockroach
questions.solution: Gizzard in cockroaches do the mastication of hard substances and act like teeth.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following tissue is a vascular?
questions.solution: Stratified squamous epithelial tissue is present on skin. The outermost larger of this tissue is a vascular.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Achondroplasia is a disease related with the defect in the formation of
questions.solution: Achondroplasia is a defect in the formation of cartilage at the epiphyses of long bones producing a form of dwarfism.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Dense regular connective tissue is present in
questions.solution: In dense regular connective tissue the collagen fibres are present in rows between many parallel bundles of fibres. Tendons which attach skeleton muscles to bones and ligaments which attach one bone to another are example of dense regular connective tissue.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Histamine is secreted by
questions.solution: Histamine is secreted by mast cells. It is an organic nitrogenous compound involved in local immune responses. It is responsible for inflammatory responses. It increases the permeability of the capillaries to white blood cells and some proteins to allow them to engage pathogens in the infected tissues.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Blood is a kind of
questions.solution: Blood is a living, vascular, fluid connective tissue, which is made of $60 \%$ plasma, $40 \%$ blood cells and platelets.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Stratified epithelium is found in
questions.solution: Epithelium made up of a series of layers is called stratified epithelium. The cells of each layer vary in size and shape. It is also called laminated epithelium. It is found in olfactory region of the nasal cavity and contains the receptors for the sense of smell.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In mammals, histamine is secreted by
questions.solution:
Histamine is a potent vasodilator formed by decarboxylation of the amino acid histidine and released by mast cells in response to appropriate antigens.
Mast cells which secrete histamine are especially prevalent in the connective tissue of the skin and respiratory tract and in surrounding blood vessels.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The blood of cockroach contains no respiratory pigment. It means that
options: oxygen goes directly into tissues by diffusion
oxygen goes into tissue by intracellular capillary system
questions.solution:
Class-Insecta of phylum-Arthropoda, is characterised by the presence of three pairs of legs, and absence of respiratory pigments in blood, eg, cockroach, mosquito.
In cockroach, respiration is aerobic and oxygen goes into tissues by diffusion through the trachea.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The velocity of conduction of nerve impulse in frog is
questions.solution: The velocity of conduction of nerve impulse in frog is 30 metres per second.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Sciatic vein of frog opens in
questions.solution: The sciatic vein joins with the renal portal of its side. Thereafter, the renal portal first runs forwards for some distance along the outer border of the kidney of its side and then enters into it an breaks into capillaries.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In Pheretima, septa are absent between which segments ?
questions.solution: In Pheretima posthuma (earthworm) septa are absent between $3 / 4$ and $9 / 10$ segments.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In cockroach, larval and nymphal characters are maintained by
questions.solution: Juvenile hormone is produced by corpora allata in insects, it favours the development of juvenile characteristics. During larval life, this hormone predominates and each moult yields another larger juvenile and keeps the larva in immature condition or maintains juvenility.Ecdysone is a steroidal, moulting hormone of the prothoracic glands, named "ecdysone" in insects that triggers metamorphosis.Salivary glands are the merocrine or epicrine type of gland. Their secretion diffuses out through the cell surface, causing no damage to the cell. In man, 3 pairs of salivary glands are present and in rabbit, they are 4 pairs. Salivary glands are absent in frog.Parotid glands are compound tubulo-acinar glands and are the type of salivary glands with a flask-like secretory portion. Poison gland of a toad is modified parotid gland. In rabbit, parotid glands are the largest salivary gland, present beneath the ears. Duct of parotid gland is known as Stenson's duct in man.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is a transparent tissue?
questions.solution: Hyaline cartilage is the most abundant kind of cartilage with no fibres and transparent matrix. It is found upon articular surfaces at joints of long bones from articular cartilage, coastal cartilages at the ventral ends of the ribs. It helps to from the nose, larynx, trachea, bronchi and bronchial tubes leading to the lungs.Fibrocartilage (fibrous cartilage) carries thick dense bundles of white collagen fibres in matrix. They are the strongest cartilage. They occur in joints between vertebrae and also pubic symphysis.Tendon is a very dense, strong fibrous connective tissue made of collagen fibres. Tendon connects a skeletal muscle to a bone.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The female genital pore of Pheretima posthuma located upon the segment
questions.solution:
In Pheretima posthuma, common Indian earthworm, female genital pores present upon segment 14th, while one pair ovary on segment 13th.
Genital papillae occurs in segments 17 and 19 and male genital apertures upon segment 18. Segments 15-26 are the pretyphlosolar region of the intestine.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In frog heart, there are cardiac muscles which consists of fibres called
questions.solution:
The heart wall of frog composed of epicardium, myocardium and endocardium. The myocardium is composed of branched and striated yet involuntary cardiac muscles which contracts and relax rhythmically at a fixed rate. The fibres of the self excitatory and conducting muscle of the heart are of three types-nodal fibres, transitional fibres and Purkinje fibres.
In frog heart, a number of muscular ridges called columnae carnae projected from the wall of ventricle into its cavity, dividing the peripheral part of the cavity into a number of pockets.

questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The idiom/phrase of To throw dust in one's eyes
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following cells in earthworm play a role similar to liver in vertebrates?
questions.solution: Chlorogogen cells are excretory in function. The chlorogogen cells take up excretory matter from the blood capillaries of the gut and from the coelomic fluid of the coelom. They also store glycogen and fat. So, these cells are analogous to the liver of vertebrates.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Chloragogen cells help in :
questions.solution: Chloragogen cells or yellow cells are present in the region of intestine in Pheretima. The exact function of these cells is not fully known but it is believed that they are concerned with storage of reserve food and also excretory in function. Chloragogen cells are analogous to liver of vertebrates.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Yellow bone marrow is found specially in the medullary cavity
questions.solution: Bone marrow is of two types; red and yellow. The yellow marrow is found especially in medullary cavity of long bones, while red is in spongy bones.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: What will happen if ligaments are torn?
options: Bone less movable at joint and pain
Bone will move freely at joint and no pain
questions.solution: Ligaments join a bone with another bone in movable/synovial joints. Torn ligaments make movement at joints very painful and restricted. It heals only after prolonged movement restriction.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Chloragogen cells of earthworms are analogous to vertebrate :
questions.solution: Chloragogen cells of earthworm are analogous to liver of vertebrates.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Lymphocytes are formed by :
questions.solution: Lymphocyte is a type of agranular leucocyte formed by lymph gland and lymph node. Mast cells are cells of connective tissue, modified from basophil of blood and secrete histamine, serotonin and heparin. Plasma cells are cells of connective tissue, which synthesize antibodies.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Inner lining of blood vessels is formed by
questions.solution: Inner lining of blood vessels is formed by squamous epithelium. Spuamous epithelium consists of a layer of thin, flat, scale-like cells with prominent nuclei. The cell have irregular boundaries that fit closely into those of neighbouring cells.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Number of cranial nerves in frog :
questions.solution: Ten pairs of cranial nerves are present in anamniotes such as fishes and amphibians (e.g., Frog). Twelve pairs of cranial nerves are present in amniotes, reptiles, birds and mammals including rabbit and humans.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Heparin is secreted by
questions.solution: Heparin is released from mast-cells. It is an anticoagulant which prevents activation of prothrombin.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In frog, the vertebra with an anterior convex surface (i.e., double convexties) is
questions.solution: The 9 th or sacral vertebra is acoelous and highly specialised the anterior face is convex for this give greater strength to them, it would be given if its anterior end are hollowed out the transverse process are stout and downwardly directed. The ileum of the pelvic girdle articulates with the transverse process of 9 th vertebra.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: RBC's are nucleated in
questions.solution: RBC in man is anucleated. Nucleated RBCs are found in camel and amphibians (e.g. frog). In frog, RBCs are typically flattened and more or less eliptical in shape but it appears biconvex when seen along its edge. They are filled with cytoplasm of faint straw colour and contains a definite round nucleus in centre.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Which of the following is a transparent tissue?
questions.solution: Hyaline cartilage has a clear, homogenous, transparent bluish-green matrix. It gives glass like appearance. A few bundles of small white fibres are imbedded in the matrix. It is present in the end of limb bones (called hyaline caps).

questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: Third cleavage of frog's development is
questions.solution: First and second cleavage in frog are holoblastic, complete, equal, vertical, whereas third cleavage is holoblastic, complete, unequal, latitudinal or horizontal and near the animal pole. It results in the formation 8 cells of which three are black micromeres which have less or no yolk, so divide faster and 4 are colourless macromeres.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: In frog chromosome number is reduced to half
questions.solution: Each primary oocyte undergo two maturation divisions, first meiotic and the second meiotic division. In the I-meiotic division, the primary oocyte divides into two unequal haploid daughter cells-a large secondary oocyte and very small first polar body. In II-maturation division, the first polar body may divide to form two second polar bodies. The secondary oocyte again divides into two unequal daughter cells, a large ootid and a very small second polar body. The ootid grows into a functional haploid ovum. The I meiotic division is reductional and II meiotic division is equational division.

questions.subject: Zoology
questions.q: The epithelial lining in the alveoli of the frogs facing lung cavity is
questions.solution: Alveoli is highly vascularised with an internal lining of very thin squamous epithelium, which always remain moist by mucus. The epithelial cells at the linar most margins of septa facing lung cavity are columnar and ciliated.
